#include <ATen/detail/XPUHooksInterface.h>\\n23: #include <c10/core/QEngine.h>\\n24: #include <c10/core/impl/DeviceGuardImplInterface.h>\\n25: #include <c10/util/CallOnce.h>\\n26: #include <c10/util/Exception.h>\\n27: #include <c10/util/env.h>\\n28: #include <c10/util/irange.h>\\n29: \\n30: #include <cstdint>\\n31: #include <map>\\n32: #include <mutex>\\n33: \\n34: namespace at {\\n35: \\n36: class Tensor;\\n37: \\n38: enum class TORCH_API Float32MatmulPrecision { HIGHEST, HIGH, MEDIUM };\\n39: \\n40: class TORCH_API Context {\\n41:  public:\\n42:   Context();\\n43: \\n44:   const Generator& defaultGenerator(Device device) {\\n45:     c10::DeviceType device_type = device.type();\\n46:     lazyInitDevice(device_type);\\n47: \\n48:     if (device_type == at::kCPU) {\\n49:       return at::detail::getDefaultCPUGenerator();\\n50:     } else {\\n51:       return getAcceleratorHooksInterface(device_type)\\n52:           .getDefaultGenerator(device.index());\\n53:     }\\n54:   }\\n55: \\n56:   const AcceleratorHooksInterface& getAcceleratorHooksInterface(\\n57:       std::optional<c10::DeviceType> opt_device_type = std::nullopt) {\\n58:     if (!opt_device_type.has_value()) {\\n59:       opt_device_type = at::getAccelerator(true);\\n60:     }\\n61:     if (opt_device_type == at::kCUDA) {\\n62:       return at::detail::getCUDAHooks();\\n63:     } else if (opt_device_type == at::kXPU) {\\n64:       return at::detail::getXPUHooks();\\n65:     } else if (opt_device_type == at::kMPS) {\\n66:       return at::detail::getMPSHooks();\\n67:     } else if (opt_device_type == at::kPrivateUse1) {\\n68:       return at::detail::getPrivateUse1Hooks();\\n69:     } else if (opt_device_type == at::kMTIA) {\\n70:       return at::detail::getMTIAHooks();\\n71:     } else if (opt_device_type == at::kHIP) {\\n72:       return at::detail::getHIPHooks();\\n73:     } else if (opt_device_type == at::kHPU) {\\n74:       return at::detail::getHPUHooks();\\n75:     } else {\\n76:       TORCH_CHECK(\\n77:           false,\\n78:           opt_device_type.has_value()\\n79:               ? c10::DeviceTypeName(opt_device_type.value())\\n80:               : \\\"None\\\",\\n81:           \\\" device type not an accelerator.\\\");\\n82:     }\\n83:   }\\n84: \\n85:   Device getDeviceFromPtr(void* data, c10::DeviceType device_type) {\\n86:     lazyInitDevice(device_type);\\n87: \\n88:     if (device_type == at::kCPU) {\\n89:       return c10::DeviceType::CPU;\\n90:     } else {\\n91:       return getAcceleratorHooksInterface(device_type).getDeviceFromPtr(data);\\n92:     }\\n93:   }\\n94: \\n95:   bool isPinnedPtr(\\n96:       const void* data,\\n97:       std::optional<c10::DeviceType> device_type = std::nullopt) {\\n98:     auto opt_device_type =\\n99:         device_type.has_value() ? device_type : at::getAccelerator();\\n100:     if (!opt_device_type.has_value() || // there is no accelerator\\n101:         !at::isAccelerator(\\n102:             opt_device_type.value())) { // passed device not an accelerator\\n103:       return false;\\n104:     }\\n105:     if (!init_[static_cast<int8_t>(opt_device_type.value())].test_once()) {\\n106:       // If the device is not initialized, no pointer can be pinned for it\\n107:       return false;\\n108:     }\\n109:     return getAcceleratorHooksInterface(opt_device_type).isPinnedPtr(data);\\n110:   }\\n111: \\n112:   Allocator* getPinnedMemoryAllocator(\\n113:       std::optional<c10::DeviceType> device_type = std::nullopt) {\\n114:     auto opt_device_type =\\n115:         device_type.has_value() ? device_type : at::getAccelerator();\\n116:     if (opt_device_type) {\\n117:       lazyInitDevice(opt_device_type.value());\\n118:     }\\n119:     return getAcceleratorHooksInterface(device_type).getPinnedMemoryAllocator();\\n120:   }\\n121: \\n122:   void lazyInitDevice(c10::DeviceType device_type) {\\n123:     if (device_type != at::kCPU) {\\n124:       c10::call_once(init_[static_cast<int8_t>(device_type)], [&] {\\n125:         getAcceleratorHooksInterface(device_type).init();\\n126:       });\\n127:     }\\n128:   }\\n129: \\n130:   static bool hasOpenMP();\\n131:   static bool hasMKL();\\n132:   static bool hasKleidiAI();\\n133:   static bool hasLAPACK();\\n134:   static bool hasMKLDNN();\\n135:   static bool ckSupported();\\n136:   static bool hasEigenSparse();\\n137:   static bool hasMAGMA() {\\n138:     return detail::getCUDAHooks().hasMAGMA();\\n139:   }\\n140:   static bool hasCUDA() {\\n141:     return detail::getCUDAHooks().hasCUDA();\\n142:   }\\n143:   static bool hasMTIA() {\\n144:     return detail::getMTIAHooks().hasMTIA();\\n145:   }\\n146:   static bool hasCUDART() {\\n147:     return detail::getCUDAHooks().hasCUDART();\\n148:   }\\n149:   static long versionCUDART() {\\n150:     return detail::getCUDAHooks().versionCUDART();\\n151:   }\\n152:   static bool hasCuDNN() {\\n153:     return detail::getCUDAHooks().hasCuDNN();\\n154:   }\\n155:   static long versionCuDNN() {\\n156:     return detail::getCUDAHooks().versionCuDNN();\\n157:   }\\n158:   static long versionRuntimeCuDNN() {\\n159:     return detail::getCUDAHooks().versionRuntimeCuDNN();\\n160:   }\\n161:   static long versionCuDNNFrontend() {\\n162:     return detail::getCUDAHooks().versionCuDNNFrontend();\\n163:   }\\n164:   static bool hasCuSOLVER() {\\n165:     return detail::getCUDAHooks().hasCuSOLVER();\\n166:   }\\n167:   static bool hasCuBLASLt() {\\n168:     return detail::getCUDAHooks().hasCuBLASLt();\\n169:   }\\n170:   static bool hasROCM() {\\n171:     return detail::getCUDAHooks().hasROCM();\\n172:   }\\n173:   static bool hasCKSDPA() {\\n174:     return detail::getCUDAHooks().hasCKSDPA();\\n175:   }\\n176:   static bool hasCKGEMM() {\\n177:     return detail::getCUDAHooks().hasCKGEMM();\\n178:   }\\n179:   static bool hasHIP() {\\n180:     return detail::getHIPHooks().hasHIP();\\n181:   }\\n182:   static bool hasMPS() {\\n183:     return detail::getMPSHooks().hasMPS();\\n184:   }\\n185:   static bool hasIPU() {\\n186:     return c10::impl::hasDeviceGuardImpl(c10::DeviceType::IPU);\\n187:   }\\n188:   static bool hasXLA() {\\n189:     return c10::impl::hasDeviceGuardImpl(c10::DeviceType::XLA);\\n190:   }\\n191:   static bool hasXPU() {\\n192:     return detail::getXPUHooks().hasXPU();\\n193:   }\\n194:   static bool hasLazy() {\\n195:     return c10::impl::hasDeviceGuardImpl(c10::DeviceType::Lazy);\\n196:   }\\n197:   static bool hasMAIA() {\\n198:     return c10::impl::hasDeviceGuardImpl(c10::DeviceType::MAIA);\\n199:   }\\n200:   static bool hasHPU() {\\n201:     return detail::getHPUHooks().hasHPU();\\n202:   }\\n203: \\n204:   static const at::cuda::NVRTC& getNVRTC() {\\n205:     return detail::getCUDAHooks().nvrtc();\\n206:   }\\n207: \\n208:   static bool setFlushDenormal(bool on);\\n209: \\n210:   // NB: This method is *purely* whether or not a user requested\\n211:   // that CuDNN was enabled, it doesn't actually say anything about\\n212:   // whether or not CuDNN is actually usable.  Use cudnn_is_acceptable\\n213:   // to test this instead\\n214:   bool userEnabledCuDNN() const;\\n215:   void setUserEnabledCuDNN(bool e);\\n216:   bool userEnabledMkldnn() const;\\n217:   void setUserEnabledMkldnn(bool e);\\n218:   bool benchmarkCuDNN() const;\\n219:   void setBenchmarkCuDNN(bool);\\n220:   int benchmarkLimitCuDNN() const;\\n221:   void setBenchmarkLimitCuDNN(int);\\n222:   bool immediateMiopen() const;\\n223:   void setImmediateMiopen(bool);\\n224:   bool deterministicCuDNN() const;\\n225:   void setDeterministicCuDNN(bool);\\n226:   bool deterministicMkldnn() const;\\n227:   void setDeterministicMkldnn(bool);\\n228:   bool userEnabledNNPACK() const;\\n229:   void setUserEnabledNNPACK(bool e);\\n230: \\n231:   // Note [Disabling Fused SDP Kernels]\\n232:   // ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\\n233:   // Flash and Memory Efficient SDP kernels are enabled by default.\\n234:   // However, they can be disabled by setting\\n235:   // at::globalContext().setUserEnabledFlashSDP(false) flag.\\n236:   // This is useful for debugging purposes. For example, if you want to\\n237:   // compare the performance of the flash SDP kernels with the unfused\\n238:   // kernel, you can disable the flash SDP kernels. By disabling\\n239:   // the math SDP kernel, you can force your code to use flash kernels.\\n240:   // The math SDP kernel can be disabled by setting\\n241:   // at::globalContext().setUserEnabledMathSDP(false) flag.\\n242:   void setSDPPriorityOrder(const std::vector<int64_t>& order);\\n243:   std::array<at::SDPBackend, at::num_sdp_backends> sDPPriorityOrder();\\n244: \\n245:   void setSDPUseFlash(bool);\\n246:   bool userEnabledFlashSDP() const;\\n247: \\n248:   void setSDPUseMemEfficient(bool);\\n249:   bool userEnabledMemEfficientSDP() const;\\n250: \\n251:   void setSDPUseMath(bool);\\n252:   bool userEnabledMathSDP() const;\\n253: \\n254:   void setSDPUseCuDNN(bool);\\n255:   bool userEnabledCuDNNSDP() const;\\n256: \\n257:   void setAllowFP16BF16ReductionMathSDP(bool);\\n258:   bool allowFP16BF16ReductionMathSDP() const;\\n259: \\n260:   void setSDPUseOverrideable(bool);\\n261:   bool userEnabledOverrideableSDP() const;\\n262: \\n263:   at::LinalgBackend linalgPreferredBackend() const;\\n264:   void setLinalgPreferredBackend(at::LinalgBackend);\\n265: \\n266:   at::BlasBackend blasPreferredBackend();\\n267:   void setBlasPreferredBackend(at::BlasBackend);\\n268: \\n269:   at::ROCmFABackend getROCmFAPreferredBackend();\\n270:   void setROCmFAPreferredBackend(at::ROCmFABackend);\\n271: \\n272:   // Note [Enabling Deterministic Operations]\\n273:   // ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\\n274:   // Operations in PyTorch that normally act nondeterministically, but have an\\n275:   // alternate deterministic implementation, should satisfy the following\\n276:   // requirements:\\n277:   //\\n278:   // * Include this comment: \\\"See Note [Enabling Deterministic Operations]\\\"\\n279:   //\\n280:   // * Check the value of `at::globalContext().deterministicAlgorithms()` to\\n281:   // toggle\\n282:   //   between nondeterministic and deterministic implementations.\\n283:   //\\n284:   // * Have an entry in the list of PyTorch operations that toggle between\\n285:   // nondeterministic\\n286:   //   and deterministic implementations, in the docstring of\\n287:   //   `use_deterministic_algorithms()` in torch/__init__.py\\n288:   //\\n289:   // `example_func()` below shows an example of toggling between\\n290:   // nondeterministic and deterministic implementations:\\n291:   //\\n292:   //    void example_func() {\\n293:   //      // See Note [Enabling Deterministic Operations]\\n294:   //      if (at::globalContext().deterministicAlgorithms()) {\\n295:   //        example_func_deterministic();\\n296:   //      } else {\\n297:   //        example_func_nondeterministic();\\n298:   //      }\\n299:   //    }\\n300: \\n301:   bool deterministicAlgorithms() const;\\n302:   bool deterministicAlgorithmsWarnOnly() const;\\n303:   void setDeterministicAlgorithms(bool, bool);\\n304:   bool deterministicFillUninitializedMemory() const;\\n305:   void setDeterministicFillUninitializedMemory(bool);\\n306: \\n307:   // Note [Writing Nondeterministic Operations]\\n308:   // ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\\n309:   // Operations in PyTorch that act nondeterministically and do not have an\\n310:   // alternate deterministic implementation should satisfy the following\\n311:   // requirements:\\n312:   //\\n313:   // * Include this comment: \\\"See Note [Writing Nondeterministic Operations]\\\"\\n314:   //\\n315:   // * Include a comment explaining why the operation is nondeterministic.\\n316:   //\\n317:   // * Throw an error when `Context::deterministicAlgorithms()` is true. Most\\n318:   //   of the time, this should be accomplished by calling\\n319:   //   `at::globalContext().alertNotDeterminstic()`.  However, if the\\n320:   //   nondeterministic behavior is caused by the CuBLAS workspace\\n321:   //   configuration in CUDA >= 10.2,\\n322:   //   `at::globalContext().alertCuBLASConfigNotDeterministic()` should be\\n323:   //   called instead (in this case, a comment explaining why the operation is\\n324:   //   nondeterministic is not necessary). See below for details on these\\n325:   //   methods.\\n326:   //\\n327:   // * Have an entry in the list of nondeterministic PyTorch operations in the\\n328:   //   docstring of `use_deterministic_algorithms()` in torch/__init__.py\\n329:   //\\n330:   // * Have a test function in `test/test_torch.py` whose name begins with\\n331:   //   `test_nondeterministic_alert_`. Alternatively, if CuBLAS workspace\\n332:   //   configuration is the reason for nondeterminism, the operation should be\\n333:   //   included in the `test_cublas_config_nondeterministic_alert` test. Any new\\n334:   //   tests should ideally follow a pattern similar to the existing ones.\\n335:   //\\n336:   // `example_func()` below shows an example of the comments and error-throwing\\n337:   // code for a nondeterministic operation:\\n338:   //\\n339:   //    void example_func() {\\n340:   //      // See Note [Writing Nondeterministic Operations]\\n341:   //      // Nondeterministic because <reason>\\n342:   //      at::globalContext().alertNondeterministic(\\\"example_func\\\");\\n343:   //      ...\\n344:   //    }\\n345: \\n346:   // Throws an error if `Context::deterministicAlgorithms()` is true\\n347:   static void alertNotDeterministic(std::string_view const& caller);\\n348: \\n349:   // Throws an error if `Context::deterministicAlgorithms()` is true, CUDA\\n350:   // >= 10.2, and CUBLAS_WORKSPACE_CONFIG is not set to either \\\":16:8\\\" or\\n351:   // \\\":4096:8\\\". For more details:\\n352:   // https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cublas/index.html#results-reproducibility\\n353:   void alertCuBLASConfigNotDeterministic() const;\\n354: \\n355:   void setFloat32MatmulPrecision(const std::string& s);\\n356:   void setFloat32Precision(\\n357:       const std::string& backend,\\n358:       const std::string& op,\\n359:       const std::string& s);\\n360:   bool allowTF32CuDNN(const std::string& op = std::string()) const;\\n361:   void setAllowTF32CuDNN(bool);\\n362:   bool allowTF32OneDNN() const;\\n363:   void setAllowTF32OneDNN(bool);\\n364:   bool allowTF32CuBLAS() const;\\n365:   void setAllowTF32CuBLAS(bool);\\n366:   Float32MatmulPrecision float32MatmulPrecision() const;\\n367:   std::string float32Precision(\\n368:       const std::string& backend,\\n369:       const std::string& op) const;\\n370:   bool allowFP16ReductionCuBLAS() const;\\n371:   void setAllowFP16ReductionCuBLAS(bool);\\n372:   bool allowBF16ReductionCuBLAS() const;\\n373:   void setAllowBF16ReductionCuBLAS(bool);\\n374:   bool allowFP16AccumulationCuBLAS() const;\\n375:   void setAllowFP16AccumulationCuBLAS(bool);\\n376: \\n377:   // Matmuls can use a so-called \\\"persistent\\\" kernel which launches one CUDA\\n378:   // block for each SM on the GPU, and each block then iterates over multiple\\n379:   // output tiles. This allows to use software pipelining to hide the begin/end\\n380:   // latencies (e.g., epilogue), especially when only one tile fits per SM.\\n381:   // However, if some SMs are busy (e.g., with a background NCCL kernel), the\\n382:   // matmul's blocks will be scheduled in two waves and, in the absence of some\\n383:   // smart load balancing, the kernel will take twice as long. This flag allows\\n384:   // to make matmuls target only a subset of the SMs, so they can fully schedule\\n385:   // even next to a comms kernel, and only be a few percent slower.\\n386:   std::optional<int32_t> _SMCarveout_EXPERIMENTAL() const;\\n387:   void _setSMCarveout_EXPERIMENTAL(std::optional<int32_t>);\\n388: \\n389:   at::QEngine qEngine() const;\\n390:   void setQEngine(at::QEngine e);\\n391:   static const std::vector<at::QEngine>& supportedQEngines();\\n392:   static bool isXNNPACKAvailable();\\n393:   void setCheckSparseTensorInvariants(bool e);\\n394:   bool checkSparseTensorInvariants() const;\\n395:   // This method is used to release the original weight after pre-packing.\\n396:   // It should be called once before loading/running the model.\\n397:   // NB: By default it is set to true for mobile builds.\\n398:   void setReleaseWeightsWhenPrepacking(bool e);\\n399:   bool releaseWeightsWhenPrepacking() const;\\n400: \\n401:   void setDisplayVmapFallbackWarnings(bool enabled);\\n402:   bool areVmapFallbackWarningsEnabled() const;\\n403: \\n404:   bool isDefaultMobileCPUAllocatorSet();\\n405:   void setDefaultMobileCPUAllocator();\\n406:   void unsetDefaultMobileCPUAllocator();\\n407:   bool allowFP16ReductionCPU() const;\\n408:   void setAllowFP16ReductionCPU(bool);\\n409: \\n410:   // Preserved for BC\\n411:   void lazyInitCUDA() {\\n412:     TORCH_WARN_DEPRECATION(\\n413:         \\\"lazyInitCUDA is deprecated. Please use lazyInitDevice(at::kCUDA) instead.\\\")\\n414:     lazyInitDevice(at::kCUDA);\\n415:   }\\n416:   void lazyInitHIP() {\\n417:     TORCH_WARN_DEPRECATION(\\n418:         \\\"lazyInitHIP is deprecated. Please use lazyInitDevice(at::kHIP) instead.\\\")\\n419:     lazyInitDevice(at::kHIP);\\n420:   }\\n421:   void lazyInitXPU() {\\n422:     TORCH_WARN_DEPRECATION(\\n423:         \\\"lazyInitXPU is deprecated. Please use lazyInitDevice(at::kXPU) instead.\\\")\\n424:     lazyInitDevice(at::kXPU);\\n425:   }\\n426:   void lazyInitMTIA() {\\n427:     TORCH_WARN_DEPRECATION(\\n428:         \\\"lazyInitMTIA is deprecated. Please use lazyInitDevice(at::kMTIA) instead.\\\")\\n429:     lazyInitDevice(at::kMTIA);\\n430:   }\\n431:   void lazyInitPrivateUse1() {\\n432:     TORCH_WARN_DEPRECATION(\\n433:         \\\"lazyInitPrivateUse1 is deprecated. Please use lazyInitDevice(at::kPrivateUse1) instead.\\\")\\n434:     lazyInitDevice(at::kPrivateUse1);\\n435:   }\\n436: \\n437:  private:\\n438:   static bool checkCuBLASConfigDeterministic();\\n439:   std::array<c10::once_flag, at::COMPILE_TIME_MAX_DEVICE_TYPES> init_;\\n440:   bool enabled_cudnn = true;\\n441:   bool deterministic_cudnn = false;\\n442:   bool deterministic_mkldnn = false;\\n443:   bool _deterministic_algorithms = false;\\n444:   bool _deterministic_algorithms_warn_only = false;\\n445:   bool _deterministic_fill_uninitialized_memory = true;\\n446:   std::array<at::SDPBackend, at::num_sdp_backends> sdp_priority_order = {\\n447:       at::SDPBackend::flash_attention,\\n448:       at::SDPBackend::efficient_attention,\\n449:       at::SDPBackend::math,\\n450:       at::SDPBackend::cudnn_attention,\\n451:       at::SDPBackend::overrideable};\\n452:   bool enabled_flashSDP = true;\\n453:   bool enabled_mem_efficientSDP = true;\\n454:   bool enabled_mathSDP = true;\\n455:   bool enabled_cudnnSDP = true;\\n456:   bool enabled_overrideable = true;\\n457:   bool allow_fp16_bf16_reduction_mathSDP = false;\\n458:   bool benchmark_cudnn = false;\\n459:   bool immediate_miopen = false;\\n460:   Float32MatmulPrecision float32_matmul_precision =\\n461:       c10::utils::check_env(\\\"TORCH_ALLOW_TF32_CUBLAS_OVERRIDE\\\") == true\\n462:       ? at::Float32MatmulPrecision::HIGH\\n463:       : at::Float32MatmulPrecision::HIGHEST;\\n464:   int benchmark_limit_cudnn = 10;\\n465:   bool allow_tf32_cudnn = true;\\n466:   bool allow_fp16_reduction_cublas = true;\\n467:   bool allow_bf16_reduction_cublas = true;\\n468:   bool allow_fp16_accumulation_cublas = false;\\n469:   std::optional<int32_t> sm_carveout = std::nullopt;\\n470:   bool enabled_mkldnn = true;\\n471:   bool allow_tf32_onednn = false;\\n472:   bool enabled_nnpack = true;\\n473:   at::LinalgBackend linalg_preferred_backend =\\n474: at::LinalgBackend::Cusolver\\n477:       : at::LinalgBackend::Default;\\n478:   at::BlasBackend blas_preferred_backend =\\n479:       (c10::utils::check_env(\\\"TORCH_BLAS_PREFER_CUBLASLT\\\") == true ||\\n480:        c10::utils::check_env(\\\"TORCH_BLAS_PREFER_HIPBLASLT\\\") == true) // alias\\n481:       ? at::BlasBackend::Cublaslt\\n482:       : at::BlasBackend::Default;\\n483: at::ROCmFABackend::Default;\\n487: #ifdef C10_MOBILE\\n488:   bool release_original_weights = true;\\n489: #else\\n490:   bool release_original_weights = false;\\n491: #endif\\n492:   bool display_vmap_fallback_warnings_ = false;\\n493:   std::atomic<at::QEngine> quantized_engine = at::QEngine::NoQEngine;\\n494:   bool enable_sparse_tensor_invariant_checks = false;\\n495:   bool allow_fp16_reduction_cpu = false;\\n496: \\n497:   std::map<std::string, std::map<std::string, std::string>> fp32_precision = {\\n498:       {\\\"generic\\\", {{\\\"all\\\", \\\"none\\\"}}},\\n499:       {\\\"mkldnn\\\",\\n500:        {{\\\"matmul\\\", \\\"none\\\"},\\n501:         {\\\"conv\\\", \\\"none\\\"},\\n502:         {\\\"rnn\\\", \\\"none\\\"},\\n503:         {\\\"all\\\", \\\"none\\\"}}},\\n504:       {\\\"cuda\\\",\\n505:        {{\\\"matmul\\\",\\n506:          float32_matmul_precision == at::Float32MatmulPrecision::HIGHEST\\n507:              ? \\\"none\\\"\\n508: Allocator* prev_allocator_ptr_{nullptr};\\n515: };\\n516: \\n517: TORCH_API Context& globalContext();\\n518: \\n519: inline void init() {\\n520:   globalContext();\\n521: }\\n522: \\n523: TORCH_API Allocator* getCPUAllocator();\\n524: \\n525: inline DeprecatedTypeProperties& getDeprecatedTypeProperties(\\n526:     Backend p,\\n527:     ScalarType s) {\\n528:   return globalDeprecatedTypePropertiesRegistry().getDeprecatedTypeProperties(\\n529:       p, s);\\n530: }\\n531: \\n532: inline DeprecatedTypeProperties& CPU(ScalarType s) {\\n533:   return globalDeprecatedTypePropertiesRegistry().getDeprecatedTypeProperties(\\n534:       Backend::CPU, s);\\n535: }\\n536: \\n537: inline DeprecatedTypeProperties& CUDA(ScalarType s) {\\n538:   return globalDeprecatedTypePropertiesRegistry().getDeprecatedTypeProperties(\\n539:       Backend::CUDA, s);\\n540: }\\n541: \\n542: inline DeprecatedTypeProperties& HIP(ScalarType s) {\\n543:   return globalDeprecatedTypePropertiesRegistry().getDeprecatedTypeProperties(\\n544:       Backend::HIP, s);\\n545: }\\n546: \\n547: inline DeprecatedTypeProperties& MPS(ScalarType s) {\\n548:   return globalDeprecatedTypePropertiesRegistry().getDeprecatedTypeProperties(\\n549:       Backend::MPS, s);\\n550: }\\n551: \\n552: inline bool hasCUDA() {\\n553:   return globalContext().hasCUDA();\\n554: }\\n555: \\n556: inline bool hasMTIA() {\\n557:   return globalContext().hasMTIA();\\n558: }\\n559: \\n560: inline bool hasHIP() {\\n561:   return globalContext().hasHIP();\\n562: }\\n563: \\n564: inline bool hasIPU() {\\n565:   return globalContext().hasIPU();\\n566: }\\n567: \\n568: inline bool hasXLA() {\\n569:   return globalContext().hasXLA();\\n570: }\\n571: \\n572: inline bool hasMPS() {\\n573:   return globalContext().hasMPS();\\n574: }\\n575: \\n576: inline bool hasMAIA() {\\n577:   return globalContext().hasMAIA();\\n578: }\\n579: \\n580: inline bool hasXPU() {\\n581:   return globalContext().hasXPU();\\n582: }\\n583: \\n584: inline bool hasHPU() {\\n585:   return globalContext().hasHPU();\\n586: }\\n587: \\n588: // Despite its name, this function returns the number of *CUDA* GPUs.\\n589: inline size_t getNumGPUs() {\\n590:   // WARNING: DO NOT ADD LOGIC TO HANDLE OTHER DEVICE TYPES TO THIS\\n591:   // FUNCTION.  If you are interested in interrogating the number of\\n592:   // devices for a specific device type, add that function to the\\n593:   // relevant library (e.g., similar to at::cuda::device_count())\\n594:   if (hasCUDA() && hasHIP()) {\\n595:     TORCH_CHECK(\\n596:         false,\\n597:         \\\"Enabling both CUDA and HIP in ATen is not supported, as HIP masquerades \\\"\\n598:         \\\"to be CUDA (e.g., when you say CUDA, on a HIP build of ATen, this actually \\\"\\n599:         \\\"means HIP.  Rebuild PyTorch with one or the other disabled.\\\");\\n600:   } else if (hasCUDA()) {\\n601:     return detail::getCUDAHooks().deviceCount();\\n602:   } else if (hasHIP()) {\\n603:     return detail::getHIPHooks().getNumGPUs();\\n604:   } else {\\n605:     return 0;\\n606:   }\\n607: }\\n608: \\n609: inline bool hasOpenMP() {\\n610:   return globalContext().hasOpenMP();\\n611: }\\n612: \\n613: inline bool hasMKL() {\\n614:   return globalContext().hasMKL();\\n615: }\\n616: \\n617: inline bool hasKleidiAI() {\\n618:   return globalContext().hasKleidiAI();\\n619: }\\n620: \\n621: inline bool hasLAPACK() {\\n622:   return globalContext().hasLAPACK();\\n623: }\\n624: \\n625: inline bool hasEigenSparse() {\\n626:   return globalContext().hasEigenSparse();\\n627: }\\n628: \\n629: inline bool hasMAGMA() {\\n630:   return globalContext().hasMAGMA();\\n631: }\\n632: \\n633: inline bool hasMKLDNN() {\\n634:   return globalContext().hasMKLDNN();\\n635: }\\n636: \\n637: inline void manual_seed(uint64_t seed) {\\n638:   {\\n639:     auto gen = globalContext().defaultGenerator(c10::DeviceType::CPU);\\n640:     // See Note [Acquire lock when using random generators]\\n641:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(gen.mutex());\\n642:     gen.set_current_seed(seed);\\n643:   }\\n644: \\n645:   const auto opt_device_type = at::getAccelerator();\\n646:   if (!opt_device_type.has_value()) {\\n647:     return;\\n648:   }\\n649:   const auto num_gpus = globalContext()\\n650:                             .getAcceleratorHooksInterface(opt_device_type)\\n651:                             .deviceCount();\\n652:   for (const auto i : c10::irange(num_gpus)) {\\n653:     auto gen = globalContext().defaultGenerator(\\n654:         Device(opt_device_type.value(), static_cast<c10::DeviceIndex>(i)));\\n655:     {\\n656:       // See Note [Acquire lock when using random generators]\\n657:       std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(gen.mutex());\\n658:       gen.set_current_seed(seed);\\n659:     }\\n660:   }\\n661: }\\n662: \\n663: // When the global flag `allow_tf32` is set to true, cuBLAS handles are\\n664: // automatically configured to use math mode CUBLAS_TF32_TENSOR_OP_MATH.\\n665: // For some operators, such as addmv, TF32 offers no performance improvement\\n666: // but causes precision loss. To help this case, this class implements\\n667: // a RAII guard that can be used to quickly disable TF32 within its scope.\\n668: //\\n669: // Usage:\\n670: //     NoTF32Guard disable_tf32;\\n671: struct TORCH_API NoTF32Guard {\\n672:   NoTF32Guard();\\n673:   NoTF32Guard(NoTF32Guard&& other) = delete;\\n674:   NoTF32Guard(const NoTF32Guard&) = delete;\\n675:   NoTF32Guard& operator=(const NoTF32Guard&) = delete;\\n676:   NoTF32Guard& operator=(NoTF32Guard&&) = delete;\\n677:   ~NoTF32Guard();\\n678:   static bool should_disable_tf32();\\n679: \\n680:  private:\\n681:   bool changed = false;\\n682: };\\n683: \\n684: struct TORCH_API ROCmBackwardPassGuard {\\n685:   ROCmBackwardPassGuard();\\n686:   ROCmBackwardPassGuard(ROCmBackwardPassGuard&& other) = delete;\\n687:   ROCmBackwardPassGuard(const ROCmBackwardPassGuard&) = delete;\\n688:   ROCmBackwardPassGuard& operator=(const ROCmBackwardPassGuard&) = delete;\\n689:   ROCmBackwardPassGuard& operator=(ROCmBackwardPassGuard&&) = delete;\\n690:   ~ROCmBackwardPassGuard();\\n691:   static bool is_backward_pass();\\n692: };\\n693: \\n694: } // namespace at\\n695: \", \"url\": \"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--5811a8d7@5811a8d7da873dd699ff6687092c225caffcf1bb/-/blob/aten/src/ATen/Context.h\"}, \"isError\": false}"

So I need to put the modified files in my working directory (at the repo-relative paths). The verifier will compare my files against the expected output.\n\nLet me create the files by reading the PRE-PR versions and applying the changes. I'll use str_replace_editor to create each file with the POST-PR content.\n\nFor very large files, I should only include the relevant portions that changed. But since the verifier compares changes, I think I need to provide the full modified files.\n\nActually, wait. Let me re-read the instructions: \"Use MCP to inspect remote code, then create or edit only the files you need directly in the working directory using repository-relative paths.\"\n\nSo I need to create the files at the proper paths in my workspace. The verifier will see what files exist in my workspace and compare the diffs.\n\nFor large files like Convolution.cpp, I should download the entire file and put it in my workspace. But that could be huge. Let me think about the scoring: \"Score = 0.35 * file_recall + 0.45 * line_recall + 0.20 * line_precision\". This means having the right files is important, and having the right lines within those files matters too.\n\nThe key question is: does the verifier compare my working directory files as a whole against the expected post-PR state? Or does it just check if certain key lines are present?\n\nGiven the description \"The verifier will compare your code changes against the expected ground-truth diff\", I think it compares the diff of my changes against the expected diff. \"annotations\": null, \"meta\": null}], \"structuredContent\": {\"content\": \"1: #pragma once\\n2: \\n3: #include <ATen/detail/CUDAHooksInterface.h>\\n4: \\n5: #include <ATen/Generator.h>\\n6: \\n7: // TODO: No need to have this whole header, we can just put it all in\\n8: // the cpp file\\n9: \\n10: namespace at::cuda::detail {\\n11: \\n12: // Set the callback to initialize Magma, which is set by\\n13: // torch_cuda_cu. This indirection is required so magma_init is called\\n14: // in the same library where Magma will be used.\\n15: TORCH_CUDA_CPP_API void set_magma_init_fn(void (*magma_init_fn)());\\n16: \\n17: \\n18: // The real implementation of CUDAHooksInterface\\n19: struct CUDAHooks : public at::CUDAHooksInterface {\\n20:   CUDAHooks(at::CUDAHooksArgs) {}\\n21:   void init() const override;\\n22:   Device getDeviceFromPtr(void* data) const override;\\n23:   bool isPinnedPtr(const void* data) const override;\\n24:   const Generator& getDefaultGenerator(\\n25:       DeviceIndex device_index = -1) const override;\\n26:   Generator getNewGenerator(\\n27:       DeviceIndex device_index = -1) const override;\\n28:   bool hasCUDA() const override;\\n29:   bool hasMAGMA() const override;\\n30:   bool hasCuDNN() const override;\\n31:   bool hasCuSOLVER() const override;\\n32:   bool hasCuBLASLt() const override;\\n33:   bool hasROCM() const override;\\n34:   bool hasCKSDPA() const override;\\n35:   bool hasCKGEMM() const override;\\n36:   const at::cuda::NVRTC& nvrtc() const override;\\n37:   DeviceIndex current_device() const override;\\n38:   bool isBuilt() const override {return true;}\\n39:   bool isAvailable() const override {return hasCUDA();}\\n40:   bool hasPrimaryContext(DeviceIndex device_index) const override;\\n41:   Allocator* getCUDADeviceAllocator() const override;\\n42:   Allocator* getPinnedMemoryAllocator() const override;\\n43:   bool compiledWithCuDNN() const override;\\n44:   bool compiledWithMIOpen() const override;\\n45:   bool supportsDilatedConvolutionWithCuDNN() const override;\\n46:   bool supportsDepthwiseConvolutionWithCuDNN() const override;\\n47:   bool supportsBFloat16ConvolutionWithCuDNNv8() const override;\\n48:   bool hasCUDART() const override;\\n49:   long versionCUDART() const override;\\n50:   long versionCuDNN() const override;\\n51:   long versionRuntimeCuDNN() const override;\\n52:   long versionCuDNNFrontend() const override;\\n53:   long versionMIOpen() const override;\\n54:   std::string showConfig() const override;\\n55:   double batchnormMinEpsilonCuDNN() const override;\\n56:   int64_t cuFFTGetPlanCacheMaxSize(DeviceIndex device_index) const override;\\n57:   void cuFFTSetPlanCacheMaxSize(DeviceIndex device_index, int64_t max_size) const override;\\n58:   int64_t cuFFTGetPlanCacheSize(DeviceIndex device_index) const override;\\n59:   void cuFFTClearPlanCache(DeviceIndex device_index) const override;\\n60:   int getNumGPUs() const override;\\n61:   DeviceIndex deviceCount() const override;\\n62:   DeviceIndex getCurrentDevice() const override;\\n63: \\n64: #ifdef USE_ROCM\\n65:   bool isGPUArch(const std::vector<std::string>& archs, DeviceIndex device_index = -1) const override;\\n66: #endif\\n67:   void deviceSynchronize(DeviceIndex device_index) const override;\\n68: };\\n69: \\n70: } // at::cuda::detail\\n71: \", \"url\": \"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--5811a8d7@5811a8d7da873dd699ff6687092c225caffcf1bb/-/blob/aten/src/ATen/cuda/detail/CUDAHooks.h\"}, \"isError\": false}"

The CUDA library MUST \\\\\\\"\\\\n35:   \\\\\\\"be loaded, EVEN IF you don't directly use any symbols from the CUDA library! \\\\\\\"\\\\n36:   \\\\\\\"One common culprit is a lack of -Wl,--no-as-needed in your link arguments; many \\\\\\\"\\\\n37:   \\\\\\\"dynamic linkers will delete dynamic library dependencies if you don't \\\\\\\"\\\\n38:   \\\\\\\"depend on any of their symbols. How do I\\\\n46: // decide if a function should live in this class?  There are two tests:\\\\n47: //\\\\n48: //  1. Does the *implementation* of this function require linking against\\\\n49: //     CUDA libraries?\\\\n50: //\\\\n51: //  2. Is this function *called* from non-CUDA ATen code?\\\\n52: //\\\\n53: // (2) should filter out many ostensible use-cases, since many times a CUDA\\\\n54: // function provided by ATen is only really ever used by actual CUDA code.\\\\n55: //\\\\n56: // TODO: Consider putting the stub definitions in another class, so that one\\\\n57: // never forgets to implement each virtual function in the real implementation\\\\n58: // in CUDAHooks. // squelch -Werror=non-virtual-dtor\\\\n62:   ~CUDAHooksInterface() override = default;\\\\n63: \\\\n64:   // Initialize THCState and, transitively, the CUDA state\\\\n65:   void init() const override {\\\\n66:     TORCH_CHECK(false, \\\\\\\"Cannot initialize CUDA without ATen_cuda library. \\\\\\\", CUDA_HELP);\\\\n67:   }\\\\n68: \\\\n69:   const Generator\\\\u0026 getDefaultGenerator(\\\\n70: {

  "file_text": "#pragma once\n\n#include <ATen/detail/CUDAHooksInterface.h>\n\n#include <ATen/Generator.h>\n\n// TODO: No need to have this whole header, we can just put it all in\n// the cpp file\n\nnamespace at::cuda::detail {\n\n// Set the callback to initialize Magma, which is set by\n// torch_cuda_cu. This indirection is required so magma_init is called\n// in the same library where Magma will be used.\nTORCH_CUDA_CPP_API void set_magma_init_fn(void (*magma_init_fn)());\n\n\n// The real implementation of CUDAHooksInterface\nstruct CUDAHooks : public at::CUDAHooksInterface {\n  CUDAHooks(at::CUDAHooksArgs) {}\n  void init() const override;\n  Device getDeviceFromPtr(void* data) const override;\n  bool isPinnedPtr(const void* data) const override;\n  const Generator& getDefaultGenerator(\n      DeviceIndex device_index = -1) const override;\n  Generator getNewGenerator(\n      DeviceIndex device_index = -1) const override;\n  bool hasCUDA() const override;\n  bool hasMAGMA() const override;\n  bool hasCuDNN() const override;\n  bool hasCuSOLVER() const override;\n  bool hasCuBLASLt() const override;\n  bool hasROCM() const override;\n  bool hasCKSDPA() const override;\n  bool hasCKGEMM() const override;\n  const at::cuda::NVRTC& nvrtc() const override;\n  DeviceIndex current_device() const override;\n  bool isBuilt() const override {return true;}\n  bool isAvailable() const override {return hasCUDA();}\n  bool hasPrimaryContext(DeviceIndex device_index) const override;\n  Allocator* getCUDADeviceAllocator() const override;\n  Allocator* getPinnedMemoryAllocator() const override;\n  bool compiledWithCuDNN() const override;\n  bool compiledWithMIOpen() const override;\n  bool supportsDilatedConvolutionWithCuDNN() const override;\n  bool supportsDepthwiseConvolutionWithCuDNN() const override;\n  bool supportsBFloat16ConvolutionWithCuDNNv8() const override;\n  bool hasCUDART() const override;\n  long versionCUDART() const override;\n  long versionCuDNN() const override;\n  long versionRuntimeCuDNN() const override;\n  long versionCuDNNFrontend() const override;\n  long versionMIOpen() const override;\n  std::string showConfig() const override;\n  double batchnormMinEpsilonCuDNN() const override;\n  int64_t cuFFTGetPlanCacheMaxSize(DeviceIndex device_index) const override;\n  void cuFFTSetPlanCacheMaxSize(DeviceIndex device_index, int64_t max_size) const override;\n  int64_t cuFFTGetPlanCacheSize(DeviceIndex device_index) const override;\n  void cuFFTClearPlanCache(DeviceIndex device_index) const override;\n  int getNumGPUs() const override;\n  DeviceIndex deviceCount() const override;\n  DeviceIndex getCurrentDevice() const override;\n\n#ifdef USE_ROCM\n  bool isGPUArch(const std::vector<std::string>& archs, DeviceIndex device_index = -1) const override;\n#endif\n  void deviceSynchronize(DeviceIndex device_index) const override;\n};\n\n} // at::cuda::detail\n",

{
  "command": "create",
  "file_text": "#pragma once\n\n#include <c10/core/Allocator.h>\n#include <c10/util/Exception.h>\n#include <c10/util/Registry.h>\n\n#include <ATen/detail/AcceleratorHooksInterface.h>\n\n// NB: Class must live in `at` due to limitations of Registry.h.\nnamespace at {\n\n// Forward-declares at::cuda::NVRTC\nnamespace cuda {\nstruct NVRTC;\n} // namespace cuda\n\n#ifdef _MSC_VER\nconstexpr const char* CUDA_HELP =\n  \"PyTorch splits its backend into two shared libraries: a CPU library \"\n  \"and a CUDA library; this error has occurred because you are trying \"\n  \"to use some CUDA functionality, but the CUDA library has not been \"\n  \"loaded by the dynamic linker for some reason.  The CUDA library MUST \"\n  \"be loaded, EVEN IF you don't directly use any symbols from the CUDA library! \"\n  \"One common culprit is a lack of -INCLUDE:?warp_size@cuda@at@@YAHXZ \"\n  \"in your link arguments; many dynamic linkers will delete dynamic library \"\n  \"dependencies if you don't depend on any of their symbols.  You can check \"\n  \"if this has occurred by using link on your binary to see if there is a \"\n  \"dependency on *_cuda.dll library.\";\n#else\nconstexpr const char* CUDA_HELP =\n  \"PyTorch splits its backend into two shared libraries: a CPU library \"\n  \"and a CUDA library; this error has occurred because you are trying \"\n  \"to use some CUDA functionality, but the CUDA library has not been \"\n  \"loaded by the dynamic linker for some reason.  The CUDA library MUST \"\n  \"be loaded, EVEN IF you don't directly use any symbols from the CUDA library! \"\n  \"One common culprit is a lack of -Wl,--no-as-needed in your link arguments; many \"\n  \"dynamic linkers will delete dynamic library dependencies if you don't \"\n  \"depend on any of their symbols.  You can check if this has occurred by \"\n  \"using ldd on your binary to see if there is a dependency on *_cuda.so \"\n  \"library.\";\n#endif\n\n// The CUDAHooksInterface is an omnibus interface for any CUDA functionality\n// which we may want to call into from CPU code (and thus must be dynamically\n// dispatched, to allow for separate compilation of CUDA code).  How do I\n// decide if a function should live in this class?  There are two tests:\n//\n//  1. Does the *implementation* of this function require linking against\n//     CUDA libraries?\n//\n//  2. Is this function *called* from non-CUDA ATen code?\n//\n// (2) should filter out many ostensible use-cases, since many times a CUDA\n// function provided by ATen is only really ever used by actual CUDA code.\n//\n// TODO: Consider putting the stub definitions in another class, so that one\n// never forgets to implement each virtual function in the real implementation\n// in CUDAHooks.  This probably doesn't buy us much though.\nstruct TORCH_API CUDAHooksInterface : AcceleratorHooksInterface {\n  // This should never actually be implemented, but it is used to\n  // squelch -Werror=non-virtual-dtor\n  ~CUDAHooksInterface() override = default;\n\n  // Initialize THCState and, transitively, the CUDA state\n  void init() const override {\n    TORCH_CHECK(false, \"Cannot initialize CUDA without ATen_cuda library. \", CUDA_HELP);\n  }\n\n  const Generator& getDefaultGenerator(\n      [[maybe_unused]] DeviceIndex device_index = -1) const override {\n    TORCH_CHECK(\n        false,\n        \"Cannot get default CUDA generator without ATen_cuda library. \",\n        CUDA_HELP);\n  }\n\n  Generator getNewGenerator(\n      [[maybe_unused]] DeviceIndex device_index = -1) const override {\n    TORCH_CHECK(\n        false,\n        \"Cannot get CUDA generator without ATen_cuda library. \",\n        CUDA_HELP);\n  }\n\n  Device getDeviceFromPtr(void* /*data*/) const override {\n    TORCH_CHECK(false, \"Cannot get device of pointer on CUDA without ATen_cuda library. \", CUDA_HELP);\n  }\n\n  bool isPinnedPtr(const void* /*data*/)  const override {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  virtual bool hasCUDA() const {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  virtual bool hasCUDART() const {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  virtual bool hasMAGMA() const {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  virtual bool hasCuDNN() const {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  virtual bool hasCuSOLVER() const {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  virtual bool hasCuBLASLt() const {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  virtual bool hasROCM() const {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  virtual bool hasCKSDPA() const {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  virtual bool hasCKGEMM() const {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  virtual const at::cuda::NVRTC& nvrtc() const {\n    TORCH_CHECK(false, \"NVRTC requires CUDA. \", CUDA_HELP);\n  }\n\n  bool hasPrimaryContext(DeviceIndex device_index) const override {\n    TORCH_CHECK(false, \"Cannot call hasPrimaryContext(\", device_index, \") without ATen_cuda library. \", CUDA_HELP);\n  }\n\n  virtual DeviceIndex current_device() const {\n    return -1;\n  }\n\n  Allocator* getPinnedMemoryAllocator() const override {\n    TORCH_CHECK(false, \"Pinned memory requires CUDA. \", CUDA_HELP);\n  }\n\n  virtual Allocator* getCUDADeviceAllocator() const {\n    TORCH_CHECK(false, \"CUDADeviceAllocator requires CUDA. \", CUDA_HELP);\n  }\n\n  virtual bool compiledWithCuDNN() const {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  virtual bool compiledWithMIOpen() const {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  virtual bool supportsDilatedConvolutionWithCuDNN() const {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  virtual bool supportsDepthwiseConvolutionWithCuDNN() const {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  virtual bool supportsBFloat16ConvolutionWithCuDNNv8() const {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  virtual long versionCuDNN() const {\n    TORCH_CHECK(false, \"Cannot query cuDNN version without ATen_cuda library. \", CUDA_HELP);\n  }\n\n  virtual long versionRuntimeCuDNN() const {\n    TORCH_CHECK(false, \"Cannot query cuDNN version without ATen_cuda library. \", CUDA_HELP);\n  }\n\n  virtual long versionCuDNNFrontend() const {\n    TORCH_CHECK(false, \"Cannot query cuDNN Frontend version without ATen_cuda library. \", CUDA_HELP);\n  }\n\n  virtual long versionMIOpen() const {\n    TORCH_CHECK(false, \"Cannot query MIOpen version without ATen_cuda library. \", CUDA_HELP);\n  }\n\n  virtual long versionCUDART() const {\n    TORCH_CHECK(false, \"Cannot query CUDART version without ATen_cuda library. \", CUDA_HELP);\n  }\n\n  virtual std::string showConfig() const {\n    TORCH_CHECK(false, \"Cannot query detailed CUDA version without ATen_cuda library. \", CUDA_HELP);\n  }\n\n  virtual double batchnormMinEpsilonCuDNN() const {\n    TORCH_CHECK(false,\n        \"Cannot query batchnormMinEpsilonCuDNN() without ATen_cuda library. \", CUDA_HELP);\n  }\n\n  virtual int64_t cuFFTGetPlanCacheMaxSize(DeviceIndex /*device_index*/) const {\n    TORCH_CHECK(false, \"Cannot access cuFFT plan cache without ATen_cuda library. \", CUDA_HELP);\n  }\n\n  virtual void cuFFTSetPlanCacheMaxSize(DeviceIndex /*device_index*/, int64_t /*max_size*/) const {\n    TORCH_CHECK(false, \"Cannot access cuFFT plan cache without ATen_cuda library. \", CUDA_HELP);\n  }\n\n  virtual int64_t cuFFTGetPlanCacheSize(DeviceIndex /*device_index*/) const {\n    TORCH_CHECK(false, \"Cannot access cuFFT plan cache without ATen_cuda library. \", CUDA_HELP);\n  }\n\n  virtual void cuFFTClearPlanCache(DeviceIndex /*device_index*/) const {\n    TORCH_CHECK(false, \"Cannot access cuFFT plan cache without ATen_cuda library. \", CUDA_HELP);\n  }\n\n  virtual int getNumGPUs() const {\n    return 0;\n  }\n\n#ifdef USE_ROCM\n  virtual bool isGPUArch(const std::vector<std::string>& /*archs*/, DeviceIndex = -1 /*device_index*/) const {\n    TORCH_CHECK(false, \"Cannot check GPU arch without ATen_cuda library. \", CUDA_HELP);\n  }\n#endif\n\n  virtual void deviceSynchronize(DeviceIndex /*device_index*/) const {\n    TORCH_CHECK(false, \"Cannot synchronize CUDA device without ATen_cuda library. \", CUDA_HELP);\n  }\n};\n\n// NB: dummy argument to suppress \"ISO C++11 requires at least one argument\n// for the \"...\" in a variadic macro\"\nstruct TORCH_API CUDAHooksArgs {};\n\nTORCH_DECLARE_REGISTRY(CUDAHooksRegistry, CUDAHooksInterface, CUDAHooksArgs);\n#define REGISTER_CUDA_HOOKS(clsname) \\\n  C10_REGISTER_CLASS(CUDAHooksRegistry, clsname, clsname)\n\nnamespace detail {\nTORCH_API const CUDAHooksInterface& getCUDAHooks();\n} // namespace detail\n} // namespace at\n",
  "path": "/workspace/aten/src/ATen/detail/CUDAHooksInterface.h",
  "security_risk": "LOW"
}

44. str_replace_editor @ 2026-03-11T18:17:43.284848

{
  "command": "create",
  "file_text": "// The clang-tidy job seems to complain that it can't find cudnn.h without this.\n// This file should only be compiled if this condition holds, so it should be\n// safe.\n#if defined(USE_CUDNN) || defined(USE_ROCM)\n#include <ATen/detail/CUDAHooksInterface.h>\n#include <torch/csrc/utils/pybind.h>\n\n#include <tuple>\n\nnamespace {\nusing version_tuple = std::tuple<size_t, size_t, size_t>;\n}\n\n#ifdef USE_CUDNN\n#include <cudnn.h>\n\nnamespace {\n\nversion_tuple getCompileVersion() {\n  return version_tuple(CUDNN_MAJOR, CUDNN_MINOR, CUDNN_PATCHLEVEL);\n}\n\nversion_tuple getRuntimeVersion() {\n#ifndef USE_STATIC_CUDNN\n  int major = 0, minor = 0, patch = 0;\n  cudnnGetProperty(MAJOR_VERSION, &major);\n  cudnnGetProperty(MINOR_VERSION, &minor);\n  cudnnGetProperty(PATCH_LEVEL, &patch);\n  return version_tuple((size_t)major, (size_t)minor, (size_t)patch);\n#else\n  return getCompileVersion();\n#endif\n}\n\nsize_t getVersionInt() {\n  return at::detail::getCUDAHooks().versionRuntimeCuDNN();\n}\n\n} // namespace\n#elif defined(USE_ROCM)\n#include <miopen/miopen.h>\n#include <miopen/version.h>\n\nnamespace {\n\nversion_tuple getCompileVersion() {\n  return version_tuple(\n      MIOPEN_VERSION_MAJOR, MIOPEN_VERSION_MINOR, MIOPEN_VERSION_PATCH);\n}\n\nversion_tuple getRuntimeVersion() {\n  // MIOpen doesn't include runtime version info before 2.3.0\n#if (MIOPEN_VERSION_MAJOR > 2) || \\\n    (MIOPEN_VERSION_MAJOR == 2 && MIOPEN_VERSION_MINOR > 2)\n  size_t major, minor, patch;\n  miopenGetVersion(&major, &minor, &patch);\n  return version_tuple(major, minor, patch);\n#else\n  return getCompileVersion();\n#endif\n}\n\nsize_t getVersionInt() {\n  // miopen version is MAJOR*1000000 + MINOR*1000 + PATCH\n  auto [major, minor, patch] = getRuntimeVersion();\n  return major * 1000000 + minor * 1000 + patch;\n}\n\n} // namespace\n#endif\n\nnamespace torch::cuda::shared {\n\nvoid initCudnnBindings(PyObject* module) {\n  auto m = py::handle(module).cast<py::module>();\n\n  auto cudnn = m.def_submodule(\"_cudnn\", \"libcudnn.so bindings\");\n\n  py::enum_<cudnnRNNMode_t>(cudnn, \"RNNMode\")\n      .value(\"rnn_relu\", CUDNN_RNN_RELU)\n      .value(\"rnn_tanh\", CUDNN_RNN_TANH)\n      .value(\"lstm\", CUDNN_LSTM)\n      .value(\"gru\", CUDNN_GRU);\n\n  // The runtime version check in python needs to distinguish cudnn from miopen\n#ifdef USE_CUDNN\n  cudnn.attr(\"is_cuda\") = true;\n#else\n  cudnn.attr(\"is_cuda\") = false;\n#endif\n\n  cudnn.def(\"getRuntimeVersion\", getRuntimeVersion);\n  cudnn.def(\"getCompileVersion\", getCompileVersion);\n  cudnn.def(\"getVersionInt\", getVersionInt);\n}\n\n} // namespace torch::cuda::shared\n#endif\n",
  "path": "/workspace/torch/csrc/cuda/shared/cudnn.cpp",
  "security_risk": "LOW"
}

  "file_text": "#pragma once\n\n#include <ATen/BlasBackend.h>\n#include <ATen/CPUGeneratorImpl.h>\n#include <ATen/DeviceAccelerator.h>\n#include <ATen/LinalgBackend.h>\n#include <ATen/ROCmFABackend.h>\n#include <ATen/SDPBackend.h>\n#include <ATen/core/ATenGeneral.h>\n#include <ATen/core/DeprecatedTypeProperties.h>\n#include <ATen/core/Generator.h>\n#include <ATen/core/LegacyTypeDispatch.h>\n#include <ATen/detail/AcceleratorHooksInterface.h>\n#include <ATen/detail/CUDAHooksInterface.h>\n#include <ATen/detail/HIPHooksInterface.h>\n#include <ATen/detail/HPUHooksInterface.h>\n#include <ATen/detail/IPUHooksInterface.h>\n#include <ATen/detail/MAIAHooksInterface.h>\n#include <ATen/detail/MPSHooksInterface.h>\n#include <ATen/detail/MTIAHooksInterface.h>\n#include <ATen/detail/PrivateUse1HooksInterface.h>\n#include <ATen/detail/XPUHooksInterface.h>\n#include <c10/core/QEngine.h>\n#include <c10/core/impl/DeviceGuardImplInterface.h>\n#include <c10/util/CallOnce.h>\n#include <c10/util/Exception.h>\n#include <c10/util/env.h>\n#include <c10/util/irange.h>\n\n#include <cstdint>\n#include <map>\n#include <mutex>\n\nnamespace at {\n\nclass Tensor;\n\nenum class TORCH_API Float32MatmulPrecision { HIGHEST, HIGH, MEDIUM };\n\nclass TORCH_API Context {\n public:\n  Context();\n\n  const Generator& defaultGenerator(Device device) {\n    c10::DeviceType device_type = device.type();\n    lazyInitDevice(device_type);\n\n    if (device_type == at::kCPU) {\n      return at::detail::getDefaultCPUGenerator();\n    } else {\n      return getAcceleratorHooksInterface(device_type)\n          .getDefaultGenerator(device.index());\n    }\n  }\n\n  const AcceleratorHooksInterface& getAcceleratorHooksInterface(\n      std::optional<c10::DeviceType> opt_device_type = std::nullopt) {\n    if (!opt_device_type.has_value()) {\n      opt_device_type = at::getAccelerator(true);\n    }\n    if (opt_device_type == at::kCUDA) {\n      return at::detail::getCUDAHooks();\n    } else if (opt_device_type == at::kXPU) {\n      return at::detail::getXPUHooks();\n    } else if (opt_device_type == at::kMPS) {\n      return at::detail::getMPSHooks();\n    } else if (opt_device_type == at::kPrivateUse1) {\n      return at::detail::getPrivateUse1Hooks();\n    } else if (opt_device_type == at::kMTIA) {\n      return at::detail::getMTIAHooks();\n    } else if (opt_device_type == at::kHIP) {\n      return at::detail::getHIPHooks();\n    } else if (opt_device_type == at::kHPU) {\n      return at::detail::getHPUHooks();\n    } else {\n      TORCH_CHECK(\n          false,\n          opt_device_type.has_value()\n              ? c10::DeviceTypeName(opt_device_type.value())\n              : \"None\",\n          \" device type not an accelerator.\");\n    }\n  }\n\n  Device getDeviceFromPtr(void* data, c10::DeviceType device_type) {\n    lazyInitDevice(device_type);\n\n    if (device_type == at::kCPU) {\n      return c10::DeviceType::CPU;\n    } else {\n      return getAcceleratorHooksInterface(device_type).getDeviceFromPtr(data);\n    }\n  }\n\n  bool isPinnedPtr(\n      const void* data,\n      std::optional<c10::DeviceType> device_type = std::nullopt) {\n    auto opt_device_type =\n        device_type.has_value() ? device_type : at::getAccelerator();\n    if (!opt_device_type.has_value() || // there is no accelerator\n        !at::isAccelerator(\n            opt_device_type.value())) { // passed device not an accelerator\n      return false;\n    }\n    if (!init_[static_cast<int8_t>(opt_device_type.value())].test_once()) {\n      // If the device is not initialized, no pointer can be pinned for it\n      return false;\n    }\n    return getAcceleratorHooksInterface(opt_device_type).isPinnedPtr(data);\n  }\n\n  Allocator* getPinnedMemoryAllocator(\n      std::optional<c10::DeviceType> device_type = std::nullopt) {\n    auto opt_device_type =\n        device_type.has_value() ? device_type : at::getAccelerator();\n    if (opt_device_type) {\n      lazyInitDevice(opt_device_type.value());\n    }\n    return getAcceleratorHooksInterface(device_type).getPinnedMemoryAllocator();\n  }\n\n  void lazyInitDevice(c10::DeviceType device_type) {\n    if (device_type != at::kCPU) {\n      c10::call_once(init_[static_cast<int8_t>(device_type)], [&] {\n        getAcceleratorHooksInterface(device_type).init();\n      });\n    }\n  }\n\n  static bool hasOpenMP();\n  static bool hasMKL();\n  static bool hasKleidiAI();\n  static bool hasLAPACK();\n  static bool hasMKLDNN();\n  static bool ckSupported();\n  static bool hasEigenSparse();\n  static bool hasMAGMA() {\n    return detail::getCUDAHooks().hasMAGMA();\n  }\n  static bool hasCUDA() {\n    return detail::getCUDAHooks().hasCUDA();\n  }\n  static bool hasMTIA() {\n    return detail::getMTIAHooks().hasMTIA();\n  }\n  static bool hasCUDART() {\n    return detail::getCUDAHooks().hasCUDART();\n  }\n  static long versionCUDART() {\n    return detail::getCUDAHooks().versionCUDART();\n  }\n  static bool hasCuDNN() {\n    return detail::getCUDAHooks().hasCuDNN();\n  }\n  static long versionCuDNN() {\n    return detail::getCUDAHooks().versionCuDNN();\n  }\n  static long versionRuntimeCuDNN() {\n    return detail::getCUDAHooks().versionRuntimeCuDNN();\n  }\n  static long versionCuDNNFrontend() {\n    return detail::getCUDAHooks().versionCuDNNFrontend();\n  }\n  static bool hasCuSOLVER() {\n    return detail::getCUDAHooks().hasCuSOLVER();\n  }\n  static bool hasCuBLASLt() {\n    return detail::getCUDAHooks().hasCuBLASLt();\n  }\n  static bool hasROCM() {\n    return detail::getCUDAHooks().hasROCM();\n  }\n  static bool hasCKSDPA() {\n    return detail::getCUDAHooks().hasCKSDPA();\n  }\n  static bool hasCKGEMM() {\n    return detail::getCUDAHooks().hasCKGEMM();\n  }\n  static bool hasHIP() {\n    return detail::getHIPHooks().hasHIP();\n  }\n  static bool hasMPS() {\n    return detail::getMPSHooks().hasMPS();\n  }\n  static bool hasIPU() {\n    return c10::impl::hasDeviceGuardImpl(c10::DeviceType::IPU);\n  }\n  static bool hasXLA() {\n    return c10::impl::hasDeviceGuardImpl(c10::DeviceType::XLA);\n  }\n  static bool hasXPU() {\n    return detail::getXPUHooks().hasXPU();\n  }\n  static bool hasLazy() {\n    return c10::impl::hasDeviceGuardImpl(c10::DeviceType::Lazy);\n  }\n  static bool hasMAIA() {\n    return c10::impl::hasDeviceGuardImpl(c10::DeviceType::MAIA);\n  }\n  static bool hasHPU() {\n    return detail::getHPUHooks().hasHPU();\n  }\n\n  static const at::cuda::NVRTC& getNVRTC() {\n    return detail::getCUDAHooks().nvrtc();\n  }\n\n  static bool setFlushDenormal(bool on);\n\n  // NB: This method is *purely* whether or not a user requested\n  // that CuDNN was enabled, it doesn't actually say anything about\n  // whether or not CuDNN is actually usable.  Use cudnn_is_acceptable\n  // to test this instead\n  bool userEnabledCuDNN() const;\n  void setUserEnabledCuDNN(bool e);\n  bool userEnabledMkldnn() const;\n  void setUserEnabledMkldnn(bool e);\n  bool benchmarkCuDNN() const;\n  void setBenchmarkCuDNN(bool);\n  int benchmarkLimitCuDNN() const;\n  void setBenchmarkLimitCuDNN(int);\n  bool immediateMiopen() const;\n  void setImmediateMiopen(bool);\n  bool deterministicCuDNN() const;\n  void setDeterministicCuDNN(bool);\n  bool deterministicMkldnn() const;\n  void setDeterministicMkldnn(bool);\n  bool userEnabledNNPACK() const;\n  void setUserEnabledNNPACK(bool e);\n\n  // Note [Disabling Fused SDP Kernels]\n  // ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n  // Flash and Memory Efficient SDP kernels are enabled by default.\n  // However, they can be disabled by setting\n  // at::globalContext().setUserEnabledFlashSDP(false) flag.\n  // This is useful for debugging purposes. For example, if you want to\n  // compare the performance of the flash SDP kernels with the unfused\n  // kernel, you can disable the flash SDP kernels. By disabling\n  // the math SDP kernel, you can force your code to use flash kernels.\n  // The math SDP kernel can be disabled by setting\n  // at::globalContext().setUserEnabledMathSDP(false) flag.\n  void setSDPPriorityOrder(const std::vector<int64_t>& order);\n  std::array<at::SDPBackend, at::num_sdp_backends> sDPPriorityOrder();\n\n  void setSDPUseFlash(bool);\n  bool userEnabledFlashSDP() const;\n\n  void setSDPUseMemEfficient(bool);\n  bool userEnabledMemEfficientSDP() const;\n\n  void setSDPUseMath(bool);\n  bool userEnabledMathSDP() const;\n\n  void setSDPUseCuDNN(bool);\n  bool userEnabledCuDNNSDP() const;\n\n  void setAllowFP16BF16ReductionMathSDP(bool);\n  bool allowFP16BF16ReductionMathSDP() const;\n\n  void setSDPUseOverrideable(bool);\n  bool userEnabledOverrideableSDP() const;\n\n  at::LinalgBackend linalgPreferredBackend() const;\n  void setLinalgPreferredBackend(at::LinalgBackend);\n\n  at::BlasBackend blasPreferredBackend();\n  void setBlasPreferredBackend(at::BlasBackend);\n\n  at::ROCmFABackend getROCmFAPreferredBackend();\n  void setROCmFAPreferredBackend(at::ROCmFABackend);\n\n  // Note [Enabling Deterministic Operations]\n  // ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n  // Operations in PyTorch that normally act nondeterministically, but have an\n  // alternate deterministic implementation, should satisfy the following\n  // requirements:\n  //\n  // * Include this comment: \"See Note [Enabling Deterministic Operations]\"\n  //\n  // * Check the value of `at::globalContext().deterministicAlgorithms()` to\n  // toggle\n  //   between nondeterministic and deterministic implementations.\n  //\n  // * Have an entry in the list of PyTorch operations that toggle between\n  // nondeterministic\n  //   and deterministic implementations, in the docstring of\n  //   `use_deterministic_algorithms()` in torch/__init__.py\n  //\n  // `example_func()` below shows an example of toggling between\n  // nondeterministic and deterministic implementations:\n  //\n  //    void example_func() {\n  //      // See Note [Enabling Deterministic Operations]\n  //      if (at::globalContext().deterministicAlgorithms()) {\n  //        example_func_deterministic();\n  //      } else {\n  //        example_func_nondeterministic();\n  //      }\n  //    }\n\n  bool deterministicAlgorithms() const;\n  bool deterministicAlgorithmsWarnOnly() const;\n  void setDeterministicAlgorithms(bool, bool);\n  bool deterministicFillUninitializedMemory() const;\n  void setDeterministicFillUninitializedMemory(bool);\n\n  // Note [Writing Nondeterministic Operations]\n  // ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n  // Operations in PyTorch that act nondeterministically and do not have an\n  // alternate deterministic implementation should satisfy the following\n  // requirements:\n  //\n  // * Include this comment: \"See Note [Writing Nondeterministic Operations]\"\n  //\n  // * Include a comment explaining why the operation is nondeterministic.\n  //\n  // * Throw an error when `Context::deterministicAlgorithms()` is true. Most\n  //   of the time, this should be accomplished by calling\n  //   `at::globalContext().alertNotDeterminstic()`.  However, if the\n  //   nondeterministic behavior is caused by the CuBLAS workspace\n  //   configuration in CUDA >= 10.2,\n  //   `at::globalContext().alertCuBLASConfigNotDeterministic()` should be\n  //   called instead (in this case, a comment explaining why the operation is\n  //   nondeterministic is not necessary). See below for details on these\n  //   methods.\n  //\n  // * Have an entry in the list of nondeterministic PyTorch operations in the\n  //   docstring of `use_deterministic_algorithms()` in torch/__init__.py\n  //\n  // * Have a test function in `test/test_torch.py` whose name begins with\n  //   `test_nondeterministic_alert_`. Alternatively, if CuBLAS workspace\n  //   configuration is the reason for nondeterminism, the operation should be\n  //   included in the `test_cublas_config_nondeterministic_alert` test. Any new\n  //   tests should ideally follow a pattern similar to the existing ones.\n  //\n  // `example_func()` below shows an example of the comments and error-throwing\n  // code for a nondeterministic operation:\n  //\n  //    void example_func() {\n  //      // See Note [Writing Nondeterministic Operations]\n  //      // Nondeterministic because <reason>\n  //      at::globalContext().alertNondeterministic(\"example_func\");\n  //      ...\n  //    }\n\n  // Throws an error if `Context::deterministicAlgorithms()` is true\n  static void alertNotDeterministic(std::string_view const& caller);\n\n  // Throws an error if `Context::deterministicAlgorithms()` is true, CUDA\n  // >= 10.2, and CUBLAS_WORKSPACE_CONFIG is not set to either \":16:8\" or\n  // \":4096:8\". For more details:\n  // https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cublas/index.html#results-reproducibility\n  void alertCuBLASConfigNotDeterministic() const;\n\n  void setFloat32MatmulPrecision(const std::string& s);\n  void setFloat32Precision(\n      const std::string& backend,\n      const std::string& op,\n      const std::string& s);\n  bool allowTF32CuDNN(const std::string& op = std::string()) const;\n  void setAllowTF32CuDNN(bool);\n  bool allowTF32OneDNN() const;\n  void setAllowTF32OneDNN(bool);\n  bool allowTF32CuBLAS() const;\n  void setAllowTF32CuBLAS(bool);\n  Float32MatmulPrecision float32MatmulPrecision() const;\n  std::string float32Precision(\n      const std::string& backend,\n      const std::string& op) const;\n  bool allowFP16ReductionCuBLAS() const;\n  void setAllowFP16ReductionCuBLAS(bool);\n  bool allowBF16ReductionCuBLAS() const;\n  void setAllowBF16ReductionCuBLAS(bool);\n  bool allowFP16AccumulationCuBLAS() const;\n  void setAllowFP16AccumulationCuBLAS(bool);\n\n  // Matmuls can use a so-called \"persistent\" kernel which launches one CUDA\n  // block for each SM on the GPU, and each block then iterates over multiple\n  // output tiles. This allows to use software pipelining to hide the begin/end\n  // latencies (e.g., epilogue), especially when only one tile fits per SM.\n  // However, if some SMs are busy (e.g., with a background NCCL kernel), the\n  // matmul's blocks will be scheduled in two waves and, in the absence of some\n  // smart load balancing, the kernel will take twice as long. This flag allows\n  // to make matmuls target only a subset of the SMs, so they can fully schedule\n  // even next to a comms kernel, and only be a few percent slower.\n  std::optional<int32_t> _SMCarveout_EXPERIMENTAL() const;\n  void _setSMCarveout_EXPERIMENTAL(std::optional<int32_t>);\n\n  at::QEngine qEngine() const;\n  void setQEngine(at::QEngine e);\n  static const std::vector<at::QEngine>& supportedQEngines();\n  static bool isXNNPACKAvailable();\n  void setCheckSparseTensorInvariants(bool e);\n  bool checkSparseTensorInvariants() const;\n  // This method is used to release the original weight after pre-packing.\n  // It should be called once before loading/running the model.\n  // NB: By default it is set to true for mobile builds.\n  void setReleaseWeightsWhenPrepacking(bool e);\n  bool releaseWeightsWhenPrepacking() const;\n\n  void setDisplayVmapFallbackWarnings(bool enabled);\n  bool areVmapFallbackWarningsEnabled() const;\n\n  bool isDefaultMobileCPUAllocatorSet();\n  void setDefaultMobileCPUAllocator();\n  void unsetDefaultMobileCPUAllocator();\n  bool allowFP16ReductionCPU() const;\n  void setAllowFP16ReductionCPU(bool);\n\n  // Preserved for BC\n  void lazyInitCUDA() {\n    TORCH_WARN_DEPRECATION(\n        \"lazyInitCUDA is deprecated. Please use lazyInitDevice(at::kCUDA) instead.\")\n    lazyInitDevice(at::kCUDA);\n  }\n  void lazyInitHIP() {\n    TORCH_WARN_DEPRECATION(\n        \"lazyInitHIP is deprecated. Please use lazyInitDevice(at::kHIP) instead.\")\n    lazyInitDevice(at::kHIP);\n  }\n  void lazyInitXPU() {\n    TORCH_WARN_DEPRECATION(\n        \"lazyInitXPU is deprecated. Please use lazyInitDevice(at::kXPU) instead.\")\n    lazyInitDevice(at::kXPU);\n  }\n  void lazyInitMTIA() {\n    TORCH_WARN_DEPRECATION(\n        \"lazyInitMTIA is deprecated. Please use lazyInitDevice(at::kMTIA) instead.\")\n    lazyInitDevice(at::kMTIA);\n  }\n  void lazyInitPrivateUse1() {\n    TORCH_WARN_DEPRECATION(\n        \"lazyInitPrivateUse1 is deprecated. Please use lazyInitDevice(at::kPrivateUse1) instead.\")\n    lazyInitDevice(at::kPrivateUse1);\n  }\n\n private:\n  static bool checkCuBLASConfigDeterministic();\n  std::array<c10::once_flag, at::COMPILE_TIME_MAX_DEVICE_TYPES> init_;\n  bool enabled_cudnn = true;\n  bool deterministic_cudnn = false;\n  bool deterministic_mkldnn = false;\n  bool _deterministic_algorithms = false;\n  bool _deterministic_algorithms_warn_only = false;\n  bool _deterministic_fill_uninitialized_memory = true;\n  std::array<at::SDPBackend, at::num_sdp_backends> sdp_priority_order = {\n      at::SDPBackend::flash_attention,\n      at::SDPBackend::efficient_attention,\n      at::SDPBackend::math,\n      at::SDPBackend::cudnn_attention,\n      at::SDPBackend::overrideable};\n  bool enabled_flashSDP = true;\n  bool enabled_mem_efficientSDP = true;\n  bool enabled_mathSDP = true;\n  bool enabled_cudnnSDP = true;\n  bool enabled_overrideable = true;\n  bool allow_fp16_bf16_reduction_mathSDP = false;\n  bool benchmark_cudnn = false;\n  bool immediate_miopen = false;\n  Float32MatmulPrecision float32_matmul_precision =\n      c10::utils::check_env(\"TORCH_ALLOW_TF32_CUBLAS_OVERRIDE\") == true\n      ? at::Float32MatmulPrecision::HIGH\n      : at::Float32MatmulPrecision::HIGHEST;\n  int benchmark_limit_cudnn = 10;\n  bool allow_tf32_cudnn = true;\n  bool allow_fp16_reduction_cublas = true;\n  bool allow_bf16_reduction_cublas = true;\n  bool allow_fp16_accumulation_cublas = false;\n  std::optional<int32_t> sm_carveout = std::nullopt;\n  bool enabled_mkldnn = true;\n  bool allow_tf32_onednn = false;\n  bool enabled_nnpack = true;\n  at::LinalgBackend linalg_preferred_backend =\n      (c10::utils::check_env(\"TORCH_LINALG_PREFER_CUSOLVER\") == true ||\n       c10::utils::check_env(\"TORCH_LINALG_PREFER_HIPSOLVER\") == true) // alias\n      ? at::LinalgBackend::Cusolver\n      : at::LinalgBackend::Default;\n  at::BlasBackend blas_preferred_backend =\n      (c10::utils::check_env(\"TORCH_BLAS_PREFER_CUBLASLT\") == true ||\n       c10::utils::check_env(\"TORCH_BLAS_PREFER_HIPBLASLT\") == true) // alias\n      ? at::BlasBackend::Cublaslt\n      : at::BlasBackend::Default;\n  at::ROCmFABackend rocm_fa_preferred_backend =\n      c10::utils::check_env(\"TORCH_ROCM_FA_PREFER_CK\") == true\n      ? at::ROCmFABackend::Ck\n      : at::ROCmFABackend::Default;\n#ifdef C10_MOBILE\n  bool release_original_weights = true;\n#else\n  bool release_original_weights = false;\n#endif\n  bool display_vmap_fallback_warnings_ = false;\n  std::atomic<at::QEngine> quantized_engine = at::QEngine::NoQEngine;\n  bool enable_sparse_tensor_invariant_checks = false;\n  bool allow_fp16_reduction_cpu = false;\n\n  std::map<std::string, std::map<std::string, std::string>> fp32_precision = {\n      {\"generic\", {{\"all\", \"none\"}}},\n      {\"mkldnn\",\n       {{\"matmul\", \"none\"},\n        {\"conv\", \"none\"},\n        {\"rnn\", \"none\"},\n        {\"all\", \"none\"}}},\n      {\"cuda\",\n       {{\"matmul\",\n         float32_matmul_precision == at::Float32MatmulPrecision::HIGHEST\n             ? \"none\"\n             : \"tf32\"},\n        {\"conv\", \"tf32\"},\n        {\"rnn\", \"tf32\"},\n        {\"all\", \"none\"}}},\n  };\n\n  Allocator* prev_allocator_ptr_{nullptr};\n};\n\nTORCH_API Context& globalContext();\n\ninline void init() {\n  globalContext();\n}\n\nTORCH_API Allocator* getCPUAllocator();\n\ninline DeprecatedTypeProperties& getDeprecatedTypeProperties(\n    Backend p,\n    ScalarType s) {\n  return globalDeprecatedTypePropertiesRegistry().getDeprecatedTypeProperties(\n      p, s);\n}\n\ninline DeprecatedTypeProperties& CPU(ScalarType s) {\n  return globalDeprecatedTypePropertiesRegistry().getDeprecatedTypeProperties(\n      Backend::CPU, s);\n}\n\ninline DeprecatedTypeProperties& CUDA(ScalarType s) {\n  return globalDeprecatedTypePropertiesRegistry().getDeprecatedTypeProperties(\n      Backend::CUDA, s);\n}\n\ninline DeprecatedTypeProperties& HIP(ScalarType s) {\n  return globalDeprecatedTypePropertiesRegistry().getDeprecatedTypeProperties(\n      Backend::HIP, s);\n}\n\ninline DeprecatedTypeProperties& MPS(ScalarType s) {\n  return globalDeprecatedTypePropertiesRegistry().getDeprecatedTypeProperties(\n      Backend::MPS, s);\n}\n\ninline bool hasCUDA() {\n  return globalContext().hasCUDA();\n}\n\ninline bool hasMTIA() {\n  return globalContext().hasMTIA();\n}\n\ninline bool hasHIP() {\n  return globalContext().hasHIP();\n}\n\ninline bool hasIPU() {\n  return globalContext().hasIPU();\n}\n\ninline bool hasXLA() {\n  return globalContext().hasXLA();\n}\n\ninline bool hasMPS() {\n  return globalContext().hasMPS();\n}\n\ninline bool hasMAIA() {\n  return globalContext().hasMAIA();\n}\n\ninline bool hasXPU() {\n  return globalContext().hasXPU();\n}\n\ninline bool hasHPU() {\n  return globalContext().hasHPU();\n}\n\n// Despite its name, this function returns the number of *CUDA* GPUs.\ninline size_t getNumGPUs() {\n  // WARNING: DO NOT ADD LOGIC TO HANDLE OTHER DEVICE TYPES TO THIS\n  // FUNCTION.  If you are interested in interrogating the number of\n  // devices for a specific device type, add that function to the\n  // relevant library (e.g., similar to at::cuda::device_count())\n  if (hasCUDA() && hasHIP()) {\n    TORCH_CHECK(\n        false,\n        \"Enabling both CUDA and HIP in ATen is not supported, as HIP masquerades \"\n        \"to be CUDA (e.g., when you say CUDA, on a HIP build of ATen, this actually \"\n        \"means HIP.  Rebuild PyTorch with one or the other disabled.\");\n  } else if (hasCUDA()) {\n    return detail::getCUDAHooks().deviceCount();\n  } else if (hasHIP()) {\n    return detail::getHIPHooks().getNumGPUs();\n  } else {\n    return 0;\n  }\n}\n\ninline bool hasOpenMP() {\n  return globalContext().hasOpenMP();\n}\n\ninline bool hasMKL() {\n  return globalContext().hasMKL();\n}\n\ninline bool hasKleidiAI() {\n  return globalContext().hasKleidiAI();\n}\n\ninline bool hasLAPACK() {\n  return globalContext().hasLAPACK();\n}\n\ninline bool hasEigenSparse() {\n  return globalContext().hasEigenSparse();\n}\n\ninline bool hasMAGMA() {\n  return globalContext().hasMAGMA();\n}\n\ninline bool hasMKLDNN() {\n  return globalContext().hasMKLDNN();\n}\n\ninline void manual_seed(uint64_t seed) {\n  {\n    auto gen = globalContext().defaultGenerator(c10::DeviceType::CPU);\n    // See Note [Acquire lock when using random generators]\n    std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(gen.mutex());\n    gen.set_current_seed(seed);\n  }\n\n  const auto opt_device_type = at::getAccelerator();\n  if (!opt_device_type.has_value()) {\n    return;\n  }\n  const auto num_gpus = globalContext()\n                            .getAcceleratorHooksInterface(opt_device_type)\n                            .deviceCount();\n  for (const auto i : c10::irange(num_gpus)) {\n    auto gen = globalContext().defaultGenerator(\n        Device(opt_device_type.value(), static_cast<c10::DeviceIndex>(i)));\n    {\n      // See Note [Acquire lock when using random generators]\n      std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(gen.mutex());\n      gen.set_current_seed(seed);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n// When the global flag `allow_tf32` is set to true, cuBLAS handles are\n// automatically configured to use math mode CUBLAS_TF32_TENSOR_OP_MATH.\n// For some operators, such as addmv, TF32 offers no performance improvement\n// but causes precision loss. To help this case, this class implements\n// a RAII guard that can be used to quickly disable TF32 within its scope.\n//\n// Usage:\n//     NoTF32Guard disable_tf32;\nstruct TORCH_API NoTF32Guard {\n  NoTF32Guard();\n  NoTF32Guard(NoTF32Guard&& other) = delete;\n  NoTF32Guard(const NoTF32Guard&) = delete;\n  NoTF32Guard& operator=(const NoTF32Guard&) = delete;\n  NoTF32Guard& operator=(NoTF32Guard&&) = delete;\n  ~NoTF32Guard();\n  static bool should_disable_tf32();\n\n private:\n  bool changed = false;\n};\n\nstruct TORCH_API ROCmBackwardPassGuard {\n  ROCmBackwardPassGuard();\n  ROCmBackwardPassGuard(ROCmBackwardPassGuard&& other) = delete;\n  ROCmBackwardPassGuard(const ROCmBackwardPassGuard&) = delete;\n  ROCmBackwardPassGuard& operator=(const ROCmBackwardPassGuard&) = delete;\n  ROCmBackwardPassGuard& operator=(ROCmBackwardPassGuard&&) = delete;\n  ~ROCmBackwardPassGuard();\n  static bool is_backward_pass();\n};\n\n} // namespace at\n",
  "path": "/workspace/aten/src/ATen/Context.h",
  "security_risk": "LOW"
}

\"annotations\": null, \"meta\": null}], \"structuredContent\": {\"content\": \"200: #else\\n201:     return false;\\n202: #endif\\n203: }\\n204: \\n205: bool CUDAHooks::hasROCM() const {\\n206:   // Currently, this is same as `compiledWithMIOpen`.\\n207:   // But in future if there are ROCm builds without MIOpen,\\n208:   // then `hasROCM` should return true while `compiledWithMIOpen`\\n209:   // should return false\\n210:   return AT_ROCM_ENABLED();\\n211: }\\n212: \\n213: #if defined(USE_DIRECT_NVRTC)\\n214: static std::pair<std::unique_ptr<at::DynamicLibrary>, at::cuda::NVRTC*> load_nvrtc() {\\n219:   return std::make_pair(nullptr, &at::cuda::detail::lazyNVRTC);\\n220: }\\n221: #else\\n222: static std::pair<std::unique_ptr<at::DynamicLibrary>, at::cuda::NVRTC*> load_nvrtc() {\\n223: #if defined(_WIN32)\\n224:   std::string libcaffe2_nvrtc = \\\"caffe2_nvrtc.dll\\\";\\n225: #elif defined(__APPLE__)\\n226:   std::string libcaffe2_nvrtc = \\\"libcaffe2_nvrtc.dylib\\\";\\n227: #else\\n228:   std::string libcaffe2_nvrtc = \\\"libcaffe2_nvrtc.so\\\";\\n229: #endif\\n230:   std::unique_ptr<at::DynamicLibrary> libnvrtc_stub(\\n231:       new at::DynamicLibrary(libcaffe2_nvrtc.c_str()));\\n232:   auto fn = (at::cuda::NVRTC * (*)()) libnvrtc_stub->sym(\\\"load_nvrtc\\\");\\n233:   return std::make_pair(std::move(libnvrtc_stub), fn());\\n234: }\\n235: #endif\\n236: \\n237: const at::cuda::NVRTC& nvrtc() {\\n238:   // must hold onto DynamicLibrary otherwise it will unload\\n239:   static auto handle = load_nvrtc();\\n240:   return *handle.second;\\n241: }\\n242: \\n243: const at::cuda::NVRTC& CUDAHooks::nvrtc() const {\\n244:   return at::cuda::detail::nvrtc();\\n245: }\\n246: \\n247: DeviceIndex current_device() {\\n248:   c10::DeviceIndex device = 0;\\n249:   cudaError_t err = c10::cuda::GetDevice(&device);\\n250:   if (err == cudaSuccess) {\\n251:     return device;\\n252:   }\\n253:   return -1;\\n254:
