{
  "command": "cat > /workspace/aten/src/ATen/native/transformers/cuda/sdp_utils.cpp << 'SDPEOF'\n#define TORCH_ASSERT_ONLY_METHOD_OPERATORS\n#include <ATen/Context.h>\n#include <ATen/NestedTensorImpl.h>\n#include <ATen/TensorSubclassLikeUtils.h>\n#include <ATen/TensorUtils.h>\n#include <ATen/core/Tensor.h>\n#include <ATen/core/grad_mode.h>\n#include <ATen/cuda/CUDAContext.h>\n#include <ATen/cuda/CUDAConfig.h>\n#include <ATen/detail/CUDAHooksInterface.h>\n#include <ATen/native/DispatchStub.h>\n#include <ATen/native/transformers/cuda/sdp_utils.h>\n#include <ATen/native/transformers/sdp_utils_cpp.h>\n#include <c10/core/ScalarType.h>\n#include <c10/util/env.h>\n#include <c10/util/irange.h>\n#include <c10/util/Array.h>\n#include <c10/util/Exception.h>\n#include <c10/util/string_view.h>\n\n#if AT_CUDNN_ENABLED()\n#include <ATen/cudnn/cudnn-wrapper.h>\n#endif\n\n#include <c10/core/SymInt.h>\n\n#if USE_ROCM\n#if defined(USE_FLASH_ATTENTION) || defined(USE_MEM_EFF_ATTENTION)\n#include <ATen/native/transformers/hip/aotriton_versions.h>\n#include <aotriton/flash.h>\n#define USE_ROCM_ATTENTION 1\n#endif\n#else\n#define USE_ROCM_ATTENTION 0\n#endif\n\n// Avoid potential compiler -Wall -Werror complains undefined macro\n#ifndef AOTRITON_VERSION_MINOR\n#define AOTRITON_VERSION_MINOR 0\n#endif\n\n/**\n* Note [SDPA Runtime Dispatch]\n* SDPA relies on a runtime dispatch mechanism to select the appropriate\n* kernel. This file contains exposes this through the `select_sdp_backend`\n* The basic structure of this function is to call `priority_order` to get a\n* list of backends to try, and then iterate through them until one succeeds.\n* Each backend defines a use_<backend> function that returns true if the\n* backend can be run with the given SDP parameters. The use_<backend> function\n* will iterate over a list of \"filters\" that check for specific properties of\n* the SDP parameters. If all filters pass, the backend can be used and use_<backend>\n* returns true. If any filter fails, then use_<backend> returns false.\n*\n* In order to aid in debugging, each filter takes sdp_params and a debug flag.\n* If the debug flag is set, the filter will print a warning message if it fails.\n* The behavior of select_sdp_backend is to return the first backend that\n* succeeds. If no backend is viable then it will run each use_<backend> function\n* with debug=true and return SDPBackend::error.\n*/\n\nnamespace sdp {\nnamespace {\n\n// tracks whether we've set the default priority order once, to avoid setting\n// it redundantly or overwriting a user-specified priority order\n// when the priority order context manager is used before the default priority\n// order is initialized the following happens:\n// (1) the current priority order is queried\n// (2) priority_order() is called, which initializes it to the default as init_ is false\n// (3) the user-specified priority order is set\n// (3.1) we are in the priority context...\n// (3.2) we exit the priority context...\n// (4) the previous priority order (default) is restored\nbool priority_order_init_ = false;\n\n// TODO(eqy): more benchmarking to determine whether this should include sm86/89\n// Needs to be kept in-sync with test_fused_chocie in test_transformers.py\nbool check_prefer_cudnn_attention() {\n  static const bool prefer_cudnn = c10::utils::check_env(\"TORCH_CUDNN_SDPA_PREFERRED\") != false;\n  if (!prefer_cudnn) {\n    return false;\n  }\n#if (defined(CUDNN_VERSION) && (CUDNN_VERSION >= 90900))\n  auto dprops = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n  auto major = dprops->major;\n  return (major == 9 || major == 10) && !dprops->minor;\n#else\n  return false;\n#endif\n}\n\n// flash_attention V2 is universally faster than efficient_attention and Math\nstd::array<SDPBackend, num_backends> priority_order(sdp_params const& params) {\n  if (!priority_order_init_) {\n    priority_order_init_ = true;\n    if (check_prefer_cudnn_attention()) {\n        const std::vector<int64_t> cudnn_order = {static_cast<int64_t>(at::SDPBackend::cudnn_attention),\n                                                  static_cast<int64_t>(at::SDPBackend::flash_attention),\n                                                  static_cast<int64_t>(at::SDPBackend::efficient_attention),\n                                                  static_cast<int64_t>(at::SDPBackend::math)};\n        at::globalContext().setSDPPriorityOrder(cudnn_order);\n    }\n  }\n  return at::globalContext().sDPPriorityOrder();\n}\n\nbool use_tensor_cores(sdp_params const& params, cudaDeviceProp* dprops, bool is_half) {\n  if (dprops->major >= 8) {\n    return true;\n  }\n  if (dprops->major >= 7) {\n    return is_half;\n  }\n  return false;\n}\nint64_t minimum_gemm_alignment(sdp_params const& params) {\n  auto dprops = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n  bool is_half = (params.query.dtype() == at::kHalf) ||\n      (params.query.dtype() == at::kBFloat16);\n  bool use_tc = use_tensor_cores(params, dprops, is_half);\n  int64_t matmul_alignment_mn = 1;\n  if (dprops->major >= 8) {\n    matmul_alignment_mn = 4;\n  }\n  int64_t bits_per_scalar = is_half ? 16 : 32;\n  if (use_tc) {\n    matmul_alignment_mn = std::max(matmul_alignment_mn, 128 / bits_per_scalar);\n  }\n  return matmul_alignment_mn;\n}\n\n// On ROCM, ME and FA share the backend, and hence they share the checking\n// function for fundamental limitations by the GPU kernel\n// caller_is_meff is added to make the TORCH_WARN message showing the correct result\ntemplate<bool caller_is_meff = false>\nbool check_head_dim_size_flash(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n#if USE_ROCM_ATTENTION\n  // AOTriton 0.9+ supports head_dim up to 512\n  const static auto max_hdim = []() {\n#if AOTRITON_VERSION_CURRENT == AOTRITON_VERSION_INT(0, 11)\n    // gfx11xx only support hdim <= 256 on AOTriton 0.11\n    auto dprops = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n    const c10::basic_string_view<char> arch(dprops->gcnArchName);\n    if (arch.starts_with(\"gfx11\")) {\n      return 256;\n    }\n#endif // AOTriton 0.11\n#if AOTRITON_VERSION_CURRENT >= AOTRITON_VERSION_INT(0, 9)\n    return 512;\n#else\n    return 256;\n#endif\n  }();\n  const auto max_size = c10::SymInt(max_hdim);\n#else\n  // All head_dim sizes must be equal and less than 256\n  const auto max_size = c10::SymInt(256);\n#endif\n  const auto query_size_last = params.query.sym_size(-1);\n  const auto key_size_last = params.key.sym_size(-1);\n  const auto value_size_last = params.value.sym_size(-1);\n  bool same_head_dim_size =\n      query_size_last == key_size_last && query_size_last == value_size_last;\n  if (!(same_head_dim_size && (query_size_last <= max_size))) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\n          caller_is_meff ? \"Efficient attention on ROCM\" : \"Flash attention\",\n          \" requires q,k,v to have the same last dimension and to be less than or equal to 256.\",\n          \" Got Query.size(-1): \",\n          query_size_last,\n          \", Key.size(-1): \",\n          key_size_last,\n          \", Value.size(-1): \",\n          value_size_last,\n          \" instead.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\n// See check_head_dim_size_flash above for the purpose of caller_is_meff\ntemplate<bool caller_is_meff = false>\nbool check_head_dim_size_flash_nested(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  const auto max_size = c10::SymInt(256);\n  const auto query_size_last = params.query.sym_size(-1);\n  const auto key_size_last = params.key.sym_size(-1);\n  const auto value_size_last = params.value.sym_size(-1);\n  bool same_head_dim_size =\n      query_size_last == key_size_last && query_size_last == value_size_last;\n  if (!(same_head_dim_size && (query_size_last % 8 == 0) &&\n        (query_size_last <= max_size))) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\n          \"For NestedTensor inputs,\",\n          caller_is_meff ? \" Efficient attention on ROCM \" : \" Flash attention\",\n          \" requires q,k,v to have the same last dimension and to be a multiple of 8 and less than or equal to 256.\",\n          \" Got Query.size(-1): \",\n          query_size_last,\n          \", Key.size(-1): \",\n          key_size_last,\n          \", Value.size(-1): \",\n          value_size_last,\n          \" instead.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool check_head_dim_size_mem_efficient(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  const auto query_size_last = params.query.sym_size(-1);\n  const auto value_size_last = params.value.sym_size(-1);\n  const int64_t alignment = minimum_gemm_alignment(params);\n  if (!(query_size_last == params.key.sym_size(-1) &&\n        query_size_last % alignment == 0 && query_size_last > 0 &&\n        value_size_last % alignment == 0 && value_size_last > 0)) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\n          \"Mem efficient attention requires last dimension of inputs to be divisible by \",\n          alignment,\n          \". \",\n          \"Got Query.size(-1): \",\n          query_size_last,\n          \", Key.size(-1): \",\n          params.key.sym_size(-1),\n          \", Value.size(-1): \",\n          params.value.sym_size(-1),\n          \" instead.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\ntemplate <int Major, int Minor>\nstruct SMVersion {\n  static constexpr int major = Major;\n  static constexpr int minor = Minor;\n  constexpr SMVersion() = default;\n};\n\n/**\n * Checks if the current CUDA device architecture is inclusively within the specified range.\n *\n * @param lower_bound The lower bound of the CUDA device architecture range.\n * @param upper_bound The upper bound of the CUDA device architecture range.\n * @param params The parameters for the current operation.\n * @return True if the current CUDA device architecture is within the specified range, false otherwise.\n */\ntemplate <typename lower_bound, typename upper_bound>\nbool check_sm_version(cudaDeviceProp * dprops) {\n  bool is_gte_lower_bound = dprops->major > lower_bound::major ||\n      (dprops->major == lower_bound::major &&\n       dprops->minor >= lower_bound::minor);\n  bool is_lte_upper_bound = dprops->major < upper_bound::major ||\n      (dprops->major == upper_bound::major &&\n       dprops->minor <= upper_bound::minor);\n  return is_gte_lower_bound && is_lte_upper_bound;\n}\n\nbool check_flash_attention_hardware_support(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  // Check that the gpu is capable of running flash attention\n  using sm80 = SMVersion<8, 0>;\n  using sm121 = SMVersion<12, 1>;\n#if USE_ROCM\n#if USE_ROCM_ATTENTION\n  if(at::globalContext().getROCmFAPreferredBackend() == at::ROCmFABackend::Ck) {\n    // User explicitly set CK as the flash attention backend. Return true for now\n    // TODO: Flesh out sanity checks\n    return true;\n  } else {\n    auto stream = at::cuda::getCurrentCUDAStream().stream();\n    if (hipSuccess != aotriton::v2::flash::check_gpu(stream)) {\n        auto dprops = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n        if (debug) {\n            TORCH_WARN(\n                    \"Flash attention was not compiled for current AMD GPU architecture. Attempting to run on architecture \", dprops->gcnArchName);\n        }\n        return false;\n    }\n#if AOTRITON_VERSION_MINOR >= 9\n    if (aotriton::isArchExperimentallySupported(stream)) {\n      static const bool enable_experimental = c10::utils::check_env(\"TORCH_ROCM_AOTRITON_ENABLE_EXPERIMENTAL\") == true;\n      if (!enable_experimental) {\n        TORCH_WARN_ONCE(\"Flash Efficient attention on Current AMD GPU is still experimental.\"\n            \" Enable it with TORCH_ROCM_AOTRITON_ENABLE_EXPERIMENTAL=1.\");\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n#endif\n  }\n#else\n  return false;\n#endif\n#else\n  auto dprops = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n  if (!check_sm_version<sm80, sm121>(dprops)) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\n          \"Flash attention only supports gpu architectures in the range [sm80, sm121]. Attempting to run on a sm \",\n          dprops->major,\n          \".\",\n          dprops->minor,\n          \" gpu.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n#endif\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool check_mem_efficient_hardware_support(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  // Mem Efficient attention supports hardware in the range [sm_50, sm_90]\n  using sm50 = SMVersion<5, 0>;\n  using sm121 = SMVersion<12, 1>;\n#if USE_ROCM\n#if USE_ROCM_ATTENTION\n  if(at::globalContext().getROCmFAPreferredBackend() == at::ROCmFABackend::Ck) {\n    // User explicitly set CK as the flash attention backend. Return true for now\n    // TODO: Flesh out sanity checks\n    return true;\n  } else {\n    auto stream = at::cuda::getCurrentCUDAStream().stream();\n    if (hipSuccess != aotriton::v2::flash::check_gpu(stream)) {\n        auto dprops = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n        if (debug) {\n            TORCH_WARN(\n                    \"Mem Efficient attention was not compiled for current AMD GPU architecture. Attempting to run on architecture \", dprops->gcnArchName);\n        }\n        return false;\n    }\n#if AOTRITON_VERSION_MINOR >= 9\n    if (aotriton::isArchExperimentallySupported(stream)) {\n      static const bool enable_experimental = c10::utils::check_env(\"TORCH_ROCM_AOTRITON_ENABLE_EXPERIMENTAL\") == true;\n      if (!enable_experimental) {\n        TORCH_WARN_ONCE(\"Mem Efficient attention on Current AMD GPU is still experimental.\"\n            \" Enable it with TORCH_ROCM_AOTRITON_ENABLE_EXPERIMENTAL=1.\");\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n#endif\n  }\n#else\n  return false;\n#endif\n#else\n  auto dprops = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n  if (!check_sm_version<sm50, sm121>(dprops)) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\n          \"Mem Efficient Attention only supports gpu architectures in the range [sm50, sm121]. Attempting to run on a sm \",\n          dprops->major,\n          \".\",\n          dprops->minor,\n          \" gpu.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n#endif\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool check_requires_grad_and_head_dim_gt192_constraints_on_sm86_89_or_120(\n    sdp_params const& params,\n    bool debug) {\n  // Flash Attention will raise an error in the backward pass if the head_dim\n  // size is greater than 192 And the device is between in the range [sm86, sm89]\n  using sm86 = SMVersion<8, 6>;\n  using sm89 = SMVersion<8, 9>;\n  using sm120 = SMVersion<12, 0>;\n  using sm121 = SMVersion<12, 1>;\n  auto dprops = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n  bool is_sm86_or_sm89 = check_sm_version<sm86, sm89>(dprops);\n  bool is_sm120_or_sm121 = check_sm_version<sm120, sm121>(dprops);\n  bool is_head_dim_gt192 = params.query.sym_size(-1) > 192;\n  bool is_head_dim_lte224 = params.query.sym_size(-1) <= 224;\n  bool is_dropout = params.dropout > 0.0;\n  //  head_dim size  in (192, 224] is not supported on sm86 and sm89\n  bool cond1 = is_head_dim_gt192 && is_head_dim_lte224;\n  // head_dim size > 224 and is_dropout is not supported on sm86 and sm89\n  bool cond2 = params.query.sym_size(-1) > 224 && is_dropout;\n  if (input_requires_grad(params) && (is_sm86_or_sm89 || is_sm120_or_sm121) && (cond1 || cond2)) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\n          \"Flash attention currently doesn't support training with head_dim \u2208 (192, 224] or \"\n          \"(head_dim \u2208 (224, 256] and dropout > 0.0) on gpu architectures in the range[sm86, sm89].\",\n          \"Attempting to run with dropout set to: \", params.dropout,\n          \"and head_dim: \",\n          params.query.sym_size(-1), \" on a sm \", dprops->major, \".\",\n          dprops->minor, \" gpu.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool check_flash_causal_non_square_seqlens(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  // FlashAttention 2 updated the default mask meaning for causal in this PR:\n  // 9e5e8bc91e it is now aligned to lower_right which would be a BC break\n  // for non-square masks. We will not support non-square masks for causal w/ FAV2\n  if (params.is_causal &&\n      !params.query.is_nested() && !params.key.is_nested() &&\n      params.query.sym_size(-2) != params.key.sym_size(-2)) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\n          \"Flash attention does not support the is_causal flag when seqlen_q != seqlen_k. \",\n          \"Got seqlen_q: \", params.query.sym_size(-2), \" seqlen_k: \",\n          params.key.sym_size(-2), \". If you would like to use causal attention with non-square masks, please see CausalAttnMask.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool check_all_tensors_on_device(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  // Check that all tensors are on the GPU device\n  // This should be handled by the stub dispatch, but we call can_use_*_attention\n  // directly from python we need to ensure that the tensors are on cuda\n  if (params.query.device().type() != at::DeviceType::CUDA) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\n          \"All tensors need to be on cuda device. Got query on device: \",\n          params.query.device(),\n          \", key on device: \",\n          params.key.device(),\n          \", value on device: \",\n          params.value.device());\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool check_cudnn_tensor_shapes(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  const auto s_q = params.query.sym_size(2);\n  const auto s_k = params.key.sym_size(2);\n  const auto d_qk = params.query.sym_size(3);\n  const auto d_v = params.value.sym_size(3);\n  long cudnn_version = at::detail::getCUDAHooks().versionRuntimeCuDNN();\n  if (cudnn_version < 8903) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\"SDPA fprop requires cudnn 8.9.3 or higher\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  if (cudnn_version < 8906 && params.dropout != 0.0) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\"Dropout reference is only supported on 8.9.6 onwards.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  auto head_dim_limit = 128;\n  if (cudnn_version >= 91000) {\n    auto dprops = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n    if (dprops->major == 9 && !dprops->minor) {\n      head_dim_limit = 256;\n    }\n  }\n  if (d_qk > head_dim_limit || d_v > head_dim_limit) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\"head_dim should be no more than \", head_dim_limit);\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  if (d_qk % 8 != 0 || d_v % 8 != 0) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\"head_dim should be a multiple of 8\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  if (cudnn_version < 8906 && s_k % 64 != 0 ) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\"not-multiple-of-64 seq_kv is not supported below 8.9.6\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  if (cudnn_version < 90000) {\n    if (s_q < 64) {\n      if (debug) {\n        TORCH_WARN(\"s_q less than 64 is not supported before cudnn 9.0.0\");\n      }\n      return false;\n    }\n    if (params.dropout != 0.0 && (s_q % 64 != 0 || s_k % 64 != 0)) {\n      if (debug) {\n        TORCH_WARN(\n            \"s_q not a multiple of 64 with padding/dropout is not supported with cudnn version 9.0.0\");\n      }\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n  if (s_k == 1) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN_ONCE(\"cudnn SDPA does not support key/value sequence length 1.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  if (s_q == 1 && params.dropout != 0.0) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN_ONCE(\"cudnn SDPA does not support query sequence length 1 with dropout.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool check_cudnn_layout(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  const int64_t h = params.query.size(1);\n  const int64_t s_q = params.query.size(2);\n  const int64_t d = params.query.size(3);\n  const int64_t s_k = params.key.size(2);\n  const int64_t s_v = params.value.size(2);\n  // corresponds to cuDNN's \"packed QKV\" layout\n  const bool packed_query_layout_ok = (params.query.stride(0) == s_q * 3 * h * d) &&\n                                 (params.query.stride(1) == d) &&\n                                 (params.query.stride(2) == 3 * h * d) &&\n                                 (params.query.stride(3) == 1);\n  const bool packed_key_layout_ok = (params.key.stride(0) == s_k * 3 * h * d) &&\n                               (params.key.stride(1) == d) &&\n                               (params.key.stride(2) == 3 * h * d) &&\n                               (params.key.stride(3) == 1);\n  const bool packed_value_layout_ok = (params.value.stride(0) == s_v * 3 * h * d) &&\n                                 (params.value.stride(1) == d) &&\n                                 (params.value.stride(2) == 3 * h * d) &&\n                                 (params.value.stride(3) == 1);\n\n  const bool packed_layout_ok = packed_query_layout_ok && packed_key_layout_ok && packed_value_layout_ok;\n\n  const bool query_layout_ok = (params.query.stride(0) == s_q * h * d) &&\n                               (params.query.stride(1) == d) &&\n                               (params.query.stride(2) == h * d) &&\n                               (params.query.stride(3) == 1);\n  const bool key_layout_ok = (params.key.stride(0) == s_k * h * d) &&\n                              (params.key.stride(1) == d) &&\n                              (params.key.stride(2) == h * d) &&\n                              (params.key.stride(3) == 1);\n  const bool value_layout_ok = (params.value.stride(0) == s_v * h * d) &&\n                               (params.value.stride(1) == d) &&\n                               (params.value.stride(2) == h * d) &&\n                               (params.value.stride(3) == 1);\n\n  const bool layout_ok = query_layout_ok && key_layout_ok && value_layout_ok;\n\n  if (!packed_value_layout_ok && !layout_ok) {\n    if (debug) {\n      if (!packed_layout_ok) {\n        if (!packed_query_layout_ok) {\n          TORCH_WARN(\"Query tensor was not in cuDNN-supported packed QKV layout\", params.query.strides());\n        }\n        if (!packed_key_layout_ok) {\n          TORCH_WARN(\"Key tensor was not in cuDNN-supported packed QKV layout\", params.key.strides());\n        }\n        if (!packed_value_layout_ok) {\n          TORCH_WARN(\"Value tensor was not in cuDNN-supported packed QKV layout\", params.value.strides());\n        }\n      }\n      if (!layout_ok) {\n        if (!query_layout_ok) {\n          TORCH_WARN(\"Query tensor was not in cuDNN-supported unpacked QKV layout\", params.query.strides());\n        }\n        if (!key_layout_ok) {\n          TORCH_WARN(\"Key tensor was not in cuDNN-supported unpacked QKV layout\", params.key.strides());\n        }\n        if (!value_layout_ok) {\n          TORCH_WARN(\"Value tensor was not in cuDNN-supported unpacked QKV layout\", params.value.strides());\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool check_cudnn_hardware_support(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  using sm80 = SMVersion<8, 0>;\n  using sm121 = SMVersion<12, 1>;\n  auto dprops = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n  if (!check_sm_version<sm80, sm121>(dprops)) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\n          \"cuDNN MHA only supports gpu architectures in the range [sm80, sm121]. Attempting to run on a sm \",\n          dprops->major,\n          \".\",\n          dprops->minor,\n          \" gpu.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool check_for_nested_inputs(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  static const bool enable_cudnn_nested = c10::utils::check_env(\"TORCH_CUDNN_SDPA_NESTED_TENSOR_ENABLED\") == true;\n  if (has_for_nested_inputs(params) && !enable_cudnn_nested) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\"Experimental cuDNN SDPA nested tensor support is not enabled.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  } else if (has_for_nested_inputs(params) && (params.query.requires_grad() || params.key.requires_grad() || params.value.requires_grad())) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\"Experimental cuDNN SDPA nested tensor support does not support backward.\");\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  const auto dprop = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n  // Check that the input is nested\n  if (!(dprop->major == 9 || dprop->major == 10) && has_for_nested_inputs(params)) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\"cuDNN SDPA supports nested tensors on SM 9.0, SM 10.0.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool check_dtypes_low_precision(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  auto dprop = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n  if (dprop->major >= 8) {\n    constexpr auto sm80_dtypes =\n        c10::array_of<at::ScalarType>(at::kHalf, at::kBFloat16);\n    return check_tensor_dtype(params, sm80_dtypes, debug);\n  } else {\n    constexpr auto default_dtypes = c10::array_of<at::ScalarType>(at::kHalf);\n    return check_tensor_dtype(params, default_dtypes, debug);\n  }\n}\n\nbool check_runtime_disabled_cudnn(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  // We check the global context to see if user has explicitly turned of cudnn\n  // sdp kernels\n  if (!at::globalContext().userEnabledCuDNNSDP()) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\"cuDNN attention has been runtime disabled.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool check_cudnn_deterministic(const sdp_params& params, bool debug) {\n  auto& ctx = at::globalContext();\n  if (ctx.deterministicAlgorithms()) {\n    if (!ctx.deterministicAlgorithmsWarnOnly()) {\n      if (debug) {\n        TORCH_WARN(\"cuDNN SDPA is not deterministic.\");\n      }\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\n} // namespace\n\nbool can_use_cudnn_attention(const sdp_params& params, bool debug) {\n#if defined(USE_ROCM) || !AT_CUDNN_ENABLED() || !defined(CUDNN_VERSION)\n  if (debug) {\n    TORCH_WARN(\"Torch was not compiled with cuDNN attention.\");\n  }\n  return false;\n#endif\n#if defined(CUDNN_VERSION) && CUDNN_VERSION < 90000\n  if (debug) {\n    TORCH_WARN(CUDNN_VERSION, \" cuDNN version too old to use cuDNN Attention (< v9.0.0)\");\n  }\n  return false;\n#endif\n#if defined(CUDNN_VERSION)\n  static auto cudnn_version = at::detail::getCUDAHooks().versionRuntimeCuDNN();\n  if (params.dropout > 0.0 && cudnn_version > 91100 && cudnn_version < 91400) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(CUDNN_VERSION, \" cuDNN version does not support droppout in SDPA (9.11 - 9.13).\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n#endif\n  // Define gate functions that determine if a flash kernel can be ran\n  // Replace with std::to_array when we migrate to c++20\n  constexpr auto general_constraints =\n      c10::array_of<bool (*)(sdp_params const&, bool)>(\n          check_runtime_disabled_cudnn,\n          check_for_nested_inputs,\n          check_all_tensors_on_device,\n          check_tensor_shapes,\n          check_cudnn_deterministic,\n          check_dtypes_low_precision,\n          check_attn_mask_shape,\n          check_cudnn_hardware_support\n          );\n  for (auto& constraint : general_constraints) {\n    if (!constraint(params, debug)) {\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n  constexpr auto dense_constraints =\n      c10::array_of<bool (*)(sdp_params const&, bool)>(\n      check_nonzero_sequence_lengths_dense,\n      check_last_dim_stride_equals_1_dense<true /*ignore_singleton_dim=*/>,\n      check_batch_size_and_num_heads_dense<true /*enable_gqa*/, false /*requires_same_num_heads*/>,\n      check_cudnn_tensor_shapes\n  );\n\n  if (has_only_dense_inputs(params)) {\n    for (auto& constraint : dense_constraints) {\n      if (!constraint(params, debug)) {\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool is_flash_attention_available() {\n#ifdef USE_FLASH_ATTENTION\n  return true;\n#else\n  return false;\n#endif\n}\n\nbool can_use_flash_attention(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n#ifndef USE_FLASH_ATTENTION\n  if (debug) {\n    TORCH_WARN(\"Torch was not compiled with flash attention.\");\n  }\n  return false;\n#else // defined(USE_FLASH_ATTENTION)\n  // Define gate functions that determine if a flash kernel can be ran\n  // Replace with std::to_array when we migrate to c++20\n  constexpr auto general_constraints = c10::array_of<bool (*)(sdp_params const&, bool)>(\n      check_runtime_disabled_flash,\n      check_all_tensors_on_device,\n      check_tensor_shapes,\n      check_for_attn_mask,\n      check_head_dim_size_flash<false /*caller_is_meff*/>,\n      check_flash_attention_hardware_support,\n      check_requires_grad_and_head_dim_gt192_constraints_on_sm86_89_or_120,\n      check_flash_causal_non_square_seqlens,\n      check_dtypes_low_precision);\n  for (auto& constraint : general_constraints) {\n    if (!constraint(params, debug)) {\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (has_for_nested_inputs(params)) {\n    constexpr auto nested_constraints = c10::array_of<bool (*)(sdp_params const&, bool)>(\n        check_batch_size_nested,\n        check_head_dim_size_flash_nested<false /*caller_is_meff*/>,\n        check_for_seq_len_0_nested_tensor);\n    for (auto& constraint : nested_constraints) {\n      if (!constraint(params, debug)) {\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  constexpr bool backend_supports_grouped_query_attention = true;\n  if (has_only_dense_inputs(params)) {\n    constexpr auto dense_constraints = c10::array_of<bool (*)(sdp_params const&, bool)>(\n        check_batch_size_and_num_heads_dense<backend_supports_grouped_query_attention>,\n        check_nonzero_sequence_lengths_dense,\n        check_last_dim_stride_equals_1_dense<true /*ignore_singleton_dim=*/>);\n    for (auto& constraint : dense_constraints) {\n      if (!constraint(params, debug)) {\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  return true;\n#endif // defined(USE_FLASH_ATTENTION)\n}\n\nbool can_use_mem_efficient_attention(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n#ifndef USE_MEM_EFF_ATTENTION\n  TORCH_WARN_ONCE(!debug, \"Torch was not compiled with memory efficient attention.\");\n  return false;\n#endif\n  // Constraints specific to mem efficient attention\n  constexpr auto less_than_sm80_mem_efficient_dtypes =\n      c10::array_of<at::ScalarType>(at::kHalf, at::kFloat);\n#ifdef USE_ROCM\n  constexpr auto aotriton_mem_efficient_dtypes =\n      c10::array_of<at::ScalarType>(at::kHalf, at::kFloat, at::kBFloat16);\n  constexpr auto ck_mem_efficient_dtypes =\n      c10::array_of<at::ScalarType>(at::kHalf, at::kBFloat16);\n#else\n  constexpr auto greater_than_or_equal_sm80_mem_efficient_dtypes =\n      c10::array_of<at::ScalarType>(at::kHalf, at::kFloat, at::kBFloat16);\n#endif\n\n  //  Define gate functions that determine if a mem efficient kernel can be ran\n  constexpr auto general_constraints = c10::array_of<bool (*)(sdp_params const&, bool)>(\n      check_runtime_disabled_mem_efficient,\n      check_all_tensors_on_device,\n      check_mem_efficient_hardware_support,\n      check_tensor_shapes,\n#ifdef USE_ROCM\n      check_head_dim_size_flash<true /* caller_is_meff */>\n#else\n      check_head_dim_size_mem_efficient\n#endif\n  );\n  for (auto& constraint : general_constraints) {\n    if (!constraint(params, debug)) {\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (has_for_nested_inputs(params)) {\n    constexpr auto nested_constraints = c10::array_of<bool (*)(sdp_params const&, bool)>(\n#ifndef USE_ROCM  // ME and FA shares backend on ROCM and thus supports training\n        check_requires_grad_and_nested,\n#else // Meanwhile ME on ROCM share the limits of FA about head dimensions\n        check_head_dim_size_flash_nested<true /* caller_is_meff */>,\n#endif\n        check_batch_size_nested,\n        check_for_seq_len_0_nested_tensor);\n    for (auto& constraint : nested_constraints) {\n      if (!constraint(params, debug)) {\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  if (has_only_dense_inputs(params)) {\n    constexpr auto dense_constraints = c10::array_of<bool (*)(sdp_params const&, bool)>(\n        check_nonzero_sequence_lengths_dense,\n        check_last_dim_stride_equals_1_dense<false /*ignore_singleton_dim=*/>,\n        check_batch_size_and_num_heads_dense<false /*supports_grouped_query_attention=*/>);\n    for (auto& constraint : dense_constraints) {\n      if (!constraint(params, debug)) {\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n#ifdef USE_ROCM\n  if (params.attn_mask.has_value()) {\n    const auto q_dtype = params.query.dtype();\n    const auto bias_dtype = params.attn_mask.value().dtype();\n    if (bias_dtype != at::kBool && bias_dtype != q_dtype) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\"Efficient attention on ROCM requires attn_mask be boolean, or has the same datatype as of q,k,v\");\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n  if(at::globalContext().getROCmFAPreferredBackend() == at::ROCmFABackend::Ck) {\n    return check_tensor_dtype(params, ck_mem_efficient_dtypes, debug);\n  }\n  return check_tensor_dtype(params, aotriton_mem_efficient_dtypes, debug);\n#else\n  auto dprop = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n  if (dprop->major >= 8) {\n    return check_tensor_dtype(params, greater_than_or_equal_sm80_mem_efficient_dtypes, debug);\n  }\n#endif\n  return check_tensor_dtype(params, less_than_sm80_mem_efficient_dtypes, debug);\n}\n\nSDPBackend select_sdp_backend(sdp_params const& kernel_params) {\n  // This function defines the priority order of the different sdp backends\n  // 1. Flash Attention\n  // 2. Mem Efficient Attention\n  // 3. Math fallback\n  auto& ctx = at::globalContext();\n  if (!ctx.userEnabledMathSDP() && !ctx.userEnabledFlashSDP() &&\n      !ctx.userEnabledMemEfficientSDP() && !ctx.userEnabledCuDNNSDP()) {\n    return SDPBackend::error;\n  }\n  // Get ideal kernel ordering\n  const auto ordering = priority_order(kernel_params);\n\n  // Because TORCHCHECK checks if condition is true we negate debug so that\n  // The statements will be printed when debug is true\n  bool print_debug = false;\n  for (auto& backend : ordering) {\n    switch (backend) {\n      case SDPBackend::cudnn_attention:\n        if (sdp::can_use_cudnn_attention(kernel_params, print_debug)) {\n              return SDPBackend::cudnn_attention;\n        }\n        break;\n      case SDPBackend::flash_attention:\n        if (sdp::can_use_flash_attention(kernel_params, print_debug)) {\n          return SDPBackend::flash_attention;\n        }\n        break;\n      case SDPBackend::efficient_attention:\n        if (sdp::can_use_mem_efficient_attention(kernel_params, print_debug)) {\n          return SDPBackend::efficient_attention;\n        }\n        break;\n      case SDPBackend::math:\n        if (ctx.userEnabledMathSDP()) {\n          return SDPBackend::math;\n        }\n        break;\n      case SDPBackend::overrideable:\n        if (ctx.userEnabledOverrideableSDP()) {\n          TORCH_CHECK(false, \"Invalid backend\");\n        }\n        break;\n      default:\n        TORCH_CHECK(false, \"Invalid backend\");\n    }\n  }\n  // If we have gotten to this point then two things have happened:\n  // 1. use_flash_attention or use_mem_efficient did not satisfy the\n  // constraints to be ran\n  // 2. The user has explicitly disabled the math kernel\n  // We then re-run the kernel checks with debug enabled to print out the\n  // reason why the kernel was not selected\n\n  print_debug = true;\n  TORCH_WARN(\"Memory efficient kernel not used because:\");\n  sdp::can_use_mem_efficient_attention(kernel_params, print_debug);\n  TORCH_WARN(\"Flash attention kernel not used because:\");\n  sdp::can_use_flash_attention(kernel_params, print_debug);\n  TORCH_WARN(\"cuDNN attention kernel not used because:\");\n  sdp::can_use_cudnn_attention(kernel_params, print_debug);\n  TORCH_CHECK(!print_debug, \"No available kernel. Aborting execution.\")\n  return SDPBackend::error;\n}\n\nbool check_for_seq_len_1_nested_tensor(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  // When this function is called we are assured that the nt is dim==4\n  if (!params.query.is_nested()) {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  const auto nt_q_tensor_impl =\n      at::native::get_nested_tensor_impl(params.query);\n  const at::Tensor& sizes = nt_q_tensor_impl->get_nested_sizes();\n  auto* sizes_ptr = sizes.data_ptr<int64_t>();\n  const int64_t n_tensors = params.query.size(0);\n  const int64_t size_tensor_stride = sizes.stride(0);\n\n  // This is being called inside sdp with shape [batch, heads, {seq_len}, dim]\n  for (const auto i : c10::irange(n_tensors)) {\n    if (sizes_ptr[(i * size_tensor_stride) + 1] <= 1) {\n      if (debug) {\n        TORCH_WARN(\n            \"Packed projection for fused kernels does not support sequence_length <= 1\");\n      }\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return true;\n}\n\n} // namespace sdp\nSDPEOF\necho \"Created sdp_utils.cpp\"",
  "security_risk": "LOW",
  "timeout": 30
}

dilation.insert(dilation.begin(), 1);\\n355:       output_padding.insert(output_padding.begin(), 0);\\n356:     }\\n357:   }\\n358: \\n359:   bool use_cpu_depthwise3x3_winograd(const at::Tensor& input, const at::Tensor& weight, const std::optional<at::Tensor>& bias) const {\\n360: #if defined(__ARM_NEON__) || (defined(__riscv_v_intrinsic) && __riscv_v_intrinsic>=12000)\\n361:     // Currently only 3x3 depthwise convolutions on tensors of float are supported.\\n362:     return (input.ndimension() == 4) &&\\n363:            (at::symint::size<T>(input, 1) == groups) &&\\n364:            (weight.ndimension() == 4 ) &&\\n365:            (at::symint::size<T>(weight, 0) % at::symint::size<T>(input, 1) == 0) &&\\n366:            (at::symint::size<T>(weight, 1) == 1) &&\\n367: false;\\n381: #endif\\n382:   }\\n383: \\n384:   bool needs_64bit_indexing_no_split(const at::Tensor& input, const at::Tensor& weight) const {\\n385:     constexpr int64_t int_max = std::numeric_limits<int>::max();\\n386:     auto numel_input = at::symint::numel<T>(input);\\n387:     // empty input\\n388:     if (numel_input == 0) {\\n389:       return false;\\n390:     }\\n391: mkldnn_bf16_device_check()) ||\\n521:          (input.scalar_type() == at::kHalf && mkldnn_fp16_device_check()))) {\\n522:       return true;\\n523:     }\\n524:     return (input.is_mkldnn()) || // input is mkldnn Tensor\\n525:       (input.device().is_cpu() &&\\n526:        input.scalar_type() == kFloat && // only on CPU Float Tensors\\n527:        // For 1x1 filters, MKLDNN is faster than THNN when multi-threaded,\\n528:        // but THNN is faster when single-threaded.\\n529:        (is_strided() || is_dilated() || at::symint::size<T>(input, 0) >= 16 ||\\n530:         at::symint::size<T>(weight, -1) != 1 || at::symint::size<T>(weight, -2) != 1 || at::get_num_threads() > 1) &&\\n531:        (groups > 1\\n532:         || (at::symint::size<T>(weight, -1) > 3 && at::symint::size<T>(weight, -2) > 3)\\n533:         || at::symint::size<T>(input, 0) > 1\\n534:         || at::symint::size<T>(input, 0)*at::symint::size<T>(input, 1)*at::symint::size<T>(input, 2)*at::symint::size<T>(input, 3) > 20480) // for some case, native is faster\\n535:         );\\n536: \\n537: #endif\\n538:     return false;\\n539:   }\\n540:   bool use_nnpack(const at::Tensor& input, const at::Tensor& weight) const  {\\n541: #if AT_NNPACK_ENABLED()\\n542:     return at::globalContext().userEnabledNNPACK() &&\\n543:            at::_nnpack_available() &&\\n544:            input.device().is_cpu() &&\\n545:            input.scalar_type() == kFloat && // only on CPU Float Tensors\\n546:            !is_dilated() && // or dilation\\n547:            !transposed &&   // or transposed tensors\\n548:            input.ndimension() == 4 && // must be in NCHW format\\n549:            weight.ndimension() == 4 &&\\n550:            (at::symint::size<T>(weight, 2) < 17) && (at::symint::size<T>(weight, 3) < 17) && // NNPACK only supports kernels up to 16x16\\n551:            (padding[0] < at::symint::size<T>(weight, 2)) && (padding[1] < at::symint::size<T>(weight, 3)) // NNPACK only supports padding < kernel_size. See https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/90142.\\n552: #if !defined(C10_MOBILE)\\n553:            && at::symint::size<T>(input, 0) >= 16 // ensure large enough batch size to ensure perf, tuneable\\n554: #endif\\n555:        ;\\n556: #endif\\n557:     return false;\\n558:   }\\n559:   bool use_xnnpack(const at::Tensor& input, const at::Tensor& weight,\\n560:                    const at::OptionalArrayRef<T> bias_sizes_opt) const {\\n561: #if defined(C10_MOBILE)\\n562:     if (!transposed) {\\n563:       // NB: for the call here, it MATTERS that we are templated. If you\\n564:       // untemplate this to always use SymInt, the function\\n565:       // xnnpack_use_convolution2d will always return false\\n566:       return (at::symint::size<T>(input, 1) == groups) &&\\n567:               xnnpack_use_convolution2d(\\n568:                   input,\\n569:                   weight,\\n570:                   bias_sizes_opt,\\n571:                   padding,\\n572:                   stride,\\n573:                   dilation,\\n574:                   groups,\\n575:                   transposed);\\n576:     }\\n577: #endif\\n578:     return false;\\n579:   }\\n580: \\n581:   bool use_mps(const at::Tensor& input, const at::Tensor& weight) const {\\n582:     // These checks need to be expanded. Currently we have very limited set of\\n583:     // checks for MPS.\\n584: #ifdef USE_MPS\\n585:     if (needs_64bit_indexing_no_split(input, weight)) {\\n586:       return false;\\n587:     }\\n588:     if (!input.is_mps()) {\\n589:       return false;\\n590:     }\\n591:     return true;\\n592: #else\\n593:     return false;\\n594: #endif\\n595:   }\\n596: \\n597:   // We currently only have depthwise support for the case where groups ==\\n598:   // nInputPlane and nInputPlane == nOutputPlane (the latter due to the lack of\\n599:   // a depthwise multiplier)\\n600:   bool is_depthwise(const at::Tensor& input, const at::Tensor& weight) const  {\\n601:     return input.is_cuda() &&\\n602:            !transposed &&\\n603:            (input.ndimension() == 4 || input.ndimension() == 5) &&\\n604:            at::symint::size<T>(input, 1) == groups &&\\n605:            groups > 1 && // no point if there is only a single group\\n606:            at::symint::size<T>(weight, 0) % at::symint::size<T>(input, 1) == 0; // output channels must be a multiple of input channels\\n607:   }\\n608: };\\n609: \\n610: DEFINE_DISPATCH(conv_depthwise2d_backward_stub);\\n611: DEFINE_DISPATCH(conv_depthwise3d_backward_stub);\\n612: DEFINE_DISPATCH(cudnn_convolution_backward_stub);\\n613: DEFINE_DISPATCH(cudnn_convolution_transpose_backward_stub);\\n614: DEFINE_DISPATCH(slow_conv_transpose3d_backward_stub);\\n615: DEFINE_DISPATCH(convolution_depthwise3x3_winograd_stub);\\n616: DEFINE_DISPATCH(miopen_convolution_backward_stub);\\n617: DEFINE_DISPATCH(miopen_convolution_transpose_backward_stub);\\n618: DEFINE_DISPATCH(miopen_depthwise_convolution_backward_stub);\\n619: DEFINE_DISPATCH(mkldnn_convolution_backward_stub);\\n620: DEFINE_DISPATCH(mkldnn_convolution_transpose_stub);\\n621: \", \"url\": \"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--5811a8d7@5811a8d7da873dd699ff6687092c225caffcf1bb/-/blob/aten/src/ATen/native/Convolution.cpp%3FL420-620\"}, \"isError\": false}"

subtensor(at::Tensor\\\\u0026 tensor, int64_t dim, int64_t groups, int64_t g) {\\\\n822:   if (!tensor.defined()) {\\\\n823:     return at::Tensor();\\\\n824:   }\\\\n825:   const auto memory_format = tensor.suggest_memory_format();\\\\n826:   int64_t n = tensor.sizes()[dim] / groups;\\\\n827:   return tensor.narrow(dim, n * g, n).contiguous(memory_format);\\\\n828: }\\\\n829: \\\\n830: namespace {\\\\n831: \\\\n832: std::pair\\\\u003cTensor, Tensor\\\\u003e complex_to_real(const Tensor\\\\u0026 inp) {\\\\n833:   auto inp_view_as_complex = at::view_as_real(inp);\\\\n834:   auto dim_i = inp_view_as_complex.dim() - 1;\\\\n835:   auto i_r = inp_view_as_complex.select(dim_i, 0);\\\\n836:   auto i_i = inp_view_as_complex.select(dim_i, 1);\\\\n837:   return std::make_pair(i_r, i_i);\\\\n838: }\\\\n839: \\\\n840: at::Tensor complex_convolution(\\\\n841:     const Tensor\\\\u0026 input,\\\\n842:     const Tensor\\\\u0026 weight,\\\\n843:     const Tensor\\\\u0026 bias,\\\\n844:     SymIntArrayRef stride,\\\\n845:     SymIntArrayRef padding,\\\\n846:     SymIntArrayRef dilation,\\\\n847:     bool transposed,\\\\n848:     SymIntArrayRef output_padding,\\\\n849: w_i] = complex_to_real(weight.resolve_conj());\\\\n853: \\\\n854:   // [NOTE] Complex Convolution\\\\n855:   // conv(W, x, b) = conv(Wr, xr, br) - conv(Wi, xi, 0) + i(conv(Wi, xr, bi) + conv(Wr, xi, 0))\\\\n856:   // where W, x and b are all complex inputs.\\\\n857:   // With Gauss Trick:\\\\n858:   // a = conv(Wr, xr, br),\\\\n859:   // b = conv(Wi, xi, 0),\\\\n860:   // c = conv(Wr + Wi, xr + {\\n635:   out << \\\"ConvParams {\\\"\\n636:       << \\\"  stride = \\\" << IntArrayRef{params.stride}\\n637:       << \\\"  padding = \\\" << ArrayRef<T>{params.padding}\\n638:       << \\\" T>\\n651: static void check_shape_forward(const at::Tensor& input,\\n652:                                 const c10::ArrayRef<T>& weight_sizes, const at::Tensor& bias,\\n653:                                 const ConvParams<T>& params) {\\n654:   int64_t k = input.ndimension();\\n655:   int64_t weight_dim = weight_sizes.size();\\n656:   auto groups = params.groups;\\n657:   const auto& padding = params.padding;\\n658:   const auto& dilation = params.dilation;\\n659:   bool transposed = params.transposed;\\n660: \\n661:   TORCH_CHECK(!params.is_padding_neg(), \\\"negative padding is not supported\\\");\\n662:   TORCH_CHECK(!params.is_output_padding_neg(), \\\"negative output_padding is not supported\\\");\\n663:   TORCH_CHECK(!params.is_stride_nonpos(), \\\"non-positive stride is not supported\\\");\\n664:   TORCH_CHECK(!params.is_dilation_neg(), \\\"dilation should be greater than zero\\\");\\n665: \\n666:   TORCH_CHECK(weight_dim == k,\\n667:            \\\"Expected \\\", weight_dim, \\\"-dimensional input for \\\", weight_dim,\\n668:            \\\"-dimensional weight \\\", weight_sizes, \\\", but got \\\", k, \\\"-dimensional input of size \\\",\\n669:            at::symint::sizes<T>(input), \\\" instead\\\");\\n670:   TORCH_CHECK(weight_sizes[0] >= groups,\\n671:            \\\"Given groups=\\\", groups, \\\", expected weight to be at least \\\", groups,\\n672:            \\\" at dimension 0, but got weight of size \\\", weight_sizes, \\\" instead\\\");\\n673:   TORCH_CHECK(weight_sizes[0] % groups == 0,\\n674:            \\\"Given groups=\\\", groups, \\\", expected weight to be divisible by \\\",\\n675:            groups, \\\" at dimension 0, but got weight of size [\\\", weight_sizes,\\n676:            \\\"] instead\\\");\\n677: \\n678:   if (!transposed) {\\n679:     std::vector<T> input_shape;\\n680:     std::vector<T> kernel_shape;\\n681:     bool kernel_size_correct = true;\\n682: \\n683:     TORCH_CHECK(at::symint::size<T>(input, 1) == (weight_sizes[1] * groups),\\n684:                 \\\"Given groups=\\\", groups, \\\", weight of size \\\", weight_sizes,\\n685:                 \\\", expected input\\\", at::symint::sizes<T>(input), \\\" to have \\\",\\n686:                 (weight_sizes[1] * groups), \\\" channels, but got \\\", at::symint::size<T>(input, 1),\\n687:                 \\\" channels instead\\\");\\n688: \\n689:     TORCH_CHECK(!bias.defined() || (bias.ndimension() == 1 && at::symint::size<T>(bias, 0) == weight_sizes[0]),\\n690:              \\\"Given weight of size \\\", weight_sizes,\\n691:              \\\", expected bias to be 1-dimensional with \\\", weight_sizes[0], \\\" elements\\\",\\n692:              \\\", but got bias of size \\\", at::symint::sizes<T>(bias), \\\" instead\\\");\\n693: \\n694:     for (const auto i : c10::irange(2, k)) {\\n695:       input_shape.push_back(at::symint::size<T>(input, i) + 2 * padding[i-2]);\\n696:       // log new kernel size considering dilation\\n697:       kernel_shape.push_back(dilation[i-2] * (weight_sizes[i]-1) + 1);\\n698:       if (input_shape.back() < kernel_shape.back()) {\\n699:         kernel_size_correct = false;\\n700:       }\\n701:     }\\n702: \\n703:     TORCH_CHECK(input_shape.size() == kernel_shape.size(), \\\"Inconsistent shape between Input and Kernel\\\");\\n704: \\n705:     if (!kernel_size_correct) {\\n706:       // If kernel size is incorrect\\n707:       std::ostringstream input_ss;\\n708:       std::ostringstream kernel_ss;\\n709:       std::string separator = \\\"\\\";\\n710: \\n711:       for (int i = 0, len = input_shape.size(); i < len; ++i) {\\n712:         input_ss << separator << input_shape[i];\\n713:         kernel_ss << separator << kernel_shape[i];\\n714:         separator = \\\" x \\\";\\n715:       }\\n716: \\n717:       TORCH_CHECK(false, \\\"Calculated padded input size per channel: (\\\", input_ss.str(), \\\"). \\\"\\n718:                \\\"Kernel size: (\\\", kernel_ss.str(), \\\"). Kernel size can't be greater than actual input size\\\");\\n719:     }\\n720:   } else { // transposed\\n721:     TORCH_CHECK(at::symint::size<T>(input, 1) == weight_sizes[0],\\n722:              \\\"Given transposed=\\\", transposed, \\\", weight of size \\\", weight_sizes,\\n723:              \\\", expected input\\\", at::symint::sizes<T>(input), \\\" to have \\\", weight_sizes[0],\\n724:              \\\" channels, but got \\\", at::symint::size<T>(input, 1), \\\" channels instead\\\");\\n725:     TORCH_CHECK(!bias.defined() || (bias.ndimension() == 1 && at::symint::size<T>(bias, 0) == weight_sizes[1] * groups),\\n726:              \\\"Given transposed=\\\", transposed, \\\", weight of size \\\", weight_sizes,\\n727:              \\\", expected bias to be 1-dimensional with \\\", weight_sizes[1] * groups, \\\" elements\\\",\\n728:              \\\", but got bias of size \\\", at::symint::sizes<T>(bias), \\\" instead\\\");\\n729:   }\\n730: }\\n731: \\n732: template <typename T>\\n733: static void check_shape_backward(\\n734:     const at::Tensor& input,\\n735:     const c10::ArrayRef<T>& weight_sizes,\\n736:     const ConvParams<T>& params) {\\n737:   check_shape_forward<T>(input, weight_sizes, /*bias=*/ Tensor(), params);\\n738: }\\n739: \\n740: // Given an input tensor and an expected number of spatial dimensions, checks that the\\n741: // input is a valid shape and returns the batched form of the input.\\n742: //\\n743: // Args:\\n744: //     input (Tensor): Input tensor\\n745: //     num_spatial_dims (int): Number of spatial dimensions expected for the input\\n746: //     func_name (string): Function name to produce a nice error message for invalid input\\n747: //\\n748: // Returns a std::tuple containing:\\n749: //     batched_input (Tensor): Input with a batch dimension\\n750: //     is_batched (bool): Indicates whether the original input was already batched\\n751: static std::tuple<Tensor, bool> batchify(\\n752:     const Tensor& input,\\n753:     const int64_t num_spatial_dims,\\n754:     const std::string& func_name) {\\n755:   // assume NTs are always batched\\n756:   if (input.is_nested()) {\\n757:     return std::make_tuple(input, true);\\n758:   }\\n759:   const auto dim_count_no_batch = num_spatial_dims + 1;\\n760:   const auto dim_count_batch = dim_count_no_batch + 1;\\n761:   const auto is_batched = (input.dim() == dim_count_batch);\\n762:   TORCH_CHECK(input.dim() == dim_count_no_batch || is_batched,\\n763:       \\\"Expected \\\", dim_count_no_batch, \\\"D (unbatched) or \\\", dim_count_batch,\\n764:       \\\"D (batched) input to \\\", func_name, \\\", but got input of size: \\\", input.sizes());\\n765:   return std::make_tuple(is_batched ? input : input.unsqueeze(0), is_batched);\\n766: }\\n767: \\n768: static void check_input_same_type_as_parameters(\\n769:     const Tensor& input,\\n770:     const Tensor& weight,\\n771:     const Tensor& bias) {\\n772:   TORCH_CHECK(input.options().type_equal(weight.options()),\\n773:       \\\"Input type (\\\", input.toString(), \\\") and weight type (\\\", weight.toString(),\\n774:       \\\") should be the same\\\");\\n775:   TORCH_CHECK(!bias.defined() || (input.options().type_equal(bias.options())),\\n776:       \\\"Input type (\\\", input.toString(), \\\") and bias type (\\\", bias.toString(),\\n777:       \\\") should be the same\\\");\\n778: }\\n779: \\n780: static void check_input_same_type_as_parameters(\\n781:     const Tensor& input,\\n782:     const Tensor& weight) {\\n783:   check_input_same_type_as_parameters(input, weight, /*bias=*/ Tensor());\\n784: }\\n785: \\n786: #if AT_MKLDNN_ENABLED()\\n787: static void check_input_same_type_as_parameters(\\n788:     const Tensor& input,\\n789:     const Tensor& weight,\\n790:     const Tensor& bias,\\n791:     const ConvBackend backend) {\\n792:   if (backend == ConvBackend::Mkldnn || backend == ConvBackend::MkldnnTranspose) {\\n793:     TORCH_CHECK(input.options().type_equal(weight.options())\\n794:         || (input.is_mkldnn() && weight.device().is_cpu() && weight.scalar_type() == kFloat),\\n795:         \\\"Input type (\\\", input.toString(), \\\") and weight type (\\\", weight.toString(),\\n796:         \\\") should be the same or input should be a MKLDNN tensor and weight is a dense tensor\\\");\\n797:     TORCH_CHECK(!bias.defined() || (input.options().type_equal(bias.options()))\\n798:         || (input.is_mkldnn() && bias.device().is_cpu() && bias.scalar_type() == kFloat),\\n799:         \\\"Input type (\\\", input.toString(), \\\") and bias type (\\\", bias.toString(),\\n800:         \\\") should be the same or input should be a MKLDNN tensor and bias is a dense tensor\\\");\\n801:   } else {\\n802:     check_input_same_type_as_parameters(input, weight, bias);\\n803:   }\\n804: }\\n805: #endif\\n806: \\n807: static auto view4d(const at::Tensor& tensor) -> at::Tensor {\\n808:   TORCH_CHECK(tensor.ndimension() == 3,\\n809:            \\\"expected 3D tensor, got tensor with \\\", tensor.ndimension(),\\n810:            \\\" dimensions instead\\\");\\n811:   return tensor.unsqueeze(2);\\n812: }\\n813: \\n814: static auto view3d(const at::Tensor& tensor) -> at::Tensor {\\n815:   TORCH_CHECK(tensor.ndimension() == 4,\\n816:            \\\"expected 4D tensor, got tensor with \\\", tensor.ndimension(),\\n817:            \\\" dimensions instead\\\");\\n818:   return tensor.squeeze(2);\\n819: }\\n820: \\n821: static at::Tensor subtensor(at::Tensor& tensor, int64_t dim, int64_t groups, int64_t g) {\\n822:   if (!tensor.defined()) {\\n823:     return at::Tensor();\\n824:   }\\n825:   const auto memory_format = tensor.suggest_memory_format();\\n826:   int64_t n = tensor.sizes()[dim] / groups;\\n827:   return tensor.narrow(dim, n * g, n).contiguous(memory_format);\\n828: }\\n829: \\n830: namespace {\\n831: \\n832: std::pair<Tensor, Tensor> complex_to_real(const Tensor& inp) {\\n833:   auto inp_view_as_complex = at::view_as_real(inp);\\n834:   auto dim_i = inp_view_as_complex.dim() - 1;\\n835:   auto i_r = inp_view_as_complex.select(dim_i, 0);\\n836:   auto i_i = inp_view_as_complex.select(dim_i, 1);\\n837:   return std::make_pair(i_r, i_i);\\n838: }\\n839: \\n840: at::Tensor complex_convolution(\\n841:     const Tensor& input,\\n842:     const Tensor& weight,\\n843:     const Tensor& bias,\\n844:     SymIntArrayRef stride,\\n845:     SymIntArrayRef padding,\\n846:     SymIntArrayRef dilation,\\n847:     bool transposed,\\n848:     SymIntArrayRef output_padding,\\n849:     const c10::SymInt& groups) {\\n850:   check_input_same_type_as_parameters(input, weight, bias);\\n851:   auto [i_r, i_i] = complex_to_real(input.resolve_conj());\\n852:   auto [w_r, w_i] = complex_to_real(weight.resolve_conj());\\n853: \\n854:   // [NOTE] Complex Convolution\\n855:   // conv(W, x, b) = conv(Wr, xr, br) - conv(Wi, xi, 0) + i(conv(Wi, xr, bi) + conv(Wr, xi, 0))\\n856:   // where W, x and b are all complex inputs.\\n857:   // With Gauss Trick:\\n858:   // a = conv(Wr, xr, br),\\n859:   // b = conv(Wi, xi, 0),\\n860:   // c = conv(Wr + Wi, xr + xi, bi + br)\\n861:   // conv(W, x, b) = a - b + i(c - a - b)\\n862:   Tensor a, b, c;\\n863:   if (!bias.defined()) {\\n864:     a = at::convolution_symint(i_r, w_r, bias, stride, padding, dilation, transposed, output_padding, groups);\\n865:     b = at::convolution_symint(i_i, w_i, bias, stride, padding, dilation, transposed, output_padding, groups);\\n866:     c = at::convolution_symint(i_r + i_i, w_r + w_i, bias, stride, padding, dilation, transposed, output_padding, groups);\\n867:   } else {\\n868:     auto [b_r, b_i] = complex_to_real(bias.resolve_conj());\\n869:     a = at::convolution_symint(i_r, w_r, b_r, stride, padding, dilation, transposed, output_padding, groups);\\n870:     b = at::convolution_symint(i_i, output_padding, groups);\\n872:   }\\n873: \\n874:   auto i = c10::Scalar(c10::complex<double>(0, 1));\\n875:   return a - b + i * (c - a - b);\\n876: }\\n877: \\n878: at::Tensor complex_convolution_mode(\\n879:     const at::Tensor& input,\\n880:     const at::Tensor& weight,\\n881:     const std::optional<at::Tensor>& bias_opt,\\n882:     c10::SymIntArrayRef stride,\\n883: dilation, false, {0}, groups);\\\\n931:   }\\\\n932:   return is_batched ? std::move(output) : output.squeeze(0);\\\\n933: }\\\\n934: \\\\n935: at::Tensor conv2d_symint(\\\\n936:     const should be the same\\\\\\\");\\\\n949: \\\\n950:   auto [input, is_batched] = batchify(input_, /*num_spatial_dims=*/ 2, \\\\\\\"conv2d\\\\\\\");\\\\n951:   Tensor output;\\\\n952:   if = at::convolution_symint(input, weight, bias, stride, padding, dilation, false, {{0, 0}}, groups);\\\\n956:   }\\\\n957:   return is_batched ? std::move(output) : output.squeeze(0);\\\\n958: }\\\\n959: \\\\n960: at::Tensor conv3d_symint(\\\\n961:     const Tensor\\\\u0026 input_, const Tensor\\\\u0026 weight, const std::optional\\\\u003cTensor\\\\u003e\\\\u0026 bias_opt,\\\\n962: (\\\\\\\",\\\\n972:     bias.dtype().name(),\\\\n973:     \\\\\\\") should be the same\\\\\\\");\\\\n974: \\\\n975:   auto [input, is_batched] = batchify(input_, /*num_spatial_dims=*/ 3, \\\\\\\"conv3d\\\\\\\");\\\\n976:   Tensor output;\\\\n977:   if (at::isComplexType(input_.scalar_type())) {\\\\n978:     output = complex_convolution(input, weight, bias, stride, padding, dilation, false, {{0, 0, 0}}, groups);\\\\n979:   } else {\\\\n980:     output = at::convolution_symint(input, weight, bias, stride, padding, dilation, false, {{0, 0, 0}}, groups);\\\\n981:   }\\\\n982:   return is_batched ? std::move(output) : output.squeeze(0);\\\\n983: }\\\\n984: \\\\n985: \\\\n986: static Tensor convolution_same(\\\\n987:     const Tensor \\\\u0026input, const Tensor \\\\u0026weight, const Tensor \\\\u0026bias,\\\\n988:     SymIntArrayRef stride, SymIntArrayRef dilation, const c10::SymInt\\\\u0026 groups) {\\\\n989: \\\\n990:   auto k = weight.dim();\\\\n991:   TORCH_CHECK(k \\\\u003e 2, \\\\\\\"weight should have at least three dimensions\\\\\\\");\\\\n992:   TORCH_CHECK(groups \\\\u003e 0, \\\\\\\"non-positive groups is not supported\\\\\\\");\\\\n993:   auto dim = static_cast\\\\u003csize_t\\\\u003e(k - 2);\\\\n994:   auto weight_sizes = weight.sym_sizes();\\\\n995:   auto input_sizes = input.sym_sizes();\\\\n996:   TORCH_CHECK(k == input.dim(),\\\\n997:               \\\\\\\"Expected \\\\\\\", k, \\\\\\\"-dimensional input for \\\\\\\",\\\\n998:               k, \\\\\\\"-dimensional weight\\\\\\\", weight_sizes, \\\\\\\", but got \\\\\\\",\\\\n999:               input.dim(), \\\\\\\"-dimensional input of size \\\\\\\",\\\\n1000:               input.sizes(), \\\\\\\" instead\\\\\\\");\\\\n1001:   TORCH_CHECK(stride.size() == dim || stride.size() == 1U,\\\\n1002:               \\\\\\\"stride cannot broadcast to \\\\\\\", dim, \\\\\\\" dimensions\\\\\\\");\\\\n1003:   TORCH_CHECK(dilation.size() == dim || dilation.size() == 1U,\\\\n1004:               \\\\\\\"dilation cannot broadcast to \\\\\\\", dim, \\\\\\\" dimensions\\\\\\\");\\\\n1005:   for (auto i: c10::irange(stride.size())) {\\\\n1006:     TORCH_CHECK(stride[i] == 1, \\\\\\\"padding='same' is not supported for strided convolutions\\\\\\\");\\\\n1007:   }\\\\n1008: \\\\n1009:   // Calculate the correct padding\\\\n1010:   SymDimVector padding_l, padding_r;\\\\n1011:   bool symmetric_padding = true;\\\\n1012:   for (auto i: c10::irange(dim)) {\\\\n1013:     auto s = stride.size() == 1 ? stride[0] : stride[i];\\\\n1014:     auto d = dilation.size() == 1 ? dilation[0] : dilation[i];\\\\n1015:     auto pad = pooling_same_mode_padding_lr(\\\\n1016:         input_sizes[i + 2], weight_sizes[i + 2], s, d);\\\\n1017:     padding_l.push_back(pad.first);\\\\n1018:     padding_r.push_back(pad.second);\\\\n1019:     if (pad.first != pad.second) {\\\\n1020:       symmetric_padding = false;\\\\n1021:     }\\\\n1022:   }\\\\n1023: \\\\n1024:   if (symmetric_padding) {\\\\n1025:     // All backends handle symmetric padding natively\\\\n1026:     SymDimVector output_padding(static_cast\\\\u003csize_t\\\\u003e(dim));\\\\n1027:     return at::convolution_symint(input, weight, bias, stride, padding_l, dilation,\\\\n1028:                                false, output_padding, groups);\\\\n1029:   }\\\\n1030: \\\\n1031:   TORCH_WARN_ONCE(\\\\\\\"Using padding='same' with even kernel lengths and odd dilation may\\\\\\\"\\\\n1032:                   \\\\\\\" require a zero-padded copy of the input be created\\\\\\\");\\\\n1033:   SmallVector\\\\u003cc10::SymInt, kDimVectorStaticSize * 2\\\\u003e pad_nd(static_cast\\\\u003csize_t\\\\u003e(2 * dim));\\\\n1034:   for (auto i: c10::irange(dim)) {\\\\n1035:     // Apply padding by the difference, leaving only a symmetric padding\\\\n1036:     auto delta_pad = padding_r[i] - padding_l[i];\\\\n1037:     auto pad_idx = 2 * (dim - 1 - i);  // F.pad goes from last dim to first\\\\n1038:     if (delta_pad \\\\u003e 0) {\\\\n1039:       pad_nd[pad_idx + 1] = delta_pad;\\\\n1040:     } else {\\\\n1041:       pad_nd[pad_idx] = delta_pad;\\\\n1042:       padding_l[i] = padding_r[i];\\\\n1043:     }\\\\n1044:   }\\\\n1045:   auto padded_input = at::constant_pad_nd_symint(input, pad_nd, 0);\\\\n1046:   SymDimVector output_padding(static_cast\\\\u003csize_t\\\\u003e(dim));\\\\n1047:   return at::convolution_symint(padded_input, weight, bias, stride, padding_l,\\\\n1048:                                 dilation, false, output_padding, groups);\\\\n1049: }\\\\n1050: \\\\n1051: Tensor _convolution_mode_symint(\\\\n1052:     const Tensor\\\\u0026 input, const Tensor\\\\u0026 weight, const std::optional\\\\u003cTensor\\\\u003e\\\\u0026 bias_opt,\\\\n1053:     SymIntArrayRef stride, std::string_view padding, SymIntArrayRef dilation,\\\\n1054:     c10::SymInt groups) {\\\\n1055:   // See [Note: hacky wrapper removal for optional tensor]\\\\n1056:   c10::MaybeOwned\\\\u003cTensor\\\\u003e bias_maybe_owned = at::borrow_from_optional_tensor(bias_opt);\\\\n1057:   const Tensor\\\\u0026 bias = *bias_maybe_owned;\\\\n1058: \\\\n1059:   if (padding == \\\\\\\"same\\\\\\\") {\\\\n1060:     return at::native::convolution_same(\\\\n1061:         input, weight, bias, stride, dilation, groups);\\\\n1062:   } else if (padding == \\\\\\\"valid\\\\\\\") {\\\\n1063:     return at::convolution_symint(\\\\n1064:         input, weight, bias, stride, {{0}}, dilation, false, {{0}}, groups);\\\\n1065:   }\\\\n1066:   TORCH_CHECK(false, \\\\\\\"Invalid padding string: '\\\\\\\", padding, \\\\\\\"'\\\\\\\");\\\\n1067: }\\\\n1068: \\\\n1069: at::Tensor conv1d_padding_symint(\\\\n1070:     const Tensor\\\\u0026 input_, const Tensor\\\\u0026 weight, const std::optional\\\\u003cTensor\\\\u003e\\\\u0026 bias,\\\\n1071:     c10::SymIntArrayRef stride, std::string_view padding, c10::SymIntArrayRef dilation,\\\\n1072:     c10::SymInt groups) {\\\\n1073:   auto [input, is_batched] = batchify(input_, /*num_spatial_dims=*/ 1, \\\\\\\"conv1d\\\\\\\");\\\\n1074:   Tensor output;\\\\n1075:   if (at::isComplexType(input_.scalar_type())) {\\\\n1076:     output = complex_convolution_mode(input, weight, bias, stride, padding, dilation, groups);\\\\n1077:   } else {\\\\n1078:     output = at::_convolution_mode_symint(input, weight, bias, stride, padding, dilation, groups);\\\\n1079:   }\\\\n1080:   return is_batched ? std::move(output) : output.squeeze(0);\\\\n1081: }\\\\n1082: \\\\n1083: at::Tensor conv2d_padding_symint(\\\\n1084:     const at::_convolution_mode_symint(input, weight, bias, stride, padding, dilation, groups);\\\\n1093:   }\\\\n1094:   return is_batched ? std::move(output) : output.squeeze(0);\\\\n1095: }\\\\n1096: removal for optional tensor]\\n939:   c10::MaybeOwned<Tensor> bias_maybe_owned = at::borrow_from_optional_tensor(bias_opt);\\n940:   const Tensor& bias = *bias_maybe_owned;\\n941: \\n942: at::Tensor conv3d_symint(\\n961:     const Tensor& input_, const Tensor& weight, const std::optional<Tensor>& bias_opt,\\n962:     SymIntArrayRef stride, SymIntArrayRef padding, SymIntArrayRef dilation, c10::SymInt groups) {\\n963:   // See [Note: hacky wrapper removal for optional tensor]\\n964:   c10::MaybeOwned<Tensor> bias_maybe_owned = at::borrow_from_optional_tensor(bias_opt);\\n965: /*num_spatial_dims=*/ 3, \\\"conv3d\\\");\\n976:   Tensor output;\\n977:   if (at::isComplexType(input_.scalar_type())) {\\n978:     output = complex_convolution(input, weight, bias, stride, padding, dilation, false, {{0, 0, 0}}, groups);\\n979:   } else {\\n980:     output = at::convolution_symint(input, weight, bias, stride, padding, dilation, false, {{0, 0, 0}}, groups);\\n981:   }\\n982:   return is_batched ? std::move(output) : output.squeeze(0);\\n983: }\\n984: \\n985: \\n986: static Tensor convolution_same(\\n987:     const Tensor &input, const Tensor &weight, const Tensor &bias,\\n988:     SymIntArrayRef stride, SymIntArrayRef dilation, const c10::SymInt& groups) {\\n989: \\n990:   auto k = weight.dim();\\n991:   TORCH_CHECK(k > 2, \\\"weight should have at least three dimensions\\\");\\n992:   TORCH_CHECK(groups > 0, \\\"non-positive groups is not supported\\\");\\n993:   auto dim = static_cast<size_t>(k - 2);\\n994:   auto weight_sizes = weight.sym_sizes();\\n995:   auto input_sizes = input.sym_sizes();\\n996:   TORCH_CHECK(k == input.dim(),\\n997:               \\\"Expected \\\", k, \\\"-dimensional input for \\\",\\n998:               k, \\\"-dimensional weight\\\", weight_sizes, \\\", but got \\\",\\n999:               input.dim(), \\\"-dimensional input of size \\\",\\n1000:               input.sizes(), \\\" instead\\\");\\n1001:   TORCH_CHECK(stride.size() == dim || stride.size() == 1U,\\n1002:               \\\"stride cannot broadcast to \\\", dim, \\\" dimensions\\\");\\n1003:   TORCH_CHECK(dilation.size() == dim || dilation.size() == 1U,\\n1004:               \\\"dilation cannot broadcast to \\\", dim, \\\" dimensions\\\");\\n1005:   for (auto i: c10::irange(stride.size())) {\\n1006:     TORCH_CHECK(stride[i] == 1, \\\"padding='same' is not supported for strided convolutions\\\");\\n1007:   }\\n1008: \\n1009:   // Calculate the correct padding\\n1010:   SymDimVector padding_l, padding_r;\\n1011:   bool symmetric_padding = true;\\n1012:   for (auto i: c10::irange(dim)) {\\n1013:     auto s = stride.size() == 1 ? stride[0] : stride[i];\\n1014:     auto d = dilation.size() == 1 ? dilation[0] : dilation[i];\\n1015:     auto pad = pooling_same_mode_padding_lr(\\n1016:         input_sizes[i + 2], weight_sizes[i + 2], s, d);\\n1017:     padding_l.push_back(pad.first);\\n1018:     padding_r.push_back(pad.second);\\n1019:     if (pad.first != pad.second) {\\n1020:       symmetric_padding = false;\\n1021:     }\\n1022:   }\\n1023: \\n1024:   if (symmetric_padding) {\\n1025:     // All backends handle symmetric padding natively\\n1026:     SymDimVector output_padding(static_cast<size_t>(dim));\\n1027:     return at::convolution_symint(input, weight, bias, stride, padding_l, dilation,\\n1028:                                false, output_padding, groups);\\n1029:   }\\n1030: \\n1031:   TORCH_WARN_ONCE(\\\"Using padding='same' with even kernel lengths and odd dilation may\\\"\\n1032:                   \\\" require a zero-padded copy of the input be created\\\");\\n1033:   SmallVector<c10::SymInt, kDimVectorStaticSize * 2> pad_nd(static_cast<size_t>(2 * dim));\\n1034:   for (auto i: c10::irange(dim)) {\\n1035:     // Apply padding by the difference, leaving only a symmetric padding\\n1036:     auto delta_pad = padding_r[i] - padding_l[i];\\n1037:     auto pad_idx = 2 * (dim - 1 - i);  // F.pad goes from last dim to first\\n1038:     if (delta_pad > 0) {\\n1039:       pad_nd[pad_idx + 1] = delta_pad;\\n1040:     } else {\\n1041:       pad_nd[pad_idx] = delta_pad;\\n1042:       padding_l[i] = padding_r[i];\\n1043:     }\\n1044:   }\\n1045:   auto padded_input = at::constant_pad_nd_symint(input, pad_nd, 0);\\n1046:   SymDimVector output_padding(static_cast<size_t>(dim));\\n1047:   return at::convolution_symint(padded_input, weight, bias, stride, padding_l,\\n1048:                                 dilation, false, output_padding, groups);\\n1049: }\\n1050: \\n1051: Tensor _convolution_mode_symint(\\n1052:     const Tensor& input, const Tensor& weight, const std::optional<Tensor>& bias_opt,\\n1053:     SymIntArrayRef stride, std::string_view padding, SymIntArrayRef dilation,\\n1054:     c10::SymInt groups) {\\n1055:   // See [Note: hacky wrapper removal for optional tensor]\\n1056:   c10::MaybeOwned<Tensor> bias_maybe_owned = at::borrow_from_optional_tensor(bias_opt);\\n1057:   const Tensor& bias = *bias_maybe_owned;\\n1058: \\n1059:   if (padding == \\\"same\\\") {\\n1060:     return at::native::convolution_same(\\n1061:         input, weight, bias, stride, dilation, groups);\\n1062:   } else if (padding == \\\"valid\\\") {\\n1063:     return at::convolution_symint(\\n1064:         input, weight, bias, stride, {{0}}, dilation, false, {{0}}, groups);\\n1065:   }\\n1066:   TORCH_CHECK(false, \\\"Invalid padding string: '\\\", padding, \\\"'\\\");\\n1067: }\\n1068: \\n1069: at::Tensor conv1d_padding_symint(\\n1070:     const Tensor& input_, const Tensor& weight, const std::optional<Tensor>& bias,\\n1071:     c10::SymIntArrayRef stride, std::string_view padding, c10::SymIntArrayRef dilation,\\n1072:     c10::SymInt groups) {\\n1073:   auto [input, is_batched] = batchify(input_, /*num_spatial_dims=*/ 1, \\\"conv1d\\\");\\n1074:   Tensor output;\\n1075:   if (at::isComplexType(input_.scalar_type())) {\\n1076:     output = complex_convolution_mode(input, weight, bias, stride, padding, dilation, groups);\\n1077:   } else {\\n1078:     output = at::_convolution_mode_symint(input, weight, bias, stride, padding, dilation, groups);\\n1079:   }\\n1080:   return is_batched ? std::move(output) : output.squeeze(0);\\n1081: }\\n1082: \\n1083:
