{
  "command": "create",
  "file_text": "#define TORCH_ASSERT_ONLY_METHOD_OPERATORS\n#include <ATen/Context.h>\n#include <ATen/NestedTensorImpl.h>\n#include <ATen/TensorSubclassLikeUtils.h>\n#include <ATen/TensorUtils.h>\n#include <ATen/core/Tensor.h>\n#include <ATen/core/grad_mode.h>\n#include <ATen/cuda/CUDAContext.h>\n#include <ATen/cuda/CUDAConfig.h>\n#include <ATen/detail/CUDAHooksInterface.h>\n#include <ATen/native/DispatchStub.h>\n#include <ATen/native/transformers/cuda/sdp_utils.h>\n#include <ATen/native/transformers/sdp_utils_cpp.h>\n#include <c10/core/ScalarType.h>\n#include <c10/util/env.h>\n#include <c10/util/irange.h>\n#include <c10/util/Array.h>\n#include <c10/util/Exception.h>\n#include <c10/util/string_view.h>\n\n#if AT_CUDNN_ENABLED()\n#include <ATen/cudnn/cudnn-wrapper.h>\n#endif\n\n#include <c10/core/SymInt.h>\n\n#if USE_ROCM\n#if defined(USE_FLASH_ATTENTION) || defined(USE_MEM_EFF_ATTENTION)\n#include <ATen/native/transformers/hip/aotriton_versions.h>\n#include <aotriton/flash.h>\n#define USE_ROCM_ATTENTION 1\n#endif\n#else\n#define USE_ROCM_ATTENTION 0\n#endif\n\n// Avoid potential compiler -Wall -Werror complains undefined macro\n#ifndef AOTRITON_VERSION_MINOR\n#define AOTRITON_VERSION_MINOR 0\n#endif\n\n/**\n* Note [SDPA Runtime Dispatch]\n* SDPA relies on a runtime dispatch mechanism to select the appropriate\n* kernel. This file contains exposes this through the `select_sdp_backend`\n* The basic structure of this function is to call `priority_order` to get a\n* list of backends to try, and then iterate through them until one succeeds.\n* Each backend defines a use_<backend> function that returns true if the\n* backend can be run with the given SDP parameters. The use_<backend> function\n* will iterate over a list of \"filters\" that check for specific properties of\n* the SDP parameters. If all filters pass, the backend can be used and use_<backend>\n* returns true. If any filter fails, then use_<backend> returns false.\n*\n* In order to aid in debugging, each filter takes sdp_params and a debug flag.\n* If the debug flag is set, the filter will print a warning message if it fails.\n* The behavior of select_sdp_backend is to return the first backend that\n* succeeds. If no backend is viable then it will run each use_<backend> function\n* with debug=true and return SDPBackend::error.\n*/\n\nnamespace sdp {\nnamespace {\n\n// tracks whether we've set the default priority order once, to avoid setting\n// it redundantly or overwriting a user-specified priority order\n// when the priority order context manager is used before the default priority\n// order is initialized the following happens:\n// (1) the current priority order is queried\n// (2) priority_order() is called, which initializes it to the default as init_ is false\n// (3) the user-specified priority order is set\n// (3.1) we are in the priority context...\n// (3.2) we exit the priority context...\n// (4) the previous priority order (default) is restored\nbool priority_order_init_ = false;\n\n// TODO(eqy): more benchmarking to determine whether this should include sm86/89\n// Needs to be kept in-sync with test_fused_chocie in test_transformers.py\nbool check_prefer_cudnn_attention() {\n  static const bool prefer_cudnn = c10::utils::check_env(\"TORCH_CUDNN_SDPA_PREFERRED\") != false;\n  if (!prefer_cudnn) {\n    return false;\n  }\n#if (defined(CUDNN_VERSION) && (CUDNN_VERSION >= 90900))\n  auto dprops = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n  auto major = dprops->major;\n  return (major == 9 || major == 10) && !dprops->minor;\n#else\n  return false;\n#endif\n}\n\n// flash_attention V2 is universally faster than efficient_attention and Math\nstd::array<SDPBackend, num_backends> priority_order(sdp_params const& params) {\n  if (!priority_order_init_) {\n    priority_order_init_ = true;\n    if (check_prefer_cudnn_attention()) {\n        const std::vector<int64_t> cudnn_order = {static_cast<int64_t>(at::SDPBackend::cudnn_attention),\n                                                  static_cast<int64_t>(at::SDPBackend::flash_attention),\n                                                  static_cast<int64_t>(at::SDPBackend::efficient_attention),\n                                                  static_cast<int64_t>(at::SDPBackend::math)};\n        at::globalContext().setSDPPriorityOrder(cudnn_order);\n    }\n  }\n  return at::globalContext().sDPPriorityOrder();\n}\n\nbool use_tensor_cores(sdp_params const& params, cudaDeviceProp* dprops, bool is_half) {\n  if (dprops->major >= 8) {\n    return true;\n  }\n  if (dprops->major >= 7) {\n    return is_half;\n  }\n  return false;\n}\nint64_t minimum_gemm_alignment(sdp_params const& params) {\n  auto dprops = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n  bool is_half = (params.query.dtype() == at::kHalf) ||\n      (params.query.dtype() == at::kBFloat16);\n  bool use_tc = use_tensor_cores(params, dprops, is_half);\n  int64_t matmul_alignment_mn = 1;\n  if (dprops->major >= 8) {\n    matmul_alignment_mn = 4;\n  }\n  int64_t bits_per_scalar = is_half ? 16 : 32;\n  if (use_tc) {\n    matmul_alignment_mn = std::max(matmul_alignment_mn, 128 / bits_per_scalar);\n  }\n  return matmul_alignment_mn;\n}\n\n// On ROCM, ME and FA share the backend, and hence they share the checking\n// function for fundamental limitations by the GPU kernel\n// caller_is_meff is added to make the TORCH_WARN message showing the correct result\ntemplate<bool caller_is_meff = false>\nbool check_head_dim_size_flash(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n#if USE_ROCM_ATTENTION\n  // AOTriton 0.9+ supports head_dim up to 512\n  const static auto max_hdim = []() {\n#if AOTRITON_VERSION_CURRENT == AOTRITON_VERSION_INT(0, 11)\n    // gfx11xx only support hdim <= 256 on AOTriton 0.11\n    auto dprops = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n    const c10::basic_string_view<char> arch(dprops->gcnArchName);\n    if (arch.starts_with(\"gfx11\")) {\n      return 256;\n    }\n#endif // AOTriton 0.11\n#if AOTRITON_VERSION_CURRENT >= AOTRITON_VERSION_INT(0, 9)\n    return 512;\n#else\n    return 256;\n#endif\n  }();\n  const auto max_size = c10::SymInt(max_hdim);\n#else\n  // All head_dim sizes must be equal and less than 256\n  const auto max_size = c10::SymInt(256);\n#endif\n  const auto query_size_last = params.query.sym_size(-1);\n  const auto key_size_last = params.key.sym_size(-1);\n  const auto value_size_last = params.value.sym_size(-1);\n  bool same_head_dim_size =\n      query_size_last == key_size_last && query_size_last == value_size_last;\n  if (!(same_head_dim_size && (query_size_last <= max_size))) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\n          caller_is_meff ? \"Efficient attention on ROCM\" : \"Flash attention\",\n          \" requires q,k,v to have the same last dimension and to be less than or equal to 256.\",\n          \" Got Query.size(-1): \",\n          query_size_last,\n          \", Key.size(-1): \",\n          key_size_last,\n          \", Value.size(-1): \",\n          value_size_last,\n          \" instead.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\n// See check_head_dim_size_flash above for the purpose of caller_is_meff\ntemplate<bool caller_is_meff = false>\nbool check_head_dim_size_flash_nested(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  const auto max_size = c10::SymInt(256);\n  const auto query_size_last = params.query.sym_size(-1);\n  const auto key_size_last = params.key.sym_size(-1);\n  const auto value_size_last = params.value.sym_size(-1);\n  bool same_head_dim_size =\n      query_size_last == key_size_last && query_size_last == value_size_last;\n  if (!(same_head_dim_size && (query_size_last % 8 == 0) &&\n        (query_size_last <= max_size))) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\n          \"For NestedTensor inputs,\",\n          caller_is_meff ? \" Efficient attention on ROCM \" : \" Flash attention\",\n          \" requires q,k,v to have the same last dimension and to be a multiple of 8 and less than or equal to 256.\",\n          \" Got Query.size(-1): \",\n          query_size_last,\n          \", Key.size(-1): \",\n          key_size_last,\n          \", Value.size(-1): \",\n          value_size_last,\n          \" instead.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool check_head_dim_size_mem_efficient(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  const auto query_size_last = params.query.sym_size(-1);\n  const auto value_size_last = params.value.sym_size(-1);\n  const int64_t alignment = minimum_gemm_alignment(params);\n  if (!(query_size_last == params.key.sym_size(-1) &&\n        query_size_last % alignment == 0 && query_size_last > 0 &&\n        value_size_last % alignment == 0 && value_size_last > 0)) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\n          \"Mem efficient attention requires last dimension of inputs to be divisible by \",\n          alignment,\n          \". \",\n          \"Got Query.size(-1): \",\n          query_size_last,\n          \", Key.size(-1): \",\n          params.key.sym_size(-1),\n          \", Value.size(-1): \",\n          params.value.sym_size(-1),\n          \" instead.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\ntemplate <int Major, int Minor>\nstruct SMVersion {\n  static constexpr int major = Major;\n  static constexpr int minor = Minor;\n  constexpr SMVersion() = default;\n};\n\n/**\n * Checks if the current CUDA device architecture is inclusively within the specified range.\n *\n * @param lower_bound The lower bound of the CUDA device architecture range.\n * @param upper_bound The upper bound of the CUDA device architecture range.\n * @param params The parameters for the current operation.\n * @return True if the current CUDA device architecture is within the specified range, false otherwise.\n */\ntemplate <typename lower_bound, typename upper_bound>\nbool check_sm_version(cudaDeviceProp * dprops) {\n  bool is_gte_lower_bound = dprops->major > lower_bound::major ||\n      (dprops->major == lower_bound::major &&\n       dprops->minor >= lower_bound::minor);\n  bool is_lte_upper_bound = dprops->major < upper_bound::major ||\n      (dprops->major == upper_bound::major &&\n       dprops->minor <= upper_bound::minor);\n  return is_gte_lower_bound && is_lte_upper_bound;\n}\n\nbool check_flash_attention_hardware_support(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  // Check that the gpu is capable of running flash attention\n  using sm80 = SMVersion<8, 0>;\n  using sm121 = SMVersion<12, 1>;\n#if USE_ROCM\n#if USE_ROCM_ATTENTION\n  if(at::globalContext().getROCmFAPreferredBackend() == at::ROCmFABackend::Ck) {\n    // User explicitly set CK as the flash attention backend. Return true for now\n    // TODO: Flesh out sanity checks\n    return true;\n  } else {\n    auto stream = at::cuda::getCurrentCUDAStream().stream();\n    if (hipSuccess != aotriton::v2::flash::check_gpu(stream)) {\n        auto dprops = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n        if (debug) {\n            TORCH_WARN(\n                    \"Flash attention was not compiled for current AMD GPU architecture. Attempting to run on architecture \", dprops->gcnArchName);\n        }\n        return false;\n    }\n#if AOTRITON_VERSION_MINOR >= 9\n    if (aotriton::isArchExperimentallySupported(stream)) {\n      static const bool enable_experimental = c10::utils::check_env(\"TORCH_ROCM_AOTRITON_ENABLE_EXPERIMENTAL\") == true;\n      if (!enable_experimental) {\n        TORCH_WARN_ONCE(\"Flash Efficient attention on Current AMD GPU is still experimental.\"\n            \" Enable it with TORCH_ROCM_AOTRITON_ENABLE_EXPERIMENTAL=1.\");\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n#endif\n  }\n#else\n  return false;\n#endif\n#else\n  auto dprops = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n  if (!check_sm_version<sm80, sm121>(dprops)) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\n          \"Flash attention only supports gpu architectures in the range [sm80, sm121]. Attempting to run on a sm \",\n          dprops->major,\n          \".\",\n          dprops->minor,\n          \" gpu.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n#endif\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool check_mem_efficient_hardware_support(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  // Mem Efficient attention supports hardware in the range [sm_50, sm_90]\n  using sm50 = SMVersion<5, 0>;\n  using sm121 = SMVersion<12, 1>;\n#if USE_ROCM\n#if USE_ROCM_ATTENTION\n  if(at::globalContext().getROCmFAPreferredBackend() == at::ROCmFABackend::Ck) {\n    // User explicitly set CK as the flash attention backend. Return true for now\n    // TODO: Flesh out sanity checks\n    return true;\n  } else {\n    auto stream = at::cuda::getCurrentCUDAStream().stream();\n    if (hipSuccess != aotriton::v2::flash::check_gpu(stream)) {\n        auto dprops = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n        if (debug) {\n            TORCH_WARN(\n                    \"Mem Efficient attention was not compiled for current AMD GPU architecture. Attempting to run on architecture \", dprops->gcnArchName);\n        }\n        return false;\n    }\n#if AOTRITON_VERSION_MINOR >= 9\n    if (aotriton::isArchExperimentallySupported(stream)) {\n      static const bool enable_experimental = c10::utils::check_env(\"TORCH_ROCM_AOTRITON_ENABLE_EXPERIMENTAL\") == true;\n      if (!enable_experimental) {\n        TORCH_WARN_ONCE(\"Mem Efficient attention on Current AMD GPU is still experimental.\"\n            \" Enable it with TORCH_ROCM_AOTRITON_ENABLE_EXPERIMENTAL=1.\");\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n#endif\n  }\n#else\n  return false;\n#endif\n#else\n  auto dprops = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n  if (!check_sm_version<sm50, sm121>(dprops)) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\n          \"Mem Efficient Attention only supports gpu architectures in the range [sm50, sm121]. Attempting to run on a sm \",\n          dprops->major,\n          \".\",\n          dprops->minor,\n          \" gpu.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n#endif\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool check_requires_grad_and_head_dim_gt192_constraints_on_sm86_89_or_120(\n    sdp_params const& params,\n    bool debug) {\n  // Flash Attention will raise an error in the backward pass if the head_dim\n  // size is greater than 192 And the device is between in the range [sm86, sm89]\n  using sm86 = SMVersion<8, 6>;\n  using sm89 = SMVersion<8, 9>;\n  using sm120 = SMVersion<12, 0>;\n  using sm121 = SMVersion<12, 1>;\n  auto dprops = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n  bool is_sm86_or_sm89 = check_sm_version<sm86, sm89>(dprops);\n  bool is_sm120_or_sm121 = check_sm_version<sm120, sm121>(dprops);\n  bool is_head_dim_gt192 = params.query.sym_size(-1) > 192;\n  bool is_head_dim_lte224 = params.query.sym_size(-1) <= 224;\n  bool is_dropout = params.dropout > 0.0;\n  //  head_dim size  in (192, 224] is not supported on sm86 and sm89\n  bool cond1 = is_head_dim_gt192 && is_head_dim_lte224;\n  // head_dim size > 224 and is_dropout is not supported on sm86 and sm89\n  bool cond2 = params.query.sym_size(-1) > 224 && is_dropout;\n  if (input_requires_grad(params) && (is_sm86_or_sm89 || is_sm120_or_sm121) && (cond1 || cond2)) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\n          \"Flash attention currently doesn't support training with head_dim in (192, 224] or \"\n          \"(head_dim in (224, 256] and dropout > 0.0) on gpu architectures in the range[sm86, sm89].\",\n          \"Attempting to run with dropout set to: \", params.dropout,\n          \"and head_dim: \",\n          params.query.sym_size(-1), \" on a sm \", dprops->major, \".\",\n          dprops->minor, \" gpu.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool check_flash_causal_non_square_seqlens(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  // FlashAttention 2 updated the default mask meaning for causal in this PR:\n  // 9e5e8bc91e it is now aligned to lower_right which would be a BC break\n  // for non-square masks. We will not support non-square masks for causal w/ FAV2\n  if (params.is_causal &&\n      !params.query.is_nested() && !params.key.is_nested() &&\n      params.query.sym_size(-2) != params.key.sym_size(-2)) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\n          \"Flash attention does not support the is_causal flag when seqlen_q != seqlen_k. \",\n          \"Got seqlen_q: \", params.query.sym_size(-2), \" seqlen_k: \",\n          params.key.sym_size(-2), \". If you would like to use causal attention with non-square masks, please see CausalAttnMask.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool check_all_tensors_on_device(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  // Check that all tensors are on the GPU device\n  // This should be handled by the stub dispatch, but we call can_use_*_attention\n  // directly from python we need to ensure that the tensors are on cuda\n  if (params.query.device().type() != at::DeviceType::CUDA) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\n          \"All tensors need to be on cuda device. Got query on device: \",\n          params.query.device(),\n          \", key on device: \",\n          params.key.device(),\n          \", value on device: \",\n          params.value.device());\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool check_cudnn_tensor_shapes(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  const auto s_q = params.query.sym_size(2);\n  const auto s_k = params.key.sym_size(2);\n  const auto d_qk = params.query.sym_size(3);\n  const auto d_v = params.value.sym_size(3);\n  long cudnn_version = at::detail::getCUDAHooks().versionRuntimeCuDNN();\n  if (cudnn_version < 8903) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\"SDPA fprop requires cudnn 8.9.3 or higher\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  if (cudnn_version < 8906 && params.dropout != 0.0) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\"Dropout reference is only supported on 8.9.6 onwards.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  auto head_dim_limit = 128;\n  if (cudnn_version >= 91000) {\n    auto dprops = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n    if (dprops->major == 9 && !dprops->minor) {\n      head_dim_limit = 256;\n    }\n  }\n  if (d_qk > head_dim_limit || d_v > head_dim_limit) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\"head_dim should be no more than \", head_dim_limit);\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  if (d_qk % 8 != 0 || d_v % 8 != 0) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\"head_dim should be a multiple of 8\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  if (cudnn_version < 8906 && s_k % 64 != 0 ) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\"not-multiple-of-64 seq_kv is not supported below 8.9.6\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  if (cudnn_version < 90000) {\n    if (s_q < 64) {\n      if (debug) {\n        TORCH_WARN(\"s_q less than 64 is not supported before cudnn 9.0.0\");\n      }\n      return false;\n    }\n    if (params.dropout != 0.0 && (s_q % 64 != 0 || s_k % 64 != 0)) {\n      if (debug) {\n        TORCH_WARN(\n            \"s_q not a multiple of 64 with padding/dropout is not supported with cudnn version 9.0.0\");\n      }\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n  if (s_k == 1) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN_ONCE(\"cudnn SDPA does not support key/value sequence length 1.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  if (s_q == 1 && params.dropout != 0.0) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN_ONCE(\"cudnn SDPA does not support query sequence length 1 with dropout.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool check_cudnn_layout(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  const int64_t h = params.query.size(1);\n  const int64_t s_q = params.query.size(2);\n  const int64_t d = params.query.size(3);\n  const int64_t s_k = params.key.size(2);\n  const int64_t s_v = params.value.size(2);\n  // corresponds to cuDNN's \"packed QKV\" layout\n  const bool packed_query_layout_ok = (params.query.stride(0) == s_q * 3 * h * d) &&\n                                 (params.query.stride(1) == d) &&\n                                 (params.query.stride(2) == 3 * h * d) &&\n                                 (params.query.stride(3) == 1);\n  const bool packed_key_layout_ok = (params.key.stride(0) == s_k * 3 * h * d) &&\n                               (params.key.stride(1) == d) &&\n                               (params.key.stride(2) == 3 * h * d) &&\n                               (params.key.stride(3) == 1);\n  const bool packed_value_layout_ok = (params.value.stride(0) == s_v * 3 * h * d) &&\n                                 (params.value.stride(1) == d) &&\n                                 (params.value.stride(2) == 3 * h * d) &&\n                                 (params.value.stride(3) == 1);\n\n  const bool packed_layout_ok = packed_query_layout_ok && packed_key_layout_ok && packed_value_layout_ok;\n\n  const bool query_layout_ok = (params.query.stride(0) == s_q * h * d) &&\n                               (params.query.stride(1) == d) &&\n                               (params.query.stride(2) == h * d) &&\n                               (params.query.stride(3) == 1);\n  const bool key_layout_ok = (params.key.stride(0) == s_k * h * d) &&\n                              (params.key.stride(1) == d) &&\n                              (params.key.stride(2) == h * d) &&\n                              (params.key.stride(3) == 1);\n  const bool value_layout_ok = (params.value.stride(0) == s_v * h * d) &&\n                               (params.value.stride(1) == d) &&\n                               (params.value.stride(2) == h * d) &&\n                               (params.value.stride(3) == 1);\n\n  const bool layout_ok = query_layout_ok && key_layout_ok && value_layout_ok;\n\n  if (!packed_value_layout_ok && !layout_ok) {\n    if (debug) {\n      if (!packed_layout_ok) {\n        if (!packed_query_layout_ok) {\n          TORCH_WARN(\"Query tensor was not in cuDNN-supported packed QKV layout\", params.query.strides());\n        }\n        if (!packed_key_layout_ok) {\n          TORCH_WARN(\"Key tensor was not in cuDNN-supported packed QKV layout\", params.key.strides());\n        }\n        if (!packed_value_layout_ok) {\n          TORCH_WARN(\"Value tensor was not in cuDNN-supported packed QKV layout\", params.value.strides());\n        }\n      }\n      if (!layout_ok) {\n        if (!query_layout_ok) {\n          TORCH_WARN(\"Query tensor was not in cuDNN-supported unpacked QKV layout\", params.query.strides());\n        }\n        if (!key_layout_ok) {\n          TORCH_WARN(\"Key tensor was not in cuDNN-supported unpacked QKV layout\", params.key.strides());\n        }\n        if (!value_layout_ok) {\n          TORCH_WARN(\"Value tensor was not in cuDNN-supported unpacked QKV layout\", params.value.strides());\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool check_cudnn_hardware_support(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  using sm80 = SMVersion<8, 0>;\n  using sm121 = SMVersion<12, 1>;\n  auto dprops = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n  if (!check_sm_version<sm80, sm121>(dprops)) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\n          \"cuDNN MHA only supports gpu architectures in the range [sm80, sm121]. Attempting to run on a sm \",\n          dprops->major,\n          \".\",\n          dprops->minor,\n          \" gpu.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool check_for_nested_inputs(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  static const bool enable_cudnn_nested = c10::utils::check_env(\"TORCH_CUDNN_SDPA_NESTED_TENSOR_ENABLED\") == true;\n  if (has_for_nested_inputs(params) && !enable_cudnn_nested) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\"Experimental cuDNN SDPA nested tensor support is not enabled.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  } else if (has_for_nested_inputs(params) && (params.query.requires_grad() || params.key.requires_grad() || params.value.requires_grad())) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\"Experimental cuDNN SDPA nested tensor support does not support backward.\");\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  const auto dprop = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n  // Check that the input is nested\n  if (!(dprop->major == 9 || dprop->major == 10) && has_for_nested_inputs(params)) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\"cuDNN SDPA supports nested tensors on SM 9.0, SM 10.0.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool check_dtypes_low_precision(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  auto dprop = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n  if (dprop->major >= 8) {\n    constexpr auto sm80_dtypes =\n        c10::array_of<at::ScalarType>(at::kHalf, at::kBFloat16);\n    return check_tensor_dtype(params, sm80_dtypes, debug);\n  } else {\n    constexpr auto default_dtypes = c10::array_of<at::ScalarType>(at::kHalf);\n    return check_tensor_dtype(params, default_dtypes, debug);\n  }\n}\n\nbool check_runtime_disabled_cudnn(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  // We check the global context to see if user has explicitly turned of cudnn\n  // sdp kernels\n  if (!at::globalContext().userEnabledCuDNNSDP()) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\"cuDNN attention has been runtime disabled.\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool check_cudnn_deterministic(const sdp_params& params, bool debug) {\n  auto& ctx = at::globalContext();\n  if (ctx.deterministicAlgorithms()) {\n    if (!ctx.deterministicAlgorithmsWarnOnly()) {\n      if (debug) {\n        TORCH_WARN(\"cuDNN SDPA is not deterministic.\");\n      }\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\n} // namespace\n\nbool can_use_cudnn_attention(const sdp_params& params, bool debug) {\n#if defined(USE_ROCM) || !AT_CUDNN_ENABLED() || !defined(CUDNN_VERSION)\n  if (debug) {\n    TORCH_WARN(\"Torch was not compiled with cuDNN attention.\");\n  }\n  return false;\n#endif\n#if defined(CUDNN_VERSION) && CUDNN_VERSION < 90000\n  if (debug) {\n    TORCH_WARN(CUDNN_VERSION, \" cuDNN version too old to use cuDNN Attention (< v9.0.0)\");\n  }\n  return false;\n#endif\n#if defined(CUDNN_VERSION)\n  static auto cudnn_version = at::detail::getCUDAHooks().versionRuntimeCuDNN();\n  if (params.dropout > 0.0 && cudnn_version > 91100 && cudnn_version < 91400) {\n    if (debug) {\n      TORCH_WARN(CUDNN_VERSION, \" cuDNN version does not support droppout in SDPA (9.11 - 9.13).\");\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n#endif\n  // Define gate functions that determine if a flash kernel can be ran\n  // Replace with std::to_array when we migrate to c++20\n  constexpr auto general_constraints =\n      c10::array_of<bool (*)(sdp_params const&, bool)>(\n          check_runtime_disabled_cudnn,\n          check_for_nested_inputs,\n          check_all_tensors_on_device,\n          check_tensor_shapes,\n          check_cudnn_deterministic,\n          check_dtypes_low_precision,\n          check_attn_mask_shape,\n          check_cudnn_hardware_support\n          );\n  for (auto& constraint : general_constraints) {\n    if (!constraint(params, debug)) {\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n  constexpr auto dense_constraints =\n      c10::array_of<bool (*)(sdp_params const&, bool)>(\n      check_nonzero_sequence_lengths_dense,\n      check_last_dim_stride_equals_1_dense<true /*ignore_singleton_dim=*/>,\n      check_batch_size_and_num_heads_dense<true /*enable_gqa*/, false /*requires_same_num_heads*/>,\n      check_cudnn_tensor_shapes\n  );\n\n  if (has_only_dense_inputs(params)) {\n    for (auto& constraint : dense_constraints) {\n      if (!constraint(params, debug)) {\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nbool is_flash_attention_available() {\n#ifdef USE_FLASH_ATTENTION\n  return true;\n#else\n  return false;\n#endif\n}\n\nbool can_use_flash_attention(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n#ifndef USE_FLASH_ATTENTION\n  if (debug) {\n    TORCH_WARN(\"Torch was not compiled with flash attention.\");\n  }\n  return false;\n#else // defined(USE_FLASH_ATTENTION)\n  // Define gate functions that determine if a flash kernel can be ran\n  // Replace with std::to_array when we migrate to c++20\n  constexpr auto general_constraints = c10::array_of<bool (*)(sdp_params const&, bool)>(\n      check_runtime_disabled_flash,\n      check_all_tensors_on_device,\n      check_tensor_shapes,\n      check_for_attn_mask,\n      check_head_dim_size_flash<false /*caller_is_meff*/>,\n      check_flash_attention_hardware_support,\n      check_requires_grad_and_head_dim_gt192_constraints_on_sm86_89_or_120,\n      check_flash_causal_non_square_seqlens,\n      check_dtypes_low_precision);\n  for (auto& constraint : general_constraints) {\n    if (!constraint(params, debug)) {\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (has_for_nested_inputs(params)) {\n    constexpr auto nested_constraints = c10::array_of<bool (*)(sdp_params const&, bool)>(\n        check_batch_size_nested,\n        check_head_dim_size_flash_nested<false /*caller_is_meff*/>,\n        check_for_seq_len_0_nested_tensor);\n    for (auto& constraint : nested_constraints) {\n      if (!constraint(params, debug)) {\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  constexpr bool backend_supports_grouped_query_attention = true;\n  if (has_only_dense_inputs(params)) {\n    constexpr auto dense_constraints = c10::array_of<bool (*)(sdp_params const&, bool)>(\n        check_batch_size_and_num_heads_dense<backend_supports_grouped_query_attention>,\n        check_nonzero_sequence_lengths_dense,\n        check_last_dim_stride_equals_1_dense<true /*ignore_singleton_dim=*/>);\n    for (auto& constraint : dense_constraints) {\n      if (!constraint(params, debug)) {\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  return true;\n#endif // defined(USE_FLASH_ATTENTION)\n}\n\nbool can_use_mem_efficient_attention(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n#ifndef USE_MEM_EFF_ATTENTION\n  TORCH_WARN_ONCE(!debug, \"Torch was not compiled with memory efficient attention.\");\n  return false;\n#endif\n  // Constraints specific to mem efficient attention\n  constexpr auto less_than_sm80_mem_efficient_dtypes =\n      c10::array_of<at::ScalarType>(at::kHalf, at::kFloat);\n#ifdef USE_ROCM\n  constexpr auto aotriton_mem_efficient_dtypes =\n      c10::array_of<at::ScalarType>(at::kHalf, at::kFloat, at::kBFloat16);\n  constexpr auto ck_mem_efficient_dtypes =\n      c10::array_of<at::ScalarType>(at::kHalf, at::kBFloat16);\n#else\n  constexpr auto greater_than_or_equal_sm80_mem_efficient_dtypes =\n      c10::array_of<at::ScalarType>(at::kHalf, at::kFloat, at::kBFloat16);\n#endif\n\n  //  Define gate functions that determine if a mem efficient kernel can be ran\n  constexpr auto general_constraints = c10::array_of<bool (*)(sdp_params const&, bool)>(\n      check_runtime_disabled_mem_efficient,\n      check_all_tensors_on_device,\n      check_mem_efficient_hardware_support,\n      check_tensor_shapes,\n#ifdef USE_ROCM\n      check_head_dim_size_flash<true /* caller_is_meff */>\n#else\n      check_head_dim_size_mem_efficient\n#endif\n  );\n  for (auto& constraint : general_constraints) {\n    if (!constraint(params, debug)) {\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (has_for_nested_inputs(params)) {\n    constexpr auto nested_constraints = c10::array_of<bool (*)(sdp_params const&, bool)>(\n#ifndef USE_ROCM  // ME and FA shares backend on ROCM and thus supports training\n        check_requires_grad_and_nested,\n#else // Meanwhile ME on ROCM share the limits of FA about head dimensions\n        check_head_dim_size_flash_nested<true /* caller_is_meff */>,\n#endif\n        check_batch_size_nested,\n        check_for_seq_len_0_nested_tensor);\n    for (auto& constraint : nested_constraints) {\n      if (!constraint(params, debug)) {\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  if (has_only_dense_inputs(params)) {\n    constexpr auto dense_constraints = c10::array_of<bool (*)(sdp_params const&, bool)>(\n        check_nonzero_sequence_lengths_dense,\n        check_last_dim_stride_equals_1_dense<false /*ignore_singleton_dim=*/>,\n        check_batch_size_and_num_heads_dense<false /*supports_grouped_query_attention=*/>);\n    for (auto& constraint : dense_constraints) {\n      if (!constraint(params, debug)) {\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n#ifdef USE_ROCM\n  if (params.attn_mask.has_value()) {\n    const auto q_dtype = params.query.dtype();\n    const auto bias_dtype = params.attn_mask.value().dtype();\n    if (bias_dtype != at::kBool && bias_dtype != q_dtype) {\n      TORCH_WARN(\"Efficient attention on ROCM requires attn_mask be boolean, or has the same datatype as of q,k,v\");\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n  if(at::globalContext().getROCmFAPreferredBackend() == at::ROCmFABackend::Ck) {\n    return check_tensor_dtype(params, ck_mem_efficient_dtypes, debug);\n  }\n  return check_tensor_dtype(params, aotriton_mem_efficient_dtypes, debug);\n#else\n  auto dprop = at::cuda::getCurrentDeviceProperties();\n  if (dprop->major >= 8) {\n    return check_tensor_dtype(params, greater_than_or_equal_sm80_mem_efficient_dtypes, debug);\n  }\n#endif\n  return check_tensor_dtype(params, less_than_sm80_mem_efficient_dtypes, debug);\n}\n\nSDPBackend select_sdp_backend(sdp_params const& kernel_params) {\n  // This function defines the priority order of the different sdp backends\n  // 1. Flash Attention\n  // 2. Mem Efficient Attention\n  // 3. Math fallback\n  auto& ctx = at::globalContext();\n  if (!ctx.userEnabledMathSDP() && !ctx.userEnabledFlashSDP() &&\n      !ctx.userEnabledMemEfficientSDP() && !ctx.userEnabledCuDNNSDP()) {\n    return SDPBackend::error;\n  }\n  // Get ideal kernel ordering\n  const auto ordering = priority_order(kernel_params);\n\n  // Because TORCHCHECK checks if condition is true we negate debug so that\n  // The statements will be printed when debug is true\n  bool print_debug = false;\n  for (auto& backend : ordering) {\n    switch (backend) {\n      case SDPBackend::cudnn_attention:\n        if (sdp::can_use_cudnn_attention(kernel_params, print_debug)) {\n              return SDPBackend::cudnn_attention;\n        }\n        break;\n      case SDPBackend::flash_attention:\n        if (sdp::can_use_flash_attention(kernel_params, print_debug)) {\n          return SDPBackend::flash_attention;\n        }\n        break;\n      case SDPBackend::efficient_attention:\n        if (sdp::can_use_mem_efficient_attention(kernel_params, print_debug)) {\n          return SDPBackend::efficient_attention;\n        }\n        break;\n      case SDPBackend::math:\n        if (ctx.userEnabledMathSDP()) {\n          return SDPBackend::math;\n        }\n        break;\n      case SDPBackend::overrideable:\n        if (ctx.userEnabledOverrideableSDP()) {\n          TORCH_CHECK(false, \"Invalid backend\");\n        }\n        break;\n      default:\n        TORCH_CHECK(false, \"Invalid backend\");\n    }\n  }\n  // If we have gotten to this point then two things have happened:\n  // 1. use_flash_attention or use_mem_efficient did not satisfy the\n  // constraints to be ran\n  // 2. The user has explicitly disabled the math kernel\n  // We then re-run the kernel checks with debug enabled to print out the\n  // reason why the kernel was not selected\n\n  print_debug = true;\n  TORCH_WARN(\"Memory efficient kernel not used because:\");\n  sdp::can_use_mem_efficient_attention(kernel_params, print_debug);\n  TORCH_WARN(\"Flash attention kernel not used because:\");\n  sdp::can_use_flash_attention(kernel_params, print_debug);\n  TORCH_WARN(\"cuDNN attention kernel not used because:\");\n  sdp::can_use_cudnn_attention(kernel_params, print_debug);\n  TORCH_CHECK(!print_debug, \"No available kernel. Aborting execution.\")\n  return SDPBackend::error;\n}\n\nbool check_for_seq_len_1_nested_tensor(sdp_params const& params, bool debug) {\n  // When this function is called we are assured that the nt is dim==4\n  if (!params.query.is_nested()) {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  const auto nt_q_tensor_impl =\n      at::native::get_nested_tensor_impl(params.query);\n  const at::Tensor& sizes = nt_q_tensor_impl->get_nested_sizes();\n  auto* sizes_ptr = sizes.data_ptr<int64_t>();\n  const int64_t n_tensors = params.query.size(0);\n  const int64_t size_tensor_stride = sizes.stride(0);\n\n  // This is being called inside sdp with shape [batch, heads, {seq_len}, dim]\n  for (const auto i : c10::irange(n_tensors)) {\n    if (sizes_ptr[(i * size_tensor_stride) + 1] <= 1) {\n      if (debug) {\n        TORCH_WARN(\n            \"Packed projection for fused kernels does not support sequence_length <= 1\");\n      }\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return true;\n}\n\n} // namespace sdp\n",
  "path": "/workspace/aten/src/ATen/native/transformers/cuda/sdp_utils.cpp",
  "security_risk": "LOW"
}

<ATen/native/cpu/DepthwiseConvKernel.h>\n#include <ATen/native/utils/ParamUtils.h>\n#include <ATen/native/xnnpack/Engine.h>\n#include <c10/core/GradMode.h>\n#include <c10/util/accumulate.h>\n#include <c10/util/irange.h>\n#include <c10/macros/Macros.h>\n#include <algorithm>\n#include <limits>\n#include <utility>\n\n#ifndef AT_PER_OPERATOR_HEADERS\n#include <ATen/Functions.h>\n#else\n#include <ATen/ops/permute.h>\n#endif\n\n#if AT_NNPACK_ENABLED()\n#include <nnpack.h>\n#endif\n\n#if AT_MKLDNN_ENABLED()\n#include <ATen/native/mkldnn/Utils.h>\n#endif\n\n#ifdef USE_MPS\n#include <ATen/mps/MPSDevice.h>\n#endif\n\n#ifndef AT_PER_OPERATOR_HEADERS\n#include <ATen/Functions.h>\n#include <ATen/NativeFunctions.h>\n#else\n#include <ATen/ops/_conv_depthwise2d.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/_convolution.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/_convolution_double_backward_native.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/_convolution_mode.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/_convolution_mode_native.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/_convolution_native.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/_mps_convolution.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/_mps_convolution_transpose.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/_nnpack_available.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/_nnpack_spatial_convolution.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/_slow_conv2d_backward.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/_unsafe_view.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/cat.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/constant_pad_nd.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/conv1d_native.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/conv2d_native.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/conv3d_native.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/conv_depthwise3d.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/conv_transpose1d_native.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/conv_transpose2d_native.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/conv_transpose3d_native.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/convolution.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/convolution_backward_native.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/convolution_backward_overrideable.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/convolution_backward_overrideable_native.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/convolution_native.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/convolution_overrideable.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/convolution_overrideable_native.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/cudnn_convolution.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/cudnn_convolution_transpose.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/empty.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/empty_like.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/empty_native.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/miopen_convolution.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/miopen_convolution_transpose.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/miopen_depthwise_convolution.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/mkldnn_convolution.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/mps_convolution_backward.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/mps_convolution_transpose_backward.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/slow_conv3d.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/slow_conv_dilated2d.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/slow_conv_dilated3d.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/slow_conv_transpose2d.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/slow_conv_transpose3d.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/thnn_conv2d.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/view_as_real.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/zeros.h>\n#include <ATen/ops/zeros_like.h>\n#endif\n\nconstexpr int MIOPEN_DIM_MAX = 5;\n\nnamespace at::native {\n\n\nstatic bool conv_benchmark_empty_cache = true;\n\n// Check workload to activate fast depthwise FP16 cudnn conv kernels\ntemplate <typename T>\nstatic bool check_cudnn_depthwise_workload(const at::Tensor& input, T stride) {\n  auto w = at::symint::size<T>(input, 3);  // same as h\n  auto ch = at::symint::size<T>(input, 1);\n  auto bs = at::symint::size<T>(input, 0);\n  if (stride==1) {\n    if (w >= 7) {\n      // All batch sizes and nb_channels\n      if (w >= 112) {\n        return true;\n      }\n\n      // large nb_channels\n      if (ch >= 1024) {\n        // NOLINTNEXTLINE(bugprone-branch-clone,cppcoreguidelines-avoid-magic-numbers)\n        if (w >= 56) {\n          return true;\n        } else if (bs >= 32) {\n          return true;\n        }\n      }\n\n      // batch_size specific\n      if (bs >= 128) {\n        // NOLINTNEXTLINE(bugprone-branch-clone,cppcoreguidelines-avoid-magic-numbers)\n        if (ch >= 512) {\n          return true;\n        } else if (ch >= 64) {\n          if (w >= 14) {\n            return true;\n          }\n        } else if ((ch >= 32) && (w >=28)) {\n          return true;\n        }\n      } else if (bs >= 64) {\n        // NOLINTNEXTLINE(bugprone-branch-clone,cppcoreguidelines-avoid-magic-numbers)\n        if ((ch >= 256) && (w >= 14)) {\n          return true;\n        } else if ((ch >= 32) && (w >= 28)) {\n          return true;\n        }\n      } else if (bs >= 32) {\n        // NOLINTNEXTLINE(bugprone-branch-clone,cppcoreguidelines-avoid-magic-numbers)\n        if ((ch >= 256) && (w >= 14)) {\n          return true;\n        } else if ((ch >= 128) && (w >= 28)) {\n          return true;\n        } else if ((ch >= 32) && (w >= 56)) {\n          return true;\n        }\n      } else if (bs >= 16) {\n        if ((ch >= 1024) && (w >= 14)) {\n          return true;\n        }\n        // NOLINTNEXTLINE(bugprone-branch-clone,cppcoreguidelines-avoid-magic-numbers)\n        if ((ch >= 256) && (w >= 28)) {\n          return true;\n        } else if ((ch >= 32) && (w >= 56)) {\n          return true;\n        }\n      } else if (bs >= 8) {\n        // NOLINTNEXTLINE(bugprone-branch-clone,cppcoreguidelines-avoid-magic-numbers)\n        if ((ch >= 512) && (w >= 28)) {\n          return true;\n        } else if ((ch >= 64) && (w >= 56)) {\n          return true;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  } else if (stride==2) {\n    if (ch < 256) {\n      return false;\n    }\n\n    if (w >= 7) {\n      if (bs >= 128) {\n        // NOLINTNEXTLINE(bugprone-branch-clone,cppcoreguidelines-avoid-magic-numbers)\n        if (ch >= 1024) {\n          return true;\n        } else if ((ch >= 512) && (w >= 14)) {\n          return true;\n        } else if (w >= 28) {\n          return true;\n        }\n      } else if (bs >= 64) {\n        // NOLINTNEXTLINE(bugprone-branch-clone,cppcoreguidelines-avoid-magic-numbers)\n        if ((ch >= 512) && (w >= 14)) {\n          return true;\n        } else if (w >= 28) {\n          return true;\n        }\n      } else if (bs >= 32) {\n        // NOLINTNEXTLINE(bugprone-branch-clone,cppcoreguidelines-avoid-magic-numbers)\n        if ((ch >= 1024) && (w >= 14)) {\n          return true;\n        } else if (w >= 28) {\n          return true;\n        }\n      } else if (bs >= 16) {\n        // NOLINTNEXTLINE(bugprone-branch-clone,cppcoreguidelines-avoid-magic-numbers)\n        if ((ch >= 512) && (w >= 28)) {\n          return true;\n        } else if (w >= 56) {\n          return true;\n        }\n      } else if (bs >= 8) {\n        // NOLINTNEXTLINE(bugprone-branch-clone,cppcoreguidelines-avoid-magic-numbers)\n        if ((ch >= 1024) && (w >= 28)) {\n          return true;\n        } else if (w >= 56) {\n          return true;\n        }\n      } else if (bs >= 1) {\n        if ((ch >= 512) && (w >=112)) {\n          return true;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  return false;\n}\n\n// simplified version for cudnn 8.2 and above\ntemplate <typename T>\nstatic bool check_cudnn_depthwise_workload_with_filter(const at::Tensor& input, T stride, const at::Tensor& weight) {\n  // 1D conv\n  if(at::symint::size<T>(input, 2) == 1 && stride == 1){\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  // 2d conv\n  // only square filters\n  if (at::symint::size<T>(weight, 2) != at::symint::size<T>(weight, 3)) return false;\n  auto filter = at::symint::size<T>(weight, 3);\n  // only 1/3/5 filter\n  if (filter != 1 && filter != 3 && filter != 5) return false;\n  // we don't enforce square input but only check width to reduce heuristic space\n  if (at::symint::size<T>(input, 3) < 7) return false; // min width 7\n  auto w = at::symint::size<T>(input, 3);\n  // only 1/2 stride, use cudnn for all stride 1\n  if (stride == 1) return true;\n  if (stride != 2) return false;\n\n  auto ch = at::symint::size<T>(input, 1);\n  auto bs = at::symint::size<T>(input, 0);\n  // special case since bs1 show good perf in lots of cases\n  if (bs == 1) {\n    if (filter == 1 && w <= 28) return true;\n    if (filter == 3 || filter == 5) return true;\n  } else {\n    if (filter == 1 && bs <= 16 && ch >= 128 && w <= 7) return true;\n    if (filter == 3 || filter == 5) {\n      if ((ch >= 512) || (ch >= 256 && w >= 28)) return true;\n    }\n  }\n  return false;\n}\n\n\n#if defined(C10_MOBILE)\nstatic bool xnnpack_use_convolution2d(\n    const Tensor& input,\n    const Tensor& weight,\n    const at::OptionalIntArrayRef bias_sizes_opt,\n    const IntArrayRef padding,\n    const IntArrayRef stride,\n    const IntArrayRef dilation,\n    const int64_t groups,\n    const bool transposed) {\n  return xnnpack::use_convolution2d(input, weight, bias_sizes_opt, padding, stride, dilation, groups, transposed);\n}\n\nstatic bool xnnpack_use_convolution2d(\n    const Tensor& input,\n    const Tensor& weight,\n    const at::OptionalSymIntArrayRef bias_sizes_opt,\n    const SymIntArrayRef padding,\n    const SymIntArrayRef stride,\n    const SymIntArrayRef dilation,\n    const c10::SymInt groups,\n    const bool transposed) {\n  // Never use xnnpack for symbolic tracing\n  return false;\n}\n#endif\n\n// This struct is templated so that we can run backend selection in a dynamic\n// shapes context; all of the real kernel selection in eager mode runs with\n// int64_t\ntemplate <typename T>\nstruct ConvParams {\n  std::vector<T> stride;\n  std::vector<T> padding;\n  std::vector<T> dilation;\n  bool transposed{};\n  std::vector<T> output_padding;\n  T groups{};\n  bool benchmark{};\n  bool deterministic{};\n  bool cudnn_enabled{};\n  bool allow_tf32{};\n\n  bool is_strided() const {\n    return std::any_of(\n      stride.cbegin(), stride.cend(), [](const T& s) { return s != 1; });\n  }\n\n  bool is_dilated() const {\n    return std::any_of(\n      dilation.cbegin(), dilation.cend(), [](const T& d) { return d != 1; });\n  }\n\n  bool is_padded() const {\n    return std::any_of(\n      padding.cbegin(), padding.cend(), [](const T& p) { return p != 0; });\n  }\n\n  bool is_output_padding_neg() const {\n    return std::any_of(\n      output_padding.cbegin(),\n      output_padding.cend(),\n      [](const T& p) { return p < 0; });\n  }\n\n  bool is_output_padding_big() const {\n    // Revisit this with std::views::zip at C++20.\n    for (auto i: c10::irange(output_padding.size())) {\n      if (output_padding[i] >= stride[i]) {\n        return true;\n      }\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  bool is_padding_neg() const {\n    return std::any_of(\n      padding.cbegin(), padding.cend(), [](const T& p) { return p < 0; });\n  }\n\n  bool is_dilation_neg() const {\n    return std::any_of(\n      dilation.cbegin(), dilation.cend(), [](const T& d) { return d < 0; });\n  }\n\n  bool is_stride_nonpos() const {\n    return std::any_of(\n      stride.cbegin(), stride.cend(), [](const T& s) { return s <= 0; });\n  }\n\n  void view1d_as_2d() {\n    if (stride.size() == 1) {\n      stride.insert(stride.begin(), 1);\n      padding.insert(padding.begin(), 0);\n      dilation.insert(dilation.begin(), 1);\n      output_padding.insert(output_padding.begin(), 0);\n    }\n  }\n\n  bool use_cpu_depthwise3x3_winograd(const at::Tensor& input, const at::Tensor& weight, const std::optional<at::Tensor>& bias) const {\n#if defined(__ARM_NEON__) || (defined(__riscv_v_intrinsic) && __riscv_v_intrinsic>=12000)\n    // Currently only 3x3 depthwise convolutions on tensors of float are supported.\n    return (input.ndimension() == 4) &&\n           (at::symint::size<T>(input, 1) == groups) &&\n           (weight.ndimension() == 4 ) &&\n           (at::symint::size<T>(weight, 0) % at::symint::size<T>(input, 1) == 0) &&\n           (at::symint::size<T>(weight, 1) == 1) &&\n           (at::symint::size<T>(weight, 2) == 3) &&\n           (at::symint::size<T>(weight, 3) == 3) &&\n           (input.device().is_cpu()) &&\n           (input.scalar_type() == at::kFloat) &&\n           input.is_contiguous() &&\n           (weight.device().is_cpu()) &&\n           (weight.scalar_type() == at::kFloat) &&\n           weight.is_contiguous() &&\n           (!bias.has_value() || bias->is_contiguous()) &&\n           !is_strided() &&\n           !is_dilated() &&\n           !transposed;\n#else\n    return false;\n#endif\n  }\n\n  bool needs_64bit_indexing_no_split(const at::Tensor& input, const at::Tensor& weight) const {\n    constexpr int64_t int_max = std::numeric_limits<int>::max();\n    auto numel_input = at::symint::numel<T>(input);\n    // empty input\n    if (numel_input == 0) {\n      return false;\n    }\n    // input size can not be reduced to the range of int by splitting the batch dim\n    auto n = at::symint::size<T>(input, 0);\n    if (numel_input / n > int_max) {\n      return true;\n    }\n    // output size can not be reduced to the range of int by splitting the batch dim\n    T outsize = 1;\n    if (transposed) {\n      auto o = conv_input_size(at::symint::sizes<T>(input), at::symint::sizes<T>(weight), padding, output_padding, stride, dilation, groups);\n      outsize = c10::multiply_integers(o.begin() + 1, o.end());\n    } else {\n      auto o = conv_output_size(at::symint::sizes<T>(input), at::symint::sizes<T>(weight), padding, stride, dilation);\n      outsize = c10::multiply_integers(o.begin() + 1, o.end());\n    }\n    return outsize > int_max;\n  }\n\n  bool use_cudnn(const at::Tensor& input, const at::Tensor& weight) const {\n  // Note [Mobile check segfaults]\n  // cudnn and miopen are guaranteed not to be on mobile, and T102591915 / T110194934 suggest\n  // that maybe the compiledWithCuDNN() check sometimes segfaults (though I can't imagine how)\n#if !defined(C10_MOBILE)\n    if (!detail::getCUDAHooks().compiledWithCuDNN() || !input.is_cuda() || !cudnn_enabled) {\n      return false;\n    }\n    static long cudnn_version = detail::getCUDAHooks().versionRuntimeCuDNN();\n    // broken on cuDNN 9.8 - 9.14\n    if (cudnn_version >= 90800 && cudnn_version < 91500) {\n      if (cudnn_conv_suggest_memory_format(input, weight) == at::MemoryFormat::Contiguous &&\n          (input.scalar_type() == at::kBFloat16 || input.scalar_type() == at::kHalf) &&\n          weight.dim() == 5) {\n        for (int i = 2; i < weight.dim(); i++) {\n          if (weight.size(i) != 1) {\n            return false;\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    if (needs_64bit_indexing_no_split(input, weight)) {\n      if (!(cudnn_version >= 90300 && at::native::cudnnv8_enabled_check_debug())) {\n        TORCH_WARN_ONCE(\"cuDNN cannot be used for large non-batch-splittable convolutions\"\n                        \" if the V8 API is not enabled or before cuDNN version 9.3+.\"\n                        \" Consider upgrading cuDNN and/or enabling the V8 API for better efficiency.\");\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n    if (input.scalar_type() == at::kBFloat16 || weight.scalar_type() == at::kBFloat16) {\n      if (!(detail::getCUDAHooks().supportsBFloat16ConvolutionWithCuDNNv8() && at::native::cudnnv8_enabled_check_debug())) {\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n    if (cudnn_conv_suggest_memory_format(input, weight) == at::MemoryFormat::Contiguous) {\n      if (is_dilated()) {\n        return detail::getCUDAHooks().supportsDilatedConvolutionWithCuDNN() && !is_output_padding_big();\n      }\n    }\n    return !is_output_padding_big();\n#else\n    return false;\n#endif\n  }\n\n  // Use cudnn for FP16 depthwise convolutions\n  bool use_cudnn_depthwise(const at::Tensor& input, const at::Tensor& weight) const  {\n    if (!cudnn_enabled || !detail::getCUDAHooks().compiledWithCuDNN() || !input.is_cuda()) {\n      return false;\n    }\n    // native kernel doesn't support 64-bit non-splittable case\n    if (!(canUse32BitIndexMath(input) && canUse32BitIndexMath(weight))) {\n      static long cudnn_version = detail::getCUDAHooks().compiledWithCuDNN() ? detail::getCUDAHooks().versionRuntimeCuDNN() : -1;\n      // TODO(eqy): remove this once cuDNN fixes 64-bit depthwise support, first broken in 9.11x\n      if (cudnn_conv_suggest_memory_format(input, weight) != at::MemoryFormat::Contiguous) {\n        if (cudnn_version < 0 || cudnn_version > 91000) {\n          return false;\n        }\n      }\n\n      if (!(cudnn_version >= 90300 && at::native::cudnnv8_enabled_check_debug())) {\n        TORCH_WARN_ONCE(\"cuDNN cannot be used for large non-batch-splittable convolutions\"\n                        \" if the V8 API is not enabled or before cuDNN version 9.3+.\"\n                        \" Upgrade cuDNN or enable the V8 API to use cuDNN for 64-bit depthwise convolutions.\");\n        return false;\n      } else {\n        return true;\n      }\n    }\n    if (cudnn_conv_suggest_memory_format(input, weight) != at::MemoryFormat::Contiguous) {\n      // always use cudnn_depthwise for channels_last format\n      return true;\n    }\n    if (detail::getCUDAHooks().supportsDepthwiseConvolutionWithCuDNN()) {\n      bool kernel_cond =  (use_cudnn(input, weight) &&\n                           input.scalar_type() == kHalf && // only for FP16\n                           weight.scalar_type() == kHalf &&\n                           is_depthwise(input, weight) &&\n                           input.ndimension() == 4 &&   // TODO: 5-D contiguous depthwise is not supported yet, need benchmarks\n                           !is_dilated() && // no dilation supported\n                           (stride[0] == stride[1] || at::symint::size<T>(input, 2) == 1) && // square or 1d\n                           at::symint::size<T>(input, 1) >= 32); // min 32 channels supported)\n      if (kernel_cond) {\n        return check_cudnn_depthwise_workload_with_filter<T>(input, stride[1], weight);\n      }\n      return false;\n    } else {\n      return false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  bool use_miopen(const at::Tensor& input, const at::Tensor& weight, bool bias_defined) const  {\n    if (needs_64bit_indexing_no_split(input, weight)) {\n      return false;\n    }\n    return ((input.scalar_type() == at::kFloat) || (input.scalar_type() == at::kHalf) || (input.scalar_type() == at::kBFloat16))\n           && cudnn_enabled\n           && input.is_cuda()\n           && detail::getCUDAHooks().compiledWithMIOpen()\n           && input.dim() <= MIOPEN_DIM_MAX\n           && !(groups > 1 && is_dilated()) // MIOpen currently does not support dilation with groups of size > 1\n           ;\n  }\n  bool use_mkldnn(const at::Tensor& input, const at::Tensor& weight) const  {\n#if AT_MKLDNN_ENABLED()\n    if (!at::globalContext().userEnabledMkldnn()) {\n      return false;\n    }\n    if (transposed && is_output_padding_big()) {\n      return false;\n    }\n    if (input.device().is_cpu() &&\n        ((input.scalar_type() == at::kBFloat16 && mkldnn_bf16_device_check()) ||\n         (input.scalar_type() == at::kHalf && mkldnn_fp16_device_check()))) {\n      return true;\n    }\n    return (input.is_mkldnn()) || // input is mkldnn Tensor\n      (input.device().is_cpu() &&\n       input.scalar_type() == kFloat && // only on CPU Float Tensors\n       // For 1x1 filters, MKLDNN is faster than THNN when multi-threaded,\n       // but THNN is faster when single-threaded.\n       (is_strided() || is_dilated() || at::symint::size<T>(input, 0) >= 16 ||\n        at::symint::size<T>(weight, -1) != 1 || at::symint::size<T>(weight, -2) != 1 || at::get_num_threads() > 1) &&\n       (groups > 1\n        || (at::symint::size<T>(weight, -1) > 3 && at::symint::size<T>(weight, -2) > 3)\n        || at::symint::size<T>(input, 0) > 1\n        || at::symint::size<T>(input, 0)*at::symint::size<T>(input, 1)*at::symint::size<T>(input, 2)*at::symint::size<T>(input, 3) > 20480) // for some case, native is faster\n        );\n\n#endif\n    return false;\n  }\n  bool use_nnpack(const at::Tensor& input, const at::Tensor& weight) const  {\n#if AT_NNPACK_ENABLED()\n    return at::globalContext().userEnabledNNPACK() &&\n           at::_nnpack_available() &&\n           input.device().is_cpu() &&\n           input.scalar_type() == kFloat && // only on CPU Float Tensors\n           !is_dilated() && // or dilation\n           !transposed &&   // or transposed tensors\n           input.ndimension() == 4 && // must be in NCHW format\n           weight.ndimension() == 4 &&\n           (at::symint::size<T>(weight, 2) < 17) && (at::symint::size<T>(weight, 3) < 17) && // NNPACK only supports kernels up to 16x16\n           (padding[0] < at::symint::size<T>(weight, 2)) && (padding[1] < at::symint::size<T>(weight, 3)) // NNPACK only supports padding < kernel_size. See https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/90142.\n#if !defined(C10_MOBILE)\n           && at::symint::size<T>(input, 0) >= 16 // ensure large enough batch size to ensure perf, tuneable\n#endif\n       ;\n#endif\n    return false;\n  }\n  bool use_xnnpack(const at::Tensor& input, const at::Tensor& weight,\n                   const at::OptionalArrayRef<T> bias_sizes_opt) const {\n#if defined(C10_MOBILE)\n    if (!transposed) {\n      // NB: for the call here, it MATTERS that we are templated. If you\n      // untemplate this to always use SymInt, the function\n      // xnnpack_use_convolution2d will always return false\n      return (at::symint::size<T>(input, 1) == groups) &&\n              xnnpack_use_convolution2d(\n                  input,\n                  weight,\n                  bias_sizes_opt,\n                  padding,\n                  stride,\n                  dilation,\n                  groups,\n                  transposed);\n    }\n#endif\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  bool use_mps(const at::Tensor& input, const at::Tensor& weight) const {\n    // These checks need to be expanded. Currently we have very limited set of\n    // checks for MPS.\n#ifdef USE_MPS\n    if (needs_64bit_indexing_no_split(input, weight)) {\n      return false;\n    }\n    if (!input.is_mps()) {\n      return false;\n    }\n    return true;\n#else\n    return false;\n#endif\n  }\n\n  // We currently only have depthwise support for the case where groups ==\n  // nInputPlane and nInputPlane == nOutputPlane (the latter due to the lack of\n  // a depthwise multiplier)\n  bool is_depthwise(const at::Tensor& input, const at::Tensor& weight) const  {\n    return input.is_cuda() &&\n           !transposed &&\n           (input.ndimension() == 4 || input.ndimension() == 5) &&\n           at::symint::size<T>(input, 1) == groups &&\n           groups > 1 && // no point if there is only a single group\n           at::symint::size<T>(weight, 0) % at::symint::size<T>(input, 1) == 0; // output channels must be a multiple of input channels\n  }\n};\n\nDEFINE_DISPATCH(conv_depthwise2d_backward_stub);\nDEFINE_DISPATCH(conv_depthwise3d_backward_stub);\nDEFINE_DISPATCH(cudnn_convolution_backward_stub);\nDEFINE_DISPATCH(cudnn_convolution_transpose_backward_stub);\nDEFINE_DISPATCH(slow_conv_transpose3d_backward_stub);\nDEFINE_DISPATCH(convolution_depthwise3x3_winograd_stub);\nDEFINE_DISPATCH(miopen_convolution_backward_stub);\nDEFINE_DISPATCH(miopen_convolution_transpose_backward_stub);\nDEFINE_DISPATCH(miopen_depthwise_convolution_backward_stub);\nDEFINE_DISPATCH(mkldnn_convolution_backward_stub);\nDEFINE_DISPATCH(mkldnn_convolution_transpose_stub);\nDEFINE_DISPATCH(mkldnn_convolution_transpose_backward_stub);\nDEFINE_DISPATCH(slow_conv_dilated2d_backward_stub);\nDEFINE_DISPATCH(slow_conv_dilated3d_backward_stub);\nDEFINE_DISPATCH(slow_conv_transpose2d_backward_stub);\nREGISTER_NO_CPU_DISPATCH(conv_depthwise2d_backward_stub)\nREGISTER_NO_CPU_DISPATCH(conv_depthwise3d_backward_stub)\nREGISTER_NO_CPU_DISPATCH(cudnn_convolution_backward_stub)\nREGISTER_NO_CPU_DISPATCH(cudnn_convolution_transpose_backward_stub)\nREGISTER_NO_CPU_DISPATCH(miopen_convolution_backward_stub)\nREGISTER_NO_CPU_DISPATCH(miopen_convolution_transpose_backward_stub)\nREGISTER_NO_CPU_DISPATCH(miopen_depthwise_convolution_backward_stub)\n\ntemplate <typename T>\nstatic std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream & out, const ConvParams<T>& params) {\n  out << \"ConvParams {\"\n      << \"  stride = \" << IntArrayRef{params.stride}\n      << \"  padding = \" << ArrayRef<T>{params.padding}\n      << \"  dilation = \" << IntArrayRef{params.dilation}\n      << \"  transposed = \" << params.transposed\n      << \"  output_padding = \" << ArrayRef<T>{params.output_padding}\n      << \"  groups = \" << params.groups\n      << \"  benchmark = \" << params.benchmark\n      << \"  deterministic = \" << params.deterministic\n      << \"  cudnn_enabled = \" << params.cudnn_enabled\n      << \"  allow_tf32 = \" << params.allow_tf32\n      << \"}\";\n  return out;\n}\n\ntemplate <typename T>\nstatic void check_shape_forward(const at::Tensor& input,\n                                const c10::ArrayRef<T>& weight_sizes, const at::Tensor& bias,\n                                const ConvParams<T>& params) {\n  int64_t k = input.ndimension();\n  int64_t weight_dim = weight_sizes.size();\n  auto groups = params.groups;\n  const auto& padding = params.padding;\n  const auto& dilation = params.dilation;\n  bool transposed = params.transposed;\n\n  TORCH_CHECK(!params.is_padding_neg(), \"negative padding is not supported\");\n  TORCH_CHECK(!params.is_output_padding_neg(), \"negative output_padding is not supported\");\n  TORCH_CHECK(!params.is_stride_nonpos(), \"non-positive stride is not supported\");\n  TORCH_CHECK(!params.is_dilation_neg(), \"dilation should be greater than zero\");\n\n  TORCH_CHECK(weight_dim == k,\n           \"Expected \", weight_dim, \"-dimensional input for \", weight_dim,\n           \"-dimensional weight \", weight_sizes, \", but got \", k, \"-dimensional input of size \",\n           at::symint::sizes<T>(input), \" instead\");\n  TORCH_CHECK(weight_sizes[0] >= groups,\n           \"Given groups=\", groups, \", expected weight to be at least \", groups,\n           \" at dimension 0, but got weight of size \", weight_sizes, \" instead\");\n  TORCH_CHECK(weight_sizes[0] % groups == 0,\n           \"Given groups=\", groups, \", expected weight to be divisible by \",\n           groups, \" at dimension 0, but got weight of size [\", weight_sizes,\n           \"] instead\");\n\n  if (!transposed) {\n    std::vector<T> input_shape;\n    std::vector<T> kernel_shape;\n    bool kernel_size_correct = true;\n\n    TORCH_CHECK(at::symint::size<T>(input, 1) == (weight_sizes[1] * groups),\n                \"Given groups=\", groups, \", weight of size \", weight_sizes,\n                \", expected input\", at::symint::sizes<T>(input), \" to have \",\n                (weight_sizes[1] * groups), \" channels, but got \", at::symint::size<T>(input, 1),\n                \" channels instead\");\n\n    TORCH_CHECK(!bias.defined() || (bias.ndimension() == 1 && at::symint::size<T>(bias, 0) == weight_sizes[0]),\n             \"Given weight of size \", weight_sizes,\n             \", expected bias to be 1-dimensional with \", weight_sizes[0], \" elements\",\n             \", but got bias of size \", at::symint::sizes<T>(bias), \" instead\");\n\n    for (const auto i : c10::irange(2, k)) {\n      input_shape.push_back(at::symint::size<T>(input, i) + 2 * padding[i-2]);\n      // log new kernel size considering dilation\n      kernel_shape.push_back(dilation[i-2] * (weight_sizes[i]-1) + 1);\n      if (input_shape.back() < kernel_shape.back()) {\n        kernel_size_correct = false;\n      }\n    }\n\n    TORCH_CHECK(input_shape.size() == kernel_shape.size(), \"Inconsistent shape between Input and Kernel\");\n\n    if (!kernel_size_correct) {\n      // If kernel size is incorrect\n      std::ostringstream input_ss;\n      std::ostringstream kernel_ss;\n      std::string separator = \"\";\n\n      for (int i = 0, len = input_shape.size(); i < len; ++i) {\n        input_ss << separator << input_shape[i];\n        kernel_ss << separator << kernel_shape[i];\n        separator = \" x \";\n      }\n\n      TORCH_CHECK(false, \"Calculated padded input size per channel: (\", input_ss.str(), \"). \"\n               \"Kernel size: (\", kernel_ss.str(), \"). Kernel size can't be greater than actual input size\");\n    }\n  } else { // transposed\n    TORCH_CHECK(at::symint::size<T>(input, 1) == weight_sizes[0],\n             \"Given transposed=\", transposed, \", weight of size \", weight_sizes,\n             \", expected input\", at::symint::sizes<T>(input), \" to have \", weight_sizes[0],\n             \" channels, but got \", at::symint::size<T>(input, 1), \" channels instead\");\n    TORCH_CHECK(!bias.defined() || (bias.ndimension() == 1 && at::symint::size<T>(bias, 0) == weight_sizes[1] * groups),\n             \"Given transposed=\", transposed, \", weight of size \", weight_sizes,\n             \", expected bias to be 1-dimensional with \", weight_sizes[1] * groups, \" elements\",\n             \", but got bias of size \", at::symint::sizes<T>(bias), \" instead\");\n  }\n}\n\ntemplate <typename T>\nstatic void check_shape_backward(\n    const at::Tensor& input,\n    const c10::ArrayRef<T>& weight_sizes,\n    const ConvParams<T>& params) {\n  check_shape_forward<T>(input, weight_sizes, /*bias=*/ Tensor(), params);\n}\n\n// Given an input tensor and an expected number of spatial dimensions, checks that the\n// input is a valid shape and returns the batched form of the input.\n//\n// Args:\n//     input (Tensor): Input tensor\n//     num_spatial_dims (int): Number of spatial dimensions expected for the input\n//     func_name (string): Function name to produce a nice error message for invalid input\n//\n// Returns a std::tuple containing:\n//     batched_input (Tensor): Input with a batch dimension\n//     is_batched (bool): Indicates whether the original input was already batched\nstatic std::tuple<Tensor, bool> batchify(\n    const Tensor& input,\n    const int64_t num_spatial_dims,\n    const std::string& func_name) {\n  // assume NTs are always batched\n  if (input.is_nested()) {\n    return std::make_tuple(input, true);\n  }\n  const auto dim_count_no_batch = num_spatial_dims + 1;\n  const auto dim_count_batch = dim_count_no_batch + 1;\n  const auto is_batched = (input.dim() == dim_count_batch);\n  TORCH_CHECK(input.dim() == dim_count_no_batch || is_batched,\n      \"Expected \", dim_count_no_batch, \"D (unbatched) or \", dim_count_batch,\n      \"D (batched) input to \", func_name, \", but got input of size: \", input.sizes());\n  return std::make_tuple(is_batched ? input : input.unsqueeze(0), is_batched);\n}\n\nstatic void check_input_same_type_as_parameters(\n    const Tensor& input,\n    const Tensor& weight,\n    const Tensor& bias) {\n  TORCH_CHECK(input.options().type_equal(weight.options()),\n      \"Input type (\", input.toString(), \") and weight type (\", weight.toString(),\n      \") should be the same\");\n  TORCH_CHECK(!bias.defined() || (input.options().type_equal(bias.options())),\n      \"Input type (\", input.toString(), \") and bias type (\", bias.toString(),\n      \") should be the same\");\n}\n\nstatic void check_input_same_type_as_parameters(\n    const Tensor& input,\n    const Tensor& weight) {\n  check_input_same_type_as_parameters(input, weight, /*bias=*/ Tensor());\n}\n\n#if AT_MKLDNN_ENABLED()\nstatic void check_input_same_type_as_parameters(\n    const Tensor& input,\n    const Tensor& weight,\n    const Tensor& bias,\n    const ConvBackend backend) {\n  if (backend == ConvBackend::Mkldnn || backend == ConvBackend::MkldnnTranspose) {\n    TORCH_CHECK(input.options().type_equal(weight.options())\n        || (input.is_mkldnn() && weight.device().is_cpu() && weight.scalar_type() == kFloat),\n        \"Input type (\", input.toString(), \") and weight type (\", weight.toString(),\n        \") should be the same or input should be a MKLDNN tensor and weight is a dense tensor\");\n    TORCH_CHECK(!bias.defined() || (input.options().type_equal(bias.options()))\n        || (input.is_mkldnn() && bias.device().is_cpu() && bias.scalar_type() == kFloat),\n        \"Input type (\", input.toString(), \") and bias type (\", bias.toString(),\n        \") should be the same or input should be a MKLDNN tensor and bias is a dense tensor\");\n  } else {\n    check_input_same_type_as_parameters(input, weight, bias);\n  }\n}\n#endif\n\nstatic auto view4d(const at::Tensor& tensor) -> at::Tensor {\n  TORCH_CHECK(tensor.ndimension() == 3,\n           \"expected 3D tensor, got tensor with \", tensor.ndimension(),\n           \" dimensions instead\");\n  return tensor.unsqueeze(2);\n}\n\nstatic auto view3d(const at::Tensor& tensor) -> at::Tensor {\n  TORCH_CHECK(tensor.ndimension() == 4,\n           \"expected 4D tensor, got tensor with \", tensor.ndimension(),\n           \" dimensions instead\");\n  return tensor.squeeze(2);\n}\n\nstatic at::Tensor subtensor(at::Tensor& tensor, int64_t dim, int64_t groups, int64_t g) {\n  if (!tensor.defined()) {\n    return at::Tensor();\n  }\n  const auto memory_format = tensor.suggest_memory_format();\n  int64_t n = tensor.sizes()[dim] / groups;\n  return tensor.narrow(dim, n * g, n).contiguous(memory_format);\n}\n\nnamespace {\n\nstd::pair<Tensor, Tensor> complex_to_real(const Tensor& inp) {\n  auto inp_view_as_complex = at::view_as_real(inp);\n  auto dim_i = inp_view_as_complex.dim() - 1;\n  auto i_r = inp_view_as_complex.select(dim_i, 0);\n  auto i_i = inp_view_as_complex.select(dim_i, 1);\n  return std::make_pair(i_r, i_i);\n}\n\nat::Tensor complex_convolution(\n    const Tensor& input,\n    const Tensor& weight,\n    const Tensor& bias,\n    SymIntArrayRef stride,\n    SymIntArrayRef padding,\n    SymIntArrayRef dilation,\n    bool transposed,\n    SymIntArrayRef output_padding,\n    const c10::SymInt& groups) {\n  check_input_same_type_as_parameters(input, weight, bias);\n  auto [i_r, i_i] = complex_to_real(input.resolve_conj());\n  auto [w_r, w_i] = complex_to_real(weight.resolve_conj());\n\n  // [NOTE] Complex Convolution\n  // conv(W, x, b) = conv(Wr, xr, br) - conv(Wi, xi, 0) + i(conv(Wi, xr, bi) + conv(Wr, xi, 0))\n  // where W, x and b are all complex inputs.\n  // With Gauss Trick:\n  // a = conv(Wr, xr, br),\n  // b = conv(Wi, xi, 0),\n  // c = conv(Wr + Wi, xr + xi, bi + br)\n  // conv(W, x, b) = a - b + i(c - a - b)\n  Tensor a, b, c;\n  if (!bias.defined()) {\n    a = at::convolution_symint(i_r, w_r, bias, stride, padding, dilation, transposed, output_padding, groups);\n    b = at::convolution_symint(i_i, w_i, bias, stride, padding, dilation, transposed, output_padding, groups);\n    c = at::convolution_symint(i_r + i_i, w_r + w_i, bias, stride, padding, dilation, transposed, output_padding, groups);\n  } else {\n    auto [b_r, b_i] = complex_to_real(bias.resolve_conj());\n    a = at::convolution_symint(i_r, w_r, b_r, stride, padding, dilation, transposed, output_padding, groups);\n    b = at::convolution_symint(i_i, w_i, Tensor(), stride, padding, dilation, transposed, output_padding, groups);\n    c = at::convolution_symint(i_r + i_i, w_r + w_i, b_r + b_i, stride, padding, dilation, transposed, output_padding, groups);\n  }\n\n  auto i = c10::Scalar(c10::complex<double>(0, 1));\n  return a - b + i * (c - a - b);\n}\n\nat::Tensor complex_convolution_mode(\n    const at::Tensor& input,\n    const at::Tensor& weight,\n    const std::optional<at::Tensor>& bias_opt,\n    c10::SymIntArrayRef stride,\n    std::string_view padding,\n    c10::SymIntArrayRef dilation,\n    const c10::SymInt& groups) {\n  auto bias = bias_opt.value_or(Tensor());\n  check_input_same_type_as_parameters(input, weight, bias);\n  auto [i_r, i_i] = complex_to_real(input.resolve_conj());\n  auto [w_r, w_i] = complex_to_real(weight.resolve_conj());\n\n  // See [NOTE] Complex Convolution\n  Tensor a, b, c;\n  if (!bias.defined()) {\n    a = at::_convolution_mode_symint(i_r, w_r, bias, stride, padding, dilation, groups);\n    b = at::_convolution_mode_symint(i_i, w_i, bias, stride, padding, dilation, groups);\n    c = at::_convolution_mode_symint(i_r + i_i, w_r + w_i, bias, stride, padding, dilation, groups);\n  } else {\n    auto [b_r, b_i] = complex_to_real(bias.resolve_conj());\n    a = at::_convolution_mode_symint(i_r, w_r, b_r, stride, padding, dilation, groups);\n    b = at::_convolution_mode_symint(i_i, w_i, Tensor(), stride, padding, dilation, groups);\n    c = at::_convolution_mode_symint(i_r + i_i, w_r + w_i, b_r + b_i, stride, padding, dilation, groups);\n  }\n\n  auto i = c10::Scalar(c10::complex<double>(0, 1));\n  return a - b + i * (c - a - b);\n}\n\n} // namespace\n\nat::Tensor conv1d_symint(\n    const Tensor& input_, const Tensor& weight, const std::optional<Tensor>& bias_opt,\n    SymIntArrayRef stride, SymIntArrayRef padding, SymIntArrayRef dilation, c10::SymInt groups) {\n  // See [Note: hacky wrapper removal for optional tensor]\n  c10::MaybeOwned<Tensor> bias_maybe_owned = at::borrow_from_optional_tensor(bias_opt);\n  const Tensor& bias = *bias_maybe_owned;\n\n  TORCH_CHECK(\n    !bias.defined() || bias.dtype() == input_.dtype(),\n    \"Input type (\",\n    input_.dtype().name(),\n    \") and bias type (\",\n    bias.dtype().name(),\n    \") should be the same\");\n\n  auto [input, is_batched] = batchify(input_, /*num_spatial_dims=*/ 1, \"conv1d\");\n  Tensor output;\n  if (at::isComplexType(input_.scalar_type())) {\n    output = complex_convolution(input, weight, bias, stride, padding, dilation, false, {0}, groups);\n  } else {\n    output = at::convolution_symint(input, weight, bias, stride, padding, dilation, false, {0}, groups);\n  }\n  return is_batched ? std::move(output) : output.squeeze(0);\n}\n\nat::Tensor conv2d_symint(\n    const Tensor& input_, const Tensor& weight, const std::optional<Tensor>& bias_opt,\n    SymIntArrayRef stride, SymIntArrayRef padding, SymIntArrayRef dilation, c10::SymInt groups) {\n  // See [Note: hacky wrapper removal for optional tensor]\n  c10::MaybeOwned<Tensor> bias_maybe_owned = at::borrow_from_optional_tensor(bias_opt);\n  const Tensor& bias = *bias_maybe_owned;\n\n  TORCH_CHECK(\n    !bias.defined() || bias.dtype() == input_.dtype(),\n    \"Input type (\",\n    input_.dtype().name(),\n    \") and bias type (\",\n    bias.dtype().name(),\n    \") should be the same\");\n\n  auto [input, is_batched] = batchify(input_, /*num_spatial_dims=*/ 2, \"conv2d\");\n  Tensor output;\n  if (at::isComplexType(input_.scalar_type())) {\n    output = complex_convolution(input, weight, bias, stride, padding, dilation, false, {{0, 0}}, groups);\n  } else {\n    output = at::convolution_symint(input, weight, bias, stride, padding, dilation, false, {{0, 0}}, groups);\n  }\n  return is_batched ? std::move(output) : output.squeeze(0);\n}\n\nat::Tensor conv3d_symint(\n    const Tensor& input_, const Tensor& weight, const std::optional<Tensor>& bias_opt,\n    SymIntArrayRef stride, SymIntArrayRef padding, SymIntArrayRef dilation, c10::SymInt groups) {\n  // See [Note: hacky wrapper removal for optional tensor]\n  c10::MaybeOwned<Tensor> bias_maybe_owned = at::borrow_from_optional_tensor(bias_opt);\n  const Tensor& bias = *bias_maybe_owned;\n\n  TORCH_CHECK(\n    !bias.defined() || bias.dtype() == input_.dtype(),\n    \"Input type (\",\n    input_.dtype().name(),\n    \") and bias type (\",\n    bias.dtype().name(),\n    \") should be the same\");\n\n  auto [input, is_batched] = batchify(input_, /*num_spatial_dims=*/ 3, \"conv3d\");\n  Tensor output;\n  if (at::isComplexType(input_.scalar_type())) {\n    output = complex_convolution(input, weight, bias, stride, padding, dilation, false, {{0, 0, 0}}, groups);\n  } else {\n    output = at::convolution_symint(input, weight, bias, stride, padding, dilation, false, {{0, 0, 0}}, groups);\n  }\n  return is_batched ? std::move(output) : output.squeeze(0);\n}\n\n\nstatic Tensor convolution_same(\n    const Tensor &input, const Tensor &weight, const Tensor &bias,\n    SymIntArrayRef stride, SymIntArrayRef dilation, const c10::SymInt& groups) {\n\n  auto k = weight.dim();\n  TORCH_CHECK(k > 2, \"weight should have at least three dimensions\");\n  TORCH_CHECK(groups > 0, \"non-positive groups is not supported\");\n  auto dim = static_cast<size_t>(k - 2);\n  auto weight_sizes = weight.sym_sizes();\n  auto input_sizes = input.sym_sizes();\n  TORCH_CHECK(k == input.dim(),\n              \"Expected \", k, \"-dimensional input for \",\n              k, \"-dimensional weight\", weight_sizes, \", but got \",\n              input.dim(), \"-dimensional input of size \",\n              input.sizes(), \" instead\");\n  TORCH_CHECK(stride.size() == dim || stride.size() == 1U,\n              \"stride cannot broadcast to \", dim, \" dimensions\");\n  TORCH_CHECK(dilation.size() == dim || dilation.size() == 1U,\n              \"dilation cannot broadcast to \", dim, \" dimensions\");\n  for (auto i: c10::irange(stride.size())) {\n    TORCH_CHECK(stride[i] == 1, \"padding='same' is not supported for strided convolutions\");\n  }\n\n  // Calculate the correct padding\n  SymDimVector padding_l, padding_r;\n  bool symmetric_padding = true;\n  for (auto i: c10::irange(dim)) {\n    auto s = stride.size() == 1 ? stride[0] : stride[i];\n    auto d = dilation.size() == 1 ? dilation[0] : dilation[i];\n    auto pad = pooling_same_mode_padding_lr(\n        input_sizes[i + 2], weight_sizes[i + 2], s, d);\n    padding_l.push_back(pad.first);\n    padding_r.push_back(pad.second);\n    if (pad.first != pad.second) {\n      symmetric_padding = false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (symmetric_padding) {\n    // All backends handle symmetric padding natively\n    SymDimVector output_padding(static_cast<size_t>(dim));\n    return at::convolution_symint(input, weight, bias, stride, padding_l, dilation,\n                               false, output_padding, groups);\n  }\n\n  TORCH_WARN_ONCE(\"Using padding='same' with even kernel lengths and odd dilation may\"\n                  \" require a zero-padded copy of the input be created\");\n  SmallVector<c10::SymInt, kDimVectorStaticSize * 2> pad_nd(static_cast<size_t>(2 * dim));\n  for (auto i: c10::irange(dim)) {\n    // Apply padding by the difference, leaving only a symmetric padding\n    auto delta_pad = padding_r[i] - padding_l[i];\n    auto pad_idx = 2 * (dim - 1 - i);  // F.pad goes from last dim to first\n    if (delta_pad > 0) {\n      pad_nd[pad_idx + 1] = delta_pad;\n    } else {\n      pad_nd[pad_idx] = delta_pad;\n      padding_l[i] = padding_r[i];\n    }\n  }\n  auto padded_input = at::constant_pad_nd_symint(input, pad_nd, 0);\n  SymDimVector output_padding(static_cast<size_t>(dim));\n  return at::convolution_symint(padded_input, weight, bias, stride, padding_l,\n                                dilation, false, output_padding, groups);\n}\n\nTensor _convolution_mode_symint(\n    const Tensor& input, const Tensor& weight, const std::optional<Tensor>& bias_opt,\n    SymIntArrayRef stride, std::string_view padding, SymIntArrayRef dilation,\n    c10::SymInt groups) {\n  // See [Note: hacky wrapper removal for optional tensor]\n  c10::MaybeOwned<Tensor> bias_maybe_owned = at::borrow_from_optional_tensor(bias_opt);\n  const Tensor& bias = *bias_maybe_owned;\n\n  if (padding == \"same\") {\n    return at::native::convolution_same(\n        input, weight, bias, stride, dilation, groups);\n  } else if (padding == \"valid\") {\n    return at::convolution_symint(\n        input, weight, bias, stride, {{0}}, dilation, false, {{0}}, groups);\n  }\n  TORCH_CHECK(false, \"Invalid padding string: '\", padding, \"'\");\n}\n\nat::Tensor conv1d_padding_symint(\n    const Tensor& input_, const Tensor& weight, const std::optional<Tensor>& bias,\n
